kikuyu: Tumĩra Harũni mũrũ wa nyũkwa nguo nyamũre, cia kũmũnenehagia na kũmũtĩĩithagia.
english: You shall make holy garments for Aaron your brother, for glory and for beauty.

kikuyu: na cia Gibeoni ciarĩ 95.
english: The children of Gibeon: ninety-five.

kikuyu: Rĩrĩa ndamaheire irio, nĩmahũũnire; na rĩrĩa maahũũnire, magĩtĩĩa; magĩcooka makĩriganĩrwo nĩ niĩ.
english: According to their pasture, so were they filled; they were filled, and their heart was exalted. Therefore they have forgotten me.

kikuyu: Ngoro ciitũ nĩcikenete nĩ ũndũ wake, nĩgũkorwo twĩhokete rĩĩtwa rĩake itheru.
english: For our heart rejoices in him, because we have trusted in his holy name.

kikuyu: Nĩnjathĩte andũ akwa arĩa atheru, nĩndũmanĩire njamba ciakwa, o icio ikenagĩra ũhootani wakwa, ciũke ikinyanĩrie marakara makwa.
english: I have commanded my consecrated ones; yes, I have called my mighty men for my anger, even my proudly exulting ones.

kikuyu: Nao magĩthiĩ bũrũri wa Juda wothe makĩũnganagia Alawii na atongoria a nyũmba cia andũ a Isiraeli kuuma matũũra- inĩ mothe. Rĩrĩa mookire Jerusalemu-rĩ,
english: They went around in Judah and gathered the Levites out of all the cities of Judah, and the heads of fathers’ households of Israel, and they came to Jerusalem.

kikuyu: Na rĩu, mũtigathĩĩnĩke kana mwĩrakarĩre nĩkũnyendia gũkũ, tondũ nĩ Ngai wandũmire njũke mbere yanyu nĩgeetha honokie mĩoyo.
english: Now don’t be grieved, nor angry with yourselves, that you sold me here, for God sent me before you to preserve life.

kikuyu: (Aaragia ũhoro wa Judasi mũrũ wa Simoni Isikariota, tondũ o na aakorwo aarĩ ũmwe wa acio ikũmi na eerĩ, nĩwe warĩ amũkunyanĩre thuutha ũcio.)
english: Now he spoke of Judas, the son of Simon Iscariot, for it was he who would betray him, being one of the twelve.

kikuyu: “Ĩrai ndũrĩrĩ ũhoro ũcio, hunjĩriai Jerusalemu muuge atĩrĩ: ‘Ita inene rĩa gũgũũkĩrĩra nĩrĩroka riumĩte bũrũri wa kũraya, na rĩroka na mbugĩrĩrio ya mbaara ya gũũkĩrĩra matũũra mothe manene ma Juda.
english: “Tell the nations, behold, publish against Jerusalem, ‘Watchers come from a far country, and raise their voice against the cities of Judah.

kikuyu: Nake Pilato akĩandĩkithia kĩbaũ na gĩkĩhũũrĩrwo mũtharaba-inĩ. Nakĩo kĩandĩkĩtwo atĩrĩ, JESŨ WA NAZARETHI, MŨTHAMAKI WA AYAHUDI.
english: Pilate wrote a title also, and put it on the cross. There was written, “JESUS OF NAZARETH, THE KING OF THE JEWS.”

kikuyu: Joshua mũrũ wa Nuni, ũrĩa wateithagia Musa kuuma arĩ mũnini, akĩarĩria Musa, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Musa, mwathi wakwa, makaanie!”
english: Joshua the son of Nun, the servant of Moses, one of his chosen men, answered, “My lord Moses, forbid them!”

kikuyu: Akĩmatongoria makiuma Misiri na agĩĩka morirũ na akĩringa ciama arĩ kũu Misiri, o na Iria-inĩ Itune, na ihinda rĩa mĩaka mĩrongo ĩna kũu werũ-inĩ.
english: This man led them out, having worked wonders and signs in Egypt, in the Red Sea, and in the wilderness for forty years.

kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, Mũkũũri wao arĩ na hinya; Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe nĩrĩo rĩĩtwa rĩake. Nĩakamarũĩrĩra na kĩyo ciira-inĩ wao, nĩguo atũme bũrũri wao ũgĩe na ũhurũko, no aagithie arĩa matũũraga Babuloni ũhurũko.”
english: Their Redeemer is strong. Yahweh of Armies is his name. He will thoroughly plead their cause, that he may give rest to the earth, and disquiet the inhabitants of Babylon.

kikuyu: Mũthenya ũcio nĩngoimagara nganiine ndũrĩrĩ ciothe iria igookĩrĩra itũũra rĩa Jerusalemu. Kũrĩrĩra ũrĩa Maathecire
english: It will happen in that day, that I will seek to destroy all the nations that come against Jerusalem.

kikuyu: Ciĩga ciothe ingĩtuĩka nĩ kĩĩga kĩmwe-rĩ, mwĩrĩ naguo ũngĩkorwo ha?
english: If they were all one member, where would the body be?

kikuyu: Agĩĩka maũndũ mooru maitho- inĩ ma Jehova, akĩrũmĩrĩra mĩtugo ĩrĩ magigi ya ndũrĩrĩ iria Jehova aaingatĩte ciehere mbere ya Isiraeli.
english: He did that which was evil in Yahweh’s sight, after the abominations of the nations whom Yahweh cast out before the children of Israel.

kikuyu: Na mũthenya ũcio warĩ wa Ihaarĩria, nayo Thabatũ yarĩ hakuhĩ kwambĩrĩria.
english: It was the day of the Preparation, and the Sabbath was drawing near.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo icooki rĩakwa nĩ ihũthũ, na mũrigo wakwa nĩ mũhũthũ.”
english: For my yoke is easy, and my burden is light.”

kikuyu: “Rĩrĩa gacaũ, kana gatũrũme, kana koori gaaciarwo, karĩikaraga na nyina mĩthenya mũgwanja. Kuuma mũthenya wa kanana na thuutha ũcio, nĩgagetĩkĩrĩka gatuĩke iruta rĩa gũcinwo na mwaki harĩ Jehova.
english: “When a bull, a sheep, or a goat is born, it shall remain seven days with its mother. From the eighth day on it shall be accepted for the offering of an offering made by fire to Yahweh.

kikuyu: Nao makĩigĩrĩra ithandũkũ rĩa Ngai ngaari-inĩ njerũ ya ngʼombe, makĩrĩruta nyũmba ya Abinadabu, ĩrĩa yarĩ kĩrĩma-inĩ. Nao Uza na Ahio, ariũ a Abinadabu, nĩo maatongoretie ngaari ĩyo njerũ
english: They set God’s ark on a new cart, and brought it out of Abinadab’s house that was on the hill; and Uzzah and Ahio, the sons of Abinadab, drove the new cart.

kikuyu: nĩ ũndũ ũcio Mwathani Jehova ekuuga atĩrĩ: Nĩngatambũrũkia guoko gwakwa njũkĩrĩre Edomu na njũrage andũ akuo na mahiũ mao. Nĩngakwananga, na kuuma Timani nginya Dedani, nĩmakooragwo na rũhiũ rwa njora.
english: therefore the Lord Yahweh says, “I will stretch out my hand on Edom, and will cut off man and animal from it; and I will make it desolate from Teman. They will fall by the sword even to Dedan.

kikuyu: Inyuĩ kiragĩrĩriai mathĩĩna marĩ taarĩ irũithia; nĩgũkorwo Ngai aramwĩka ũguo ta mũrĩ ciana ciake. Nĩ ũndũ-rĩ, nĩ mwana ũrĩkũ ũtarũithagio nĩ ithe?
english: It is for discipline that you endure. God deals with you as with children, for what son is there whom his father doesn’t discipline?

kikuyu: kana mwĩhĩte na thĩ, nĩgũkorwo nĩkuo gĩturwa gĩa kũigĩrĩra magũrũ make; o na kana mwĩhĩte na Jerusalemu, nĩgũkorwo nĩrĩo itũũra inene rĩa Mũthamaki mũnene.
english: nor by the earth, for it is the footstool of his feet; nor by Jerusalem, for it is the city of the great King.

kikuyu: Anene mangĩigua atĩ nĩtwaranĩirie nawe, nao moke kũrĩ we makũũrie atĩrĩ, ‘Twĩre ũrĩa wĩrĩte mũthamaki, na ũrĩa nake mũthamaki akwĩrĩte; ndũgatũhithe ũndũ kana tũkũũrage,’
english: But if the princes hear that I have talked with you, and they come to you, and tell you, ‘Declare to us now what you have said to the king; don’t hide it from us, and we will not put you to death; also tell us what the king said to you;’

kikuyu: No rĩrĩa rĩothe aatoonyaga harĩ Jehova maranĩrie, nĩarutaga taama ũcio wa kwĩhumbĩra ũthiũ nginya oime ho. Na rĩrĩa oka kũrĩ andũ a Isiraeli na aameera ũrĩa aathĩtwo,
english: But when Moses went in before Yahweh to speak with him, he took the veil off, until he came out; and he came out, and spoke to the children of Israel that which he was commanded.

kikuyu: “Rĩrĩa anene a Isiraeli maatongoria, rĩrĩa andũ meheana meyendeire-rĩ, goocai Jehova!
english: “Because the leaders took the lead in Israel, because the people offered themselves willingly, be blessed, Yahweh!

kikuyu: Ĩno nĩyo thakame yakwa ya kĩrĩkanĩro, ĩrĩa ĩgũitwo nĩ ũndũ wa andũ aingĩ nĩ ũndũ wa kũrekanĩrwo kwa mehia.
english: for this is my blood of the new covenant, which is poured out for many for the remission of sins.

kikuyu: Arata akwa arĩa manguhĩrĩirie othe nĩmathũire; andũ arĩa nyendeete nĩmahutatĩire makaanjũkĩrĩra.
english: All my familiar friends abhor me. They whom I loved have turned against me.

kikuyu: Alawii arĩa maarĩ thĩinĩ wa Itũũra rĩu itheru maarĩ andũ 284.
english: All the Levites in the holy city were two hundred eighty-four.

kikuyu: Rĩtirĩ mũndũ rĩathĩkagĩra, rĩtiĩtĩkagĩra gũtaarwo. Rĩtiĩhokaga Jehova, na rĩtikuhagĩrĩria Ngai warĩo.
english: She didn’t obey the voice. She didn’t receive correction. She didn’t trust in Yahweh. She didn’t draw near to her God.

kikuyu: Jehova aanjĩĩrire atĩrĩ: “Thiĩ ũrũgame kĩhingo-inĩ kĩrĩa gĩĩtagwo kĩhingo kĩa andũ, kĩrĩa athamaki a Juda mageraga magĩtoonya itũũra-inĩ na makiuma; ningĩ ũrũgame ihingo-inĩ iria ingĩ ciothe cia
english: Yahweh said this to me: “Go and stand in the gate of the children of the people, through which the kings of Judah come in and by which they go out, and in all the gates of Jerusalem.

kikuyu: Jehozadaku nĩathaamirio hĩndĩ ĩrĩa Jehova aarekereirie andũ a Juda na a Jerusalemu matahwo nĩ Nebukadinezaru.
english: The sons of Levi: Gershom, Kohath, and Merari.

kikuyu: Thuutha wa Rakeli gũciara Jusufu, Jakubu akĩĩra Labani atĩrĩ, “Ndekereria ndĩthiĩre, nĩguo njooke bũrũri witũ.
english: When Rachel had borne Joseph, Jacob said to Laban, “Send me away, that I may go to my own place, and to my country.

kikuyu: Agaathikwo o ta ũrĩa ndigiri ĩthikagwo: agaakururio, aikio na kũu nja ya ihingo cia Jerusalemu.”
english: He will be buried with the burial of a donkey, drawn and cast out beyond the gates of Jerusalem.”

kikuyu: Nao nĩmakarĩhwo ũũru na ũũru kũringana na ũrĩa matũire mekaga. O monaga atĩ kũrĩĩo mũthenya barigici nĩkuo gwĩkenia. Andũ acio nĩ imeni na ũũgũ, rĩrĩa megwĩkenia maruga-inĩ mao ma ikeno hĩndĩ ĩrĩa mekũrĩanĩra na inyuĩ.
english: receiving the wages of unrighteousness; people who count it pleasure to revel in the daytime, spots and defects, reveling in their deceit while they feast with you;

kikuyu: Nao andũ a Gadi maatũũire mariganĩtie na andũ a Rubeni kũu bũrũri wa Bashani,† o nginya Saleka:
english: The sons of Gad lived beside them in the land of Bashan to Salecah:

kikuyu: ngĩkoona ndegwa kana ngʼondu ya mũrũ wa ithe wanyu yũrĩte, ndũkamĩtiganĩrie, no tigĩrĩra wona nĩwamĩcookia kũrĩ we.
english: ou shall not see your brother’s ox or his sheep go astray and hide yourself from them. You shall surely bring them again to your brother.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, kuona atĩ maũndũ macio matingĩkaanĩka-rĩ, mwagĩrĩirwo nĩ gũkira, na mũtikae gwĩka ũndũ wa ihenya.
english: Seeing then that these things can’t be denied, you ought to be quiet and to do nothing rash.

kikuyu: Nake mũtumia ũcio akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Mũthuuri ũyũ, ndũrĩ na kĩndũ gĩa gũtaha maaĩ nakĩo, na gĩthima gĩkĩ nĩ kĩriku. Ũngĩkĩruta kũ maaĩ macio ma muoyo?
english: The woman said to him, “Sir, you have nothing to draw with, and the well is

kikuyu: Nayonyokaĩyoĩkĩhorora maaĩ kuuma kanua kayo o ta rũũĩ, nĩgeetha ĩkinyĩrie mũndũ-wa-nja ũcio, ĩtũme atwarwo nĩ kĩguũ kĩu.
english: The serpent spewed water out of his mouth after the woman like a river, that he might cause her to be carried away by the stream.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio ruta watho nĩguo mbĩrĩra ĩyo ĩrangĩrwo nginya mũthenya wa gatatũ. Kwaga ũguo, arutwo ake maahota gũthiĩ maiye mwĩrĩ ũcio macooke meere andũ atĩ nĩariũkĩtio kuuma kũrĩ arĩa akuũ. Maheeni macio maahota gũtuĩka mooru gũkĩra marĩa ma mbere.”
english: Command therefore that the tomb be made secure until the third day, lest perhaps his disciples come at night and steal him away, and tell the people, ‘He is risen from the dead;’ and the last deception will be worse than the first.”  or, vinegar  Gall is a bitter-tasting, dark green oil from a wormwood plant that is alcoholic in its effect.  TR adds “that it might be fulfilled which was spoken by the prophet: ‘They divided my garments amongthem, andformyclothingtheycastlots;’ ”[seePsalms22:18andJohn19:24] TRomits“thePharisees”  noon  3:00 p.m.  TR reads “lama” instead of “lima”  Psalms 22:1

kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, mũndũ angĩkũgũtha rũthĩa rũmwe, mũhũgũkĩrie rũu rũngĩ o naruo. Na mũndũ angĩgũtunya igooti rĩaku, ndũkamũgirie kuoya o na kanjũ yaku.
english: To him who strikes you on the cheek, offer also the other; and from him who takes away your cloak, don’t withhold your coat also.

kikuyu: Jehova nĩarekereirie mangʼũrĩ make biũ; aitũrũrĩte marakara make mahiũ. Nĩakĩtie mwaki kũu Zayuni ũrĩa ũcinĩte mĩthingi yakuo.
english: Yahweh has accomplished his wrath. He has poured out his fierce anger. He has kindled a fire in Zion, which has devoured its foundations.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo Jehova ndateanagĩria andũ nginya tene.
english: For the Lord will not cast off forever.

kikuyu: “Kũgwatwo nĩ kĩrumi-rĩ, nĩ mũndũ ũrĩa ũkomaga na mũtumia wa ithe, nĩgũkorwo nĩ ũrĩrĩ wa ithe aagĩire gĩtĩĩo.” Hĩndĩ ĩyo nao andũ othe moige, “Ameni!”
english: ‘Cursed is he who lies with† his father’s wife, because he dishonors his father’s bed.’ All the people shall say, ‘Amen.’

kikuyu: Ngĩĩcũũrania maũndũ macio, na ngĩrũithia andũ arĩa maarĩ igweta na anene, ngĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Nĩmũreetia andũ a bũrũri wanyu uumithio wa mbeeca iria mũmakombithĩtie!” Nĩ ũndũ ũcio ngĩĩtana mũcemanio mũnene wa kũmaciirithia,
english: Then I consulted with myself, and contended with the nobles and the rulers, and said to them, “You exact usury, everyone of his brother.” I held a great assembly against them.

kikuyu: Nowe Jesũ akĩona ũhinga ũrĩa maarĩ naguo akĩmeera atĩrĩ,
english: But he perceived their craftiness, and said to them, “Why do you test me?

kikuyu: “Ndiihũ cia gĩkuũ nĩciathiũrũrũkĩirie;
english: For the waves of death surrounded me. The floods of ungodliness made me afraid.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa ngano yamerire na igĩciara-rĩ, hĩndĩ ĩyo itindiĩ o narĩo rĩkĩonekana.
english: But when the blade sprang up and produced grain, then the darnel weeds appeared also.

kikuyu: Agĩtũma bũrũri ũcio ũgĩe ngʼaragu, na akĩniina mĩthiithũ yao yothe ya irio;
english: He called for a famine on the land. He destroyed the food supplies.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo maũndũ marĩa mothe ndoigire ma gũũkĩrĩra Eli na nyũmba yake nĩngamahingia kuuma kĩambĩrĩria nginya mũthia.
english: A man of Benjamin ran out of the army and came to Shiloh the same day, with his clothes torn and with dirt on his head.

kikuyu: Ringai mũthiĩ hũgũrũrũ-inĩ cia Kitimu mũkerorere, ningĩ mũtũmane gũthiio Kedari gũgatuĩrio wega,* muone kana kũrĩ kuoneka ũndũ ta ũyũ:
english: For pass over to the islands of Kittim, and see. Send to Kedar, and consider diligently, and see if there has been such a thing.

kikuyu: “ ‘Ihinda rĩothe rĩa mwĩhĩtwa ũcio wa kwĩyamũra, ndakanenjwo mũtwe. No nginya aikarage arĩ mũtheru o nginya ihinda rĩake rĩa kwĩyamũrĩra Jehova rĩthire; no nginya areke njuĩrĩ cia mũtwe wake ikũre iraihe.
english: “ ‘All the days of his vow of separation no razor shall come on his head, until the days are fulfilled in which he separates himself to Yahweh. He shall be holy. He shall let the locks of the hair of his head grow long.

kikuyu: Maaĩ macio maandigiicĩirie makenda kũnjũraga, nakuo kũu kũriku gũgaathiũrũrũkĩria; riya rĩa iria-inĩ rĩeohereire mũtwe wakwa.
english: The waters surrounded me, even to the soul. The deep was around me. The weeds were wrapped around my head.  “Yahweh” is God’s proper Name, sometimes rendered “LORD” (all caps) in other translations.  or, gods  or, gods  The Hebrew word rendered “God” is “אֱֹלהִ ֑ים” (Elohim).  Sheol is the place of the dead.

kikuyu: Nĩhithĩte kiugo gĩaku thĩinĩ wa ngoro yakwa, nĩguo ndikae gũkwĩhĩria.
english: I have hidden your word in my heart, that I might not sin against you.

kikuyu: Nayo thĩ ĩgĩathamia kanua kayo ĩkĩmameria, hamwe na andũ ao na arũmĩrĩri othe a Kora, na indo ciao ciothe.
english: The earth opened its mouth and swallowed them up with their households, all of Korah’s men, and all their goods.

kikuyu: Gũtirĩ kĩndũ o na kĩmwe kĩagire: mũndũ mwĩthĩ kana mũkũrũ, kamwana kana kairĩtu, indo iria ciothe ciatahĩtwo kana kĩndũ o gĩothe kĩrĩa maakuuĩte. Daudi agĩcookithia indo ciothe.
english: There was nothing lacking to them, neither small nor great, neither sons nor daughters, neither plunder, nor anything that they had taken. David brought them all back.

kikuyu: Maathani maya ndĩrakũhe ũmũthĩ ũreke matũũre ngoro-inĩ yaku.
english: These words, which I command you today, shall be on your heart;

kikuyu: “Nĩũkũmatuĩra ciira? Nĩũkũmatuĩra ciira, wee mũrũ wa mũndũ? Nĩ ũndũ ũcio marũithie, ũmeere maũndũ marĩ magigi marĩa meekagwo nĩ maithe mao,
english: “Will you judge them, son of man? Will you judge them? Cause them to know the abominations of their fathers.

kikuyu: nake Isaaka aarĩ na mĩaka mĩrongo ĩna rĩrĩa aahikirie Rebeka, mwarĩ wa Bethueli ũrĩa Mũsuriata wa Padani-Aramu, na aarĩ mwarĩ wa nyina na Labani ũrĩa Mũsuriata.
english: Isaac was forty years old when he took Rebekah, the daughter of Bethuel the Syrian of Paddan Aram, the sister of Laban the Syrian, to be his wife.

kikuyu: Wee ũkĩrĩ ũ nĩguo ũtuĩre ndungata ya mũndũ ũngĩ ciira? Mwathi wayo nĩwe ũmenyaga ĩrũngiĩ wega kana ĩgwĩte. Nayo nĩĩrĩrũgamaga, tondũ Mwathani nĩwe ũrĩ na ũhoti wa gũtũma ĩrũgame.
english: Who are you who judge another’s servant? To his own lord he stands or falls. Yes, he will be made to stand, for God has power to make him stand.

kikuyu: Amidiani makĩnyariira andũ a Isiraeli o nginya andũ a Isiraeli magĩkaĩra Jehova amateithie.
english: Israel was brought very low because of Midian; and the children of Israel cried to Yahweh.

kikuyu: Nake agĩcookia atĩrĩ, “Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa mwana ararĩ muoyo-rĩ, ndĩrehingaga na ngarĩra. ngĩĩciiria atĩrĩ, ‘Nũũ ũũĩ? Jehova aahota kũnjiguĩra tha areke mwana ũcio atũũre muoyo.’
english: He said, “While the child was yet alive, I fasted and wept; for I said, ‘Who knows whether Yahweh will not be gracious to me, that the child may live?’

kikuyu: Jakubu akĩhaanda gĩtugĩ igũrũ rĩa mbĩrĩra yake, na nginya ũmũthĩ ũyũ gĩtugĩ kĩu nĩkĩo kĩonanagia mbĩrĩra ya Rakeli.
english: Jacob set up a pillar on her grave. The same is the Pillar of Rachel’s grave to this day.

kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, mũtigakene tondũ ngoma nĩimwathĩkagĩra, no kenagai tondũ marĩĩtwa manyu nĩmandĩkĩtwo kũũrĩa Igũrũ.”
english: Nevertheless, don’t rejoice in this, that the spirits are subject to you, but rejoice that your names are written in heaven.”

kikuyu: Nao moona ũguo-rĩ, magĩtembũranga nguo ciao. Magĩcooka makĩhaicia mĩrigo yao ndigiri igũrũ magĩcooka na thuutha kũu itũũra-inĩ inene.
english: Then they tore their clothes, and each man loaded his donkey, and returned to the city.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio magĩthiĩ harĩ mũthamaki na makĩaria nake igũrũ rĩa uuge ũcio wa mũthamaki: Makĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Githĩ ndwandĩkithirie watho atĩ handũ ha matukũ mĩrongo ĩtatũ, mũndũ ũngĩhooya ngai o na ĩrĩkũ kana ahooe mũndũ o na ũrĩkũ tiga o we mũthamaki, no nginya aikio irima-inĩ rĩa mĩrũũthi?” Mũthamaki agĩcookia atĩrĩ, “Watho ũcio ũrĩ o ho, kũringana na mawatho ma Amedi na Aperisia, marĩa matagarũragwo.”
english: Then they came near, and spoke before the king concerning the king’s decree: “Haven’t you signed a decree that every man who makes a petition to any god or man within thirty days, except to you, O king, shall be cast into the den of lions?” The king answered, “This thing is true, according to the law of the Medes and Persians, which doesn’t alter.”

kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa kĩrĩndĩ kĩamenyire atĩ Jesũ kana arutwo ake gũtirĩ warĩ ho, gĩkĩingĩra marikabu icio, gĩgĩthiĩ Kaperinaumu gũcaria Jesũ. Jesũ nĩwe Mũgate wa Muoyo
english: When the multitude therefore saw that Jesus wasn’t there, nor his disciples, they themselves got into the boats and came to Capernaum, seeking Jesus.

kikuyu: “Mwena wa ithũĩro wa nja ĩyo ũkorwo ũrĩ wa mĩkono mĩrongo ĩtano§ kwarama na ũgĩe na itambaya cia gũcuurio na itugĩ ikũmi na itina ikũmi.
english: For the width of the court on the west side shall be hangings of fifty cubits; their pillars ten, and their sockets ten.

kikuyu: Watho ũrĩa woigire gĩthukĩ na mĩri yakĩo gĩtigwo, nĩ kuuga atĩ nĩũgacookerio ũthamaki waku rĩrĩa ũkaamenya atĩ wathani uumaga Igũrũ.*
english: Whereas it was commanded to leave the stump of the roots of the tree, your kingdom shall be sure to you after you know that Heaven rules.

kikuyu: Nao eerĩ makĩĩonithania irangĩro-inĩ rĩa Afilisti. Nao Afilisti makiuga atĩrĩ, “Hĩ, ta rorai! Ahibirania makiuma marima-inĩ makurumĩte kũrĩa marehithĩte.”
english: Both of them revealed themselves to the garrison of the Philistines; and the Philistines said, “Behold, the Hebrews are coming out of the holes where they had hidden themselves!”

kikuyu: Nake Azaria ũrĩa mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai hamwe na athĩnjĩri-Ngai omĩrĩru mĩrongo ĩnana a Jehova, makĩmuuma thuutha o nginya na kũu thĩinĩ.
english: Azariah the priest went in after him, and with him eighty priests of Yahweh, who were valiant men.

kikuyu: “ ‘No rĩrĩ, mũthenya ũcio ngeeka ũndũ ũrĩ ngũrani bũrũri-inĩ wa Gosheni, kũrĩa andũ akwa matũũraga; gũtikagĩa na mĩrumbĩ ya ngi kũu, nĩgeetha ũmenye atĩ niĩ, Jehova, ndĩ bũrũri-inĩ ũyũ.
english: I will set apart in that day the land of Goshen, in which my people dwell, that no swarms of flies shall be there, to the end you may know that I am Yahweh on the earth.

kikuyu: Nĩanyiihagia andũ arĩa maikaraga kũrĩa gũtũũgĩru, o na itũũra rĩrĩa itũũgĩru akarĩharũrũkia, akarĩharagania thĩ, agacooka akarĩtungumania rũkũngũ-inĩ.
english: For he has brought down those who dwell on high, the lofty city. He lays it low. He lays it low even to the ground. He brings it even to the dust.

kikuyu: Andũ othe makĩiyũrwo nĩ guoya na makĩgooca Ngai, makiuga atĩrĩ, “Mũnabii mũnene nĩokĩte gatagatĩ gaitũ. Ngai nĩokĩte gũteithia andũ ake.”
english: Fear took hold of all, and they glorified God, saying, “A great prophet has arisen among us!” and, “God has visited his people!”  The phrase “only born” is from the Greek word “μονογενη”, which is sometimes translated “only begotten” or “one and only”.

kikuyu: No Labani mũrũ wa nyina na mũirĩtu, o na nyina, makiuga atĩrĩ, “Reke mũirĩtu ũyũ aikare na ithuĩ ta matukũ ikũmi; mũcooke mũthiĩ.”
english: Her brother and her mother said, “Let the young lady stay with us a few days, at least ten. After that she will go.”

kikuyu: Nĩonetie andũ ake mawĩra make ma hinya, nĩ ũndũ wa kũmahe mabũrũri ma ndũrĩrĩ iria ingĩ.
english: He has shown his people the power of his works, in giving them the heritage of the nations.

kikuyu: Ningĩ akĩrehe ithandũkũ rĩa kĩrĩkanĩro thĩinĩ wa hema ĩyo nyamũre, na agĩĩkĩra gĩtambaya gĩa kũhakania na akĩhakania ithandũkũ rĩa Ũira o ta ũrĩa Jehova aamwathĩte.
english: He brought the ark into the tabernacle, and set up the veil of the screen, and screened the ark of the covenant, as Yahweh commanded Moses.

kikuyu: Na to indo icio icaayaga, no o na ithuĩ arĩa tũrĩ na maciaro ma mbere ma Roho, nĩtũcaayaga ngoro-inĩ ciitũ thĩinĩ, twetereire hĩndĩ ĩkinye ya gũtuuo ciana cia Ngai, na nĩkuo gũkũũrwo kwa mĩĩrĩ iitũ kuuma ũkombo-inĩ.
english: Not only so, but ourselves also, who have the first fruits of the Spirit, even we ourselves groan within ourselves, waiting for adoption, the redemption of our body.

kikuyu: Abisalomu nĩathuurĩte Amasa atuĩke mũnene wa mbũtũ cia ita ithenya rĩa Joabu. Amasa aarĩ mũriũ wa mũndũ wetagwo Jetheri, Mũisiraeli ũrĩa wahikĩtie Abigaili, mwarĩ wa Nahashu, mwarĩ wa nyina na Zeruia nyina wa Joabu.
english: Absalom set Amasa over the army instead of Joab. Now Amasa was the son of a man whose name was Ithra the Israelite, who went in to Abigail the daughter of Nahash, sister to Zeruiah, Joab’s mother.

kikuyu: Ũnjaramagĩria njĩra ya kũrĩa thiiagĩra, nĩgeetha magũrũ makwa matigatirũke.
english: You have enlarged my steps under me, My feet have not slipped.

kikuyu: Iburahĩmu nĩagĩire na kĩĩrĩgĩrĩro o na harĩa hataarĩ kĩndũ gĩa kwĩrĩgĩrĩra na agĩĩtĩkia, na nĩ ũndũ ũcio agĩtuĩka ithe wa ndũrĩrĩ nyingĩ, o ta ũrĩa erĩtwo atĩrĩ, “Ũguo nĩguo rũciaro rwaku rũkaigana.”
english: Against hope, Abraham in hope believed, to the end that he might become a father of many nations, according to

kikuyu: No Herode akiuga atĩrĩ, “Johana nĩndamũrengithirie mũtwe. Ũyũ ndĩraigua ũhoro wake- rĩ, nĩwe ũ?” Nake akĩĩrirĩria kũmuona. Jesũ Kũhũũnia Andũ Ngiri Ithano
english: Herod said, “I beheaded John, but who is this about whom I hear such things?” He sought to see him.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Elija agĩthiĩ kwĩonania kũrĩ Ahabu. Na rĩrĩ, ngʼaragu yarĩ nene mũno kũu Samaria,
english: Elijah went to show himself to Ahab. The famine was severe in Samaria.

kikuyu: ĩu ta thikĩrĩriai, inyuĩ itonga ici, rĩrai na mũgirĩke tondũ wa mathĩĩna marĩa maramũkora.
english: ome now, you rich, weep and howl for your miseries that are coming on you.

kikuyu: Inyuĩ inyuothe mũrĩ ciana cia ũtheri na ciana cia mũthenya. Ithuĩ tũtirĩ a ũtukũ kana a nduma.
english: You are all children of light and children of the day. We don’t belong to the night, nor to darkness,

kikuyu: Nayo ndungata ĩyo ĩkĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, ‘Mũrũ wa maitũguo nĩokĩte, nake thoguo nĩamũthĩnjĩire njaũ ya gĩcegũ ĩrĩa noru, tondũ nĩamũcookereire arĩ mwega na arĩ mũgima.’
english: He said to him, ‘Your brother has come, and your father has killed the fattened calf, because he has received him back safe and healthy.’

kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Solomoni aarĩkirie gwaka hekarũ ya Jehova na nyũmba ya ũthamaki, na aarĩkia gwĩka ũrĩa wothe eeciirĩtie gwĩka hekarũ-inĩ ya Jehova o na nyũmba-inĩ yake ya ũthamaki-rĩ,
english: Thus Solomon finished Yahweh’s house and the king’s house; and he successfully completed all that came into Solomon’s heart to make in Yahweh’s house and in his own house.

kikuyu: Ningĩ akĩruta mĩgogo ya mĩtarakwa ĩtangĩtarĩka, nĩgũkorwo andũ a Sidoni na a Turo nĩmareheire Daudi mĩgogo mĩingĩ.
english: and cedar trees without number, for the Sidonians and the people of Tyre brought cedar trees in abundance to David.

kikuyu: Kaĩ ũtangĩtũrurumũkia ngoro ciitũ, nĩguo andũ aku magũkenere?
english: Won’t you revive us again, that your people may rejoice in you?

kikuyu: Tiga andũ a Benjamini-rĩ, Isiraeli nĩmacookanĩrĩirie thigari 400 arĩa marũaga na hiũ cia njora, na othe maarĩ andũ a ita.
english: The men of Israel, besides Benjamin, were counted four hundred thousand men who drew sword. All these were men of war.

kikuyu: a rĩrĩ, rĩrĩa Daudi aakũrire na agĩkorwo arĩ na mĩaka mĩingĩ-rĩ, agĩtua mũriũ Solomoni mũthamaki wa gũthamakĩra Isiraeli.
english: ow David was old and full of days; and he made Solomon his son king over Israel.

kikuyu: Mũirĩtu ũcio nĩamũkenirie na akĩendeka nĩwe. O hĩndĩ ĩyo akĩmũhe indo cia gwĩthakaria nacio o na akĩmũhe irio cia mwanya. Ningĩ akĩmũhe ndungata mũgwanja cia airĩtu, arĩa maathuurĩtwo kuuma nyũmba-inĩ ya mũthamaki na akĩmũthaamia marĩ hamwe na ndungata icio ciake cia airĩtu akĩmũtwara handũ harĩa hega mũno kũu nyũmba-inĩ ĩyo yatũũragwo nĩ andũ-a- nja.
english: The maiden pleased him, and she obtained kindness from him. He quickly gave her cosmetics and her portions of food, and the seven choice maidens who were to be given her out of the king’s house. He moved her and her maidens to the best place in the women’s house.

kikuyu: Mũthamaki mwathi wakwa, andũ a Isiraeli othe nĩwe macũthĩrĩirie, nĩguo ũmamenyithie nũũ ũgaikarĩra gĩtĩ kĩa ũnene thuutha waku wee mũthamaki, mwathi wakwa.
english: You, my lord the king, the eyes of all Israel are on you, that you should tell them who will sit on the throne of my lord the king after him.

kikuyu: Ariũ a Kohathu maarĩ Amuramu, na Iziharu, na Hebironi, na Uzieli. Kohathu aatũũrire muoyo mĩaka 133.
english: The sons of Kohath: Amram, and Izhar, and Hebron, and Uzziel; and the years of the life of Kohath were one hundred thirty-three years.

kikuyu: Nao athamaki acio atano a Aamori, nao nĩ athamaki a Jerusalemu, na Hebironi, na Jaramuthu, na Lakishi, na Egiloni, magĩcookanĩrĩra hamwe. Makĩambata na mbũtũ ciao ciothe cia ita, nao makĩamba hema ciao mangʼetheire itũũra rĩa Gibeoni, makĩrĩtharĩkĩra.
english: Therefore the five kings of the Amorites, the king of Jerusalem, the king of Hebron, the king of Jarmuth, the king of Lachish, and the king of Eglon, gathered themselves together and went up, they and all their armies, and encamped against Gibeon, and made war against it.

kikuyu: Menyai atĩ Jehova nĩwe Ngai. Nĩwe watũũmbire, na ithuĩ tũrĩ ake, tũrĩ andũ ake, ngʼondu cia ũrĩithio wake.
english: Know that Yahweh, he is God. It is he who has made us, and we are his. We are his people, and the sheep of his pasture.

kikuyu: O na acio maruaga matiathĩkagĩra watho, no nĩmendaga inyuĩ mũrue nĩguo metĩĩage nĩ ũndũ wa mĩĩrĩ yanyu.
english: For even they who receive circumcision don’t keep the law themselves, but they desire to have you circumcised, so that they may boast in your flesh.

kikuyu: kuuma kũrĩ Jezeri, nĩ mbarĩ ya Ajezeri; kuuma kũrĩ Shilemu, nĩ mbarĩ ya Ashilemu.
english: of Jezer, the family of the Jezerites; of Shillem, the family of the Shillemites.

kikuyu: Mũndũ ũyũ mũthĩĩni aakaĩire Jehova, nake Jehova akĩmũigua, akĩmũhonokia mathĩĩna-inĩ make mothe.
english: This poor man cried, and Yahweh heard him, and saved him out of all his troubles.

kikuyu: Nĩmeyohage iremba cia gatani mĩtwe, na nguo cia gatani cia thĩinĩ njohero-inĩ. Matikanehumbe kĩndũ o na kĩrĩkũ kĩngĩtũma mathigine.
english: They shall have linen turbans on their heads, and shall have linen trousers on their waists. They shall not clothe themselves with anything that makes them sweat.

kikuyu: “Ĩra andũ a Isiraeli ũũ: ‘Rĩrĩa mũndũ o na ũrĩkũ angĩĩhia atekwenda, na eke ũndũ ũrĩa mũkananie thĩinĩ wa maathani mothe ma Jehova:
english: “Speak to the children of Israel, saying, ‘If anyone sins unintentionally, in any of the things which Yahweh has commanded not to be done, and does any one of them,

kikuyu: Ndĩ na hinya wa gũkũgera ngero; no rĩrĩ, ũtukũ wa ira Ngai wa thoguo aranjĩĩrire atĩrĩ, ‘Menya wĩre Jakubu ũndũ, ũrĩ mwega kana ũrĩ mũũru.’
english: It is in the power of my hand to hurt you, but the God of your father spoke to me last night, saying, ‘Be careful that you don’t speak to Jacob either good or bad.’

kikuyu: Huhai karumbeta kũu Zayuni, anĩrĩrai gĩathĩ kĩamũre gĩa kwĩhinga kũrĩa irio, ĩtanai kĩũngano gĩtheru.
english: Blow the trumpet in Zion! Sanctify a fast. Call a solemn assembly.

kikuyu: ũkahaana ta njagĩ ĩrĩa ĩmenyerete gũikara werũ-inĩ, ĩrĩa ĩnuhanuhaga rĩrĩa ĩkwenda kũhaicwo; rĩrĩa ĩrĩ na mũrukĩ-rĩ, nũũ ũngĩhota kũmĩrigĩrĩria? Njamba iria ingĩmĩenda, itingĩĩnogia ikĩmĩcaria;
english: a wild donkey used to the wilderness, that sniffs the wind in her craving. When she is in heat, who can turn her away? All those who seek her will not weary themselves. In her month, they will find her.

kikuyu: Mũthamaki Zedekia akĩĩra Jeremia atĩrĩ, “Nĩndĩretigĩra Ayahudi arĩa meneanĩte kũrĩ andũ a Babuloni, nĩ ũndũ andũ a Babuloni mahota kũũneana kũrĩ Ayahudi acio nao maanyamarie.”
english: Zedekiah the king said to Jeremiah, “I am afraid of the Jews who have defected to the Chaldeans, lest they deliver me into their hand, and they mock me.”

kikuyu: akamũguthũkĩra angʼathĩtie, arĩ na ngo yake mata ya hinya.
english: he runs at him with a stiff neck, with the thick shields of his bucklers,

kikuyu: No Ngai akiumĩrĩra Labani ũcio Mũsuriata kĩroto-inĩ, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Menya wĩre Jakubu ũndũ o na ũrĩkũ, ũrĩ mwega kana ũrĩ mũũru.”
english: God came to Laban the Syrian in a dream of the night, and said to him, “Be careful that you don’t speak to Jacob either good or bad.”

kikuyu: Ningĩ mũraika ũcio akĩnjĩĩra atĩrĩ, “Maaĩ macio wonire hau mũmaraya ũcio aikarĩte nĩ iruka cia andũ, na irĩndĩ, na ndũrĩrĩ, na thiomi.
english: He said to me, “The waters which you saw, where the prostitute sits, are peoples, multitudes, nations, and languages.

kikuyu: Nĩngamũrehe werũ-inĩ wa ndũrĩrĩ, na kũu nĩkuo ngaamũtuĩra ciira tũkĩonanaga ũthiũ kwa ũthiũ.
english: I will bring you into the wilderness of the peoples, and there I will enter into judgment with you face to face.

kikuyu: Marigithathi macio marĩ ma mweri ũmwe, no nginya ũkaamakũũrithia na thogora wa gũkũũrana ũrĩa ũtuĩtwo wa cekeri ithano cia betha,† kũringana na cekeri ya handũ-harĩa-haamũre, ĩrĩa ĩrĩ na ũritũ wa geera mĩrongo ĩĩrĩ.
english: You shall redeem those who are to be redeemed of them from a month old, according to your estimation, for five shekels of money, according to the shekel† of the sanctuary, which weighs twenty gerahs.‡

kikuyu: Andũ arĩa makũũrĩtwo nĩ Jehova nĩmakiuge ũguo: o acio akũũrĩte kuuma guoko-inĩ gwa thũ,
english: Let the redeemed by Yahweh say so, whom he has redeemed from the hand of the adversary,

kikuyu: nĩndĩrabanga gwĩka ũguo ngĩthiĩ Sipania. Nĩndĩrehoka gũkaamũceerera ngĩhĩtũkĩra kũu kwanyu, na atĩ nĩmũkandeithĩrĩria gũthiĩ rũgendo rũu, thuutha wa gũkenanĩra na inyuĩ ihinda inini.
english: whenever I travel to Spain, I will come to you. For I hope to see you on my journey, and to be helped on my way there by you, if first I may enjoy your company for a while.

kikuyu: Harĩ indo cianyu cia wĩra-rĩ, gĩagai na kĩndũ gĩa gũcimbaga nakĩo, na rĩrĩa mũndũ athiĩ gwĩteithia, akenja irima agathika kĩoro gĩake.
english: You shall have a trowel among your weapons. It shall be, when you relieve yourself, you shall dig with it, and shall turn back and cover your excrement;

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, mũndũ ũcio agĩthiĩ akĩambĩrĩria kũheana ũhoro kũu Dekapoli wa maũndũ marĩa mothe Jesũ aamwĩkĩire. Nao andũ othe makĩgega. Kairĩtu Gaakuĩte na Mũtumia warĩ Mũrũaru
english: He went his way, and began to proclaim in Decapolis how Jesus had done great things for him, and everyone marveled.

kikuyu: Mokĩte ta matoonyeire mwanya-inĩ mwariĩ; mokĩire gatagatĩ ga kũu kwanangĩku, makaamomokera.
english: As through a wide breach they come. They roll themselves in amid the ruin.

kikuyu: Maarĩkia kuumĩria marikabu, makĩona mwaki wa makara wakĩtio hau, na thamaki irĩ njĩkĩre riiko, na mũgate.
english: So when they got out on the land, they saw a fire of coals there, with fish and bread laid on it.

kikuyu: Mũtikahaane ta maithe manyu ma tene arĩa maahunjagĩrio nĩ anabii a tene, makeerwo atĩrĩ: Ũũ nĩguo Jehova Mwene-Hinya- Wothe ekuuga: ‘Garũrũkai mũtige mĩthiĩre yanyu mĩũru o na mĩtugo yanyu mĩũru.’ No makĩaga kũnjigua kana kũrũmbũiya uuge wakwa, nĩguo Jehova ekuuga.
english: Don’t you be like your fathers, to whom the former prophets proclaimed, saying: Yahweh of Armies says, ‘Return now from your evil ways and from your evil doings;’ but they didn’t hear nor listen to me, says Yahweh.

kikuyu: No Jona agĩũkĩra nĩguo orĩre Jehova, akĩerekera Tarishishi. Agĩikũrũka nginya Jopa na agĩkorerera marikabu yathiiaga Tarishishi. Aarĩkia kũrĩha thogora wa rũgendo, akĩingĩra marikabu nĩguo athiĩ Tarishishi, orĩre Jehova.
english: But Jonah rose up to flee to Tarshish from the presence of Yahweh. He went down to Joppa, and found a ship going to Tarshish; so he paid its fare, and went down into it, to go with them to Tarshish from the presence of Yahweh.

kikuyu: Nao athĩnjĩri-Ngai magĩcooka makĩrehe ithandũkũ rĩu rĩa kĩrĩkanĩro kĩa Jehova handũ harĩo harĩa haamũre thĩinĩ wa hekarũ, ũguo nĩ ta kuuga Handũ-harĩa-Hatheru-Mũno, makĩrĩiga rungu rwa mathagu ma makerubi.
english: The priests brought in the ark of Yahweh’s covenant to its place, into the inner sanctuary of the house, to the most holy place, even under the wings of the cherubim.

kikuyu: Eeheragia irĩma itekũmenya, na agacingʼaũrania nĩ kũrakara.
english: He removes the mountains, and they don’t know it, when he overturns them in his anger.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo ndungata icio cia andũ-a-nja na ciana ciacio igĩkuhĩrĩria na makĩinamĩrĩra.
english: Then the servants came near with their children, and they bowed themselves.

kikuyu: Guoko-inĩ gwake kwa ũrĩo aanyiitĩte njata mũgwanja, na kuuma kanua-inĩ gake hakoima rũhiũ rwa njora rũũgĩ mĩena yeerĩ.* Naguo ũthiũ wake watariĩ ta riũa rĩrĩa rĩarĩte mũno.
english: He had seven stars in his right hand. Out of his mouth proceeded a sharp two-edged sword. His face was like the sun shining at its brightest.

kikuyu: Wamanangire o ta ũrĩa marikabu cia Tarishishi cianangagwo nĩ rũhuho ruumĩte mwena wa irathĩro.
english: With the east wind, you break the ships of Tarshish.

kikuyu: Mũthamaki Rehoboamu agĩtũma Adoniramu ũrĩa warĩ mũrũgamĩrĩri wa andũ arĩa maarutitha- gio wĩra na hinya, no andũ a Isiraeli makĩmũhũũra na mahiga nyuguto, agĩkua. No Rehoboamu agĩĩthara, akĩhaica ngaari-inĩ yake ya ita, akĩũrĩra Jerusalemu.
english: Then King Rehoboam sent Adoram, who was over the men subject to forced labor; and all Israel stoned him to death with stones. King Rehoboam hurried to get himself up to his chariot, to flee to Jerusalem.

kikuyu: Makĩĩra Samũeli atĩrĩ, “Ndũgaatige gũkaĩra Jehova Ngai witũ nĩ ũndũ witũ, nĩgeetha atũhonokie kuuma guoko-inĩ kwa Afilisti.”
english: The children of Israel said to Samuel, “Don’t stop crying to Yahweh our God for us, that he will save us out of the hand of the Philistines.”

kikuyu: ci nĩ thimo cia Solomoni mũrũ wa Daudi, ũrĩa warĩ mũthamaki wa Isiraeli:
english: he proverbs of Solomon, the son of David, king of Israel:

kikuyu: Saũlũ akĩĩra ndungata yake atĩrĩ, “Tũngĩthiĩ-rĩ, nĩ kĩĩ tũngĩhe mũndũ ũcio? Irio nĩ thiru makũnia- inĩ maitũ. Tũtirĩ na kĩheo gĩa gũtwarĩra mũndũ wa Ngai. Nĩ kĩĩ tũrĩ nakĩo?”
english: Then Saul said to his servant, “But behold, if we go, what should we bring the man? For the bread is spent in our sacks, and there is not a present to bring to the man of God. What do we have?”

kikuyu: Gĩcango kĩrĩa kĩarutirwo itugĩ-inĩ icio cierĩ, na Karia kau, na ndegwa iria ikũmi na igĩrĩ cia gĩcango iria ciarĩ rungu rwako, na metha, iria Mũthamaki Solomoni aathondekete cia hekarũ ya Jehova, gĩtingĩathimĩkire nĩ kũingĩha.
english: They took the two pillars, the one sea, and the twelve bronze bulls that were under the bases, which King Solomon had made for Yahweh’s house. The bronze of all these vessels was without weight.

kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, mũmwĩtiirie, mwĩhaandĩte wega thĩinĩ wa wĩtĩkio, tondũ nĩmũmenyete atĩ ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe wanyu gũkũ thĩ guothe maranyariirĩka o ta inyuĩ.
english: Withstand him steadfast in your faith, knowing that your brothers who are in the world are undergoing the same sufferings.

kikuyu: Ti-itherũ nĩũiguĩte ũrĩa athamaki a Ashuri mekĩte mabũrũri-inĩ mothe, ũrĩa maamaniinire biũ. Inyuĩ mũgũkĩhonoka atĩa?
english: Behold, you have heard what the kings of Assyria have done to all lands, by destroying them utterly. Shall you be delivered?

kikuyu: “Rĩrĩa mũkoona kĩrĩa kĩrĩ thaahu na gĩa kũrehe ihooru kĩarũgama harĩa gĩtagĩrĩirwo nĩ kũrũgama-rĩ, (ũrĩa ũrathoma nĩamenye ũhoro ũcio), hĩndĩ ĩyo arĩa marĩ Judea nĩmoorĩre irĩma-inĩ.
english: “But when you see the abomination of desolation,* spoken of by Daniel the prophet, standing where it ought not” (let the reader understand), “then let those who are in Judea flee to the mountains,

kikuyu: na Mathayo, na Toma, na Jakubu wa Alufayo, na Simoni ũrĩa wetagwo Zelote,
english: Matthew; Thomas; James the son of Alphaeus; Simon who was called the Zealot;

kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, wonjorithia warĩo na uumithio warĩo nĩikamũrĩrwo Jehova; itikaigwo igĩĩna-inĩ kana ihithwo. Uumithio warĩo ũgaatuĩka wa arĩa matũũraga marongoragia Jehova, moonage irio nyingĩ na nguo njega.
english: Her merchandise and her wages will be holiness to Yahweh. It will not be treasured nor laid up; for her merchandise will be for those who dwell before Yahweh, to eat sufficiently, and for durable clothing.

kikuyu: ehova, tũteithie, nĩgũkorwo andũ arĩa etigĩri Ngai nĩmaniinĩtwo; andũ arĩa ehokeku nĩmathirĩte makoima gatagatĩ-inĩ ka andũ.
english: elp, Yahweh; for the godly man ceases. For the faithful fail from among the children of men.

kikuyu: ti na ũndũ wa ciĩko iria mekĩte, no nĩ ũndũ wa wendi wa ũcio wĩtanaga, nĩerire Rebeka atĩrĩ, “Ũrĩa mũkũrũ nĩagatungatĩra ũrĩa mũnini.”
english: it was said to her, “The elder will serve the younger.”*

kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo mũndũ o wothe ũrĩa ũtoonyaga kĩhurũko-inĩ kĩa Ngai o nake nĩahurũkaga agatigana na wĩra wake, o ta ũrĩa Ngai aahurũkire agĩtigana na wĩra wake.
english: For he who has entered into his rest has himself also rested from his works, as God did from his.

kikuyu: Mĩĩrĩ yothe ndĩhaanaine: Andũ marĩ mũthemba ũmwe wa mwĩrĩ, nacio nyamũ mũthemba ũngĩ, na nyoni mũthemba ũngĩ, o nacio thamaki irĩ mwĩrĩ wa mũthemba ũngĩ.
english: All flesh is not the same flesh, but there is one flesh of men, another flesh of animals, another of fish, and another of birds.

kikuyu: Ririkana atĩ wanyũũmbire ta rĩũmba. Rĩu ũgũcooka ũndue rũkũngũ rĩngĩ?
english: Remember, I beg you, that you have fashioned me as clay. Will you bring me into dust again?

kikuyu: Rĩrĩa maathiiaga na njĩra, mũndũ ũmwe akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Nĩndĩkũrũmagĩrĩra kũrĩa guothe ũrĩthiiaga.”
english: As they went on the way, a certain man said to him, “I want to follow you wherever you go, Lord.”

kikuyu: No Jehova ũrĩa wamũrutire bũrũri wa Misiri na hinya mũnene na atambũrũkĩtie guoko gwake, ũcio nowe wiki mwagĩrĩirwo nĩkũhooyaga. Nĩwe
english: but you shall fear Yahweh, who brought you up out of the land of Egypt with great power and with an outstretched arm, and you shall bow

kikuyu: Nao makĩrehe ndegwa mũgwanja, na ndũrũme mũgwanja, na tũtũrũme mũgwanja, o na thenge mũgwanja cia igongona rĩa kũhoroherio mehia makoniĩ ũthamaki ũcio, na ma handũ-harĩa-haamũre o na ma Juda. Nake mũthamaki agĩatha athĩnjĩri-Ngai, o acio a njiaro cia Harũni, macithĩnjĩre hau kĩgongona-inĩ kĩa Jehova.
english: They brought seven bulls, seven rams, seven lambs, and seven male goats, for a sin offering for the kingdom, for the sanctuary, and for Judah. He commanded the priests the sons of Aaron to offer them on Yahweh’s altar.

kikuyu: Gũgĩkinya mũthenya wa mĩrongo ĩĩrĩ na mũgwanja wa mweri wa keerĩ-rĩ, thĩ nĩyaniarĩte biũ.
english: In the second month, on the twenty-seventh day of the month, the earth was dry.

kikuyu: Wee Jehova, hĩndĩ nĩnginyu wĩke ũndũ, tondũ andũ nĩmathũkĩtie watho waku.
english: It is time to act, Yahweh, for they break your law.

kikuyu: agĩtũmana macookio mamwĩre atĩrĩ, “Ndũgeciirie atĩ tondũ ũrĩ thĩinĩ wa nyũmba ya mũthamaki atĩ wee nowe wiki ũkaahonoka, nao Ayahudi othe maniinwo.
english: Then Mordecai asked them to return this answer to Esther: “Don’t think to yourself that you will escape in the king’s house any more than all the Jews.

kikuyu: Niĩ ndiũrĩte ngatiga gũtuĩka mũrĩithi waku; wee nĩũũĩ atĩ ndiĩrirĩirie mũthenya wa mũtino. Ũrĩa wothe njaragia na kanua gakwa ndũhithĩtwo harĩwe.
english: As for me, I have not hurried from being a shepherd after you. I haven’t desired the woeful day. You know. That which came out of my lips was before your face.

kikuyu: Aakuhĩrĩria Bethifage na Bethania, o kũu Kĩrĩma-inĩ kĩa Mĩtamaiyũ, agĩtũma arutwo ake eerĩ, akĩmeera atĩrĩ,
english: When he came near to Bethsphage‡ and Bethany, at the mountain that is called Olivet, he sent two of his disciples,

kikuyu: mũthenya ũrĩa agooka nĩguo atũũgĩrio thĩinĩ wa andũ ake arĩa atheru, o na andũ othe arĩa mamwĩtĩkĩtie magege nĩ ũndũ wake. O na inyuĩ mũgaakorwo mũrĩ amwe ao tondũ nĩmwetĩkirie ũira witũ ũrĩa twamũheire.
english: when he comes in that day to be glorified in his saints and to be admired among all those who have believed, because our testimony to you was believed.

kikuyu: Ithuĩ tũhatĩkĩtwo mũno na mĩena yothe, no tũtihehenjetwo; nĩtũrigĩtwo nĩ wa gwĩka, no tũtikuĩte ngoro;
english: We are pressed on every side, yet not crushed; perplexed, yet not to despair;

kikuyu: Nao magĩkĩrathima Rebeka makĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Mwarĩ wa maitũ, ũroingĩha ũtuĩke nyina wa ngiri maita ngiri, naruo rũciaro rwaku rũroĩgwatĩra ihingo cia thũ ciao.”
english: They blessed Rebekah, and said to her, “Our sister, may you be the mother of thousands of ten thousands, and let your offspring possess the gate of those who hate them.”

kikuyu: Mũndũ o wothe angĩrega kũmũnyiita ũgeni kana arege gũthikĩrĩria ciugo cianyu-rĩ, mũkiuma mũciĩ ũcio kana itũũra rĩu-rĩ, mũkaaribariba magũrũ manyu nĩguo rũkũngũ rũitĩke.*
english: Whoever doesn’t receive you or hear your words, as you go out of that house or that city, shake the dust off your feet.

kikuyu: Ũũ nĩguo Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe, Ngai wa Isiraeli, ekuuganĩũndũwaAhabumũrũwaKolaia, naZedekiamũrũwaMaaseia, oaciomamũrathagĩramo- horo ma maheeni makĩgwetaga rĩĩtwa rĩakwa: “Nĩngamaneana kũrĩ Nebukadinezaru mũthamaki wa Babuloni, nake nĩakamooraga mũkĩĩonagĩra na maitho manyu.
english: Yahweh of Armies, the God of Israel, says concerning Ahab the son of Kolaiah, and concerning Zedekiah the son of Maaseiah, who prophesy a lie to you in my name: “Behold, I will deliver them into the hand of Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon; and he will kill them before your eyes.

kikuyu: Nake akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Rĩu akorwo ũrĩ na kabeti kana mũhuko-rĩ, kuua; na akorwo ndũrĩ na rũhiũ rwa njora-rĩ, endia nguo yaku ya igũrũ, ũrũgũre.
english: Then he said to them, “But now, whoever has a purse, let him take it, and likewise a bag. Whoever has none, let him sell his cloak, and buy a sword.

kikuyu: Mahĩtũkaga na ihenya mũno ta tũtarũ twa irura,* kana ta nderi igũcuuhũkĩra kĩndũ gĩa kũrĩa.
english: They have passed away as the swift ships, as the eagle that swoops on the prey.

kikuyu: O na ũngĩthuthuuria ngoro yakwa na ũnduĩrie ũtukũ, o na ũngĩngeria, ndũrĩ ũndũ mũũru ũngĩona; nĩnduĩte atĩ ndikehia na kanua gakwa.
english: You have proved my heart. You have visited me in the night. You have tried me, and found nothing. I have resolved that my mouth shall not disobey.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio-rĩ, twabatithio nĩgũthikwo twathikirwo hamwe nake gĩkuũ-inĩ gĩake, nĩgeetha o ta ũrĩa Kristũ aariũkirio kuuma kũrĩ arĩa akuũ nĩ hinya wa riiri wa Ithe, o na ithuĩ tũtũũre na mũtũũrĩre mwerũ.
english: We were buried therefore with him through baptism into death, that just as Christ was raised from the dead through the glory of the Father, so we also might walk in newness of life.

kikuyu: nake Roho wa Jehova akĩambĩrĩria gũkaarahũra o hĩndĩ ĩyo kaarĩ kũu Mahane-Dani, gatagatĩ ga Zora na Eshitaoli.
english: Yahweh’s Spirit began to move him in Mahaneh Dan, between Zorah and Eshtaol.

kikuyu: na makĩĩra Musa atĩrĩ, “Twarĩrie arĩ we, na nĩtũgũkũigua, no tiga kũreka twarĩrio nĩ Ngai, tũtigakue.”
english: They said to Moses, “Speak with us yourself, and we will listen; but don’t let God speak with us, lest we die.”

kikuyu: Mumunyai Mũriũ, ndakae kũrakara, na inyuĩ mũniinĩrwo njĩra-inĩ, nĩgũkorwo mangʼũrĩ make maahota kũrĩrĩmbũka o rĩmwe. Kũrathimwo-rĩ, nĩ arĩa othe moragĩra harĩ we.
english: Give sincere homage to the Son,§ lest he be angry, and you perish on the way, for his wrath will soon be kindled. Blessed are all those who take refuge in him.

kikuyu: nĩguo marũmagie mataaro make, na maathĩkagĩre mawatho make. Goocai Jehova.
english: that they might keep his statutes, and observe his laws. Praise Yah!

kikuyu: Naguo mweri ũkaara ta riũa, na ũtheri wa riũa ũnenehio maita mũgwanja, ũhaane ta ũtheri wa mĩthenya mũgwanja mĩgima, rĩrĩa Jehova akooha nguraro cia andũ ake na amahonie ironda iria aamatiihirie.
english: Moreover the light of the moon will be like the light of the sun, and the light of the sun will be seven times brighter, like the light of seven days, in the day that Yahweh binds up the fracture of his people, and heals the wound they were struck with.

kikuyu: Kuuma kũrĩ andũ a Amuramu, na Iziharu, na Hebironi, na Uzieli maarĩ:
english: Of the Amramites, of the Izharites, of the Hebronites, of the Uzzielites:

kikuyu: nĩguo agarũre roho wake ndũkaharũrũkio mbĩrĩra, nĩgeetha ũtheri ũcio wa muoyo ũmũtherere.
english: to bring back his soul from the pit, that he may be enlightened with the light of the living.

kikuyu: Matũ makwa nĩmaiguĩte ũhoro waku no rĩu maitho makwa nĩgũkuona makuonete.
english: I had heard of you by the hearing of the ear, but now my eye sees you.

kikuyu: “Mũtikanahinyĩrĩrie mũtumia wa ndigwa kana mwana wa ngoriai.
english: “You shall not take advantage of any widow or fatherless child.

kikuyu: Nake Ruthu ũcio Mũmoabi akĩĩra Naomi atĩrĩ, “Reke thiĩ mĩgũnda-inĩ ngahaare cairi ĩrĩa ĩtigĩtio;* ndĩ thuutha wa mũndũ o wothe ũrĩa ingĩĩtĩkĩrĩka maitho-inĩ make.” Nake Naomi akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Thiĩ, mwarĩ wakwa.”
english: :21 2 1 Naomi had a relative of her husband’s, a mighty man of wealth, of the family of Elimelech, and his name was Boaz. 2 Ruth the Moabitess said to Naomi, “Let me now go to the field, and glean among the ears of grain after him in whose sight I find favor.” She said to her, “Go, my daughter.”

kikuyu: Akĩmeera atĩrĩ, ‘O na inyuĩ-rĩ, thiĩi mũkarute wĩra mũgũnda-inĩ wakwa wa mĩthabibũ, na nĩngũmũrĩha o kĩrĩa kĩagĩrĩire.’
english: He said to them, ‘You also go into the vineyard, and whatever is right I will give you.’ So they went their way.

kikuyu: Matukũ-inĩ ma thuutha, rĩrĩa mũrũguo agaakũũria atĩrĩ, “Gĩtũmi kĩa irĩkanĩro icio, na kĩrĩra kĩa watho wa kũrũmĩrĩrwo na mawatho marĩa Jehova Ngai aamwathĩte mũrũmagĩrĩre, nĩ kĩĩ?”
english: When your son asks you in time to come, saying, “What do the testimonies, the statutes, and the ordinances, which Yahweh our God has commanded you mean?”

kikuyu: ake agĩtoonya gatarũ, akĩringa iria, agĩkinya itũũra rĩa kwao.
english: e entered into a boat and crossed over, and came into his own city.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Esiteri, mũtumia wa mũthamaki, mwarĩ wa Abihaili, marĩ na Moridekai ũrĩa Mũyahudi, makĩandĩka marĩ na ũhoti wothe ũrĩa maarĩ naguo, nĩguo mekĩre marũa macio ma keerĩ meegiĩ Purimu hinya.
english: Then Esther the queen, the daughter of Abihail, and Mordecai the Jew wrote with all authority to confirm this second letter of Purim.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Daudi akĩĩhitha kũu mũgũnda-inĩ, na hĩndĩ ĩrĩa gĩathĩ gĩa Karũgamo ka Mweri gĩakinyire-rĩ, mũthamaki agĩikara thĩ arĩe irio.
english: Concerning the matter which you and I have spoken of, behold, Yahweh is between you and me forever.” When the new moon had come, the king sat himself down to eat food.

kikuyu: Tondũ nĩendeete maithe manyu ma tene, na agĩthuura njiaro iria igooka thuutha wao, nĩamũrũtire bũrũri wa Misiri na hinya mũnene, na agĩtwarana na inyuĩ,
english: Because he loved your fathers, therefore he chose their offspring after them, and brought you out with his presence, with his great power, out of Egypt;

kikuyu: Mũcirutanĩrie na thenge ĩmwe ĩrĩ igongona rĩa kũhoroherio mehia, yongererwo harĩ igongona rĩa njino rĩa hĩndĩ ciothe, hamwe na iruta rĩa mũtu na iruta rĩao rĩa kũnyuuo.
english: and one male goat for a sin offering, in addition to the continual burnt offering, with its meal offering and their drink offerings.

kikuyu: Magĩthondeka mĩrĩnga ĩĩrĩ ya thahabu na makĩmĩnyiitithania na koine igĩrĩ cia gakuo ga gĩthũri mũico-inĩ wa na thĩinĩ, kũrigania na ebodi.
english: They made two rings of gold, and put them on the two ends of the breastplate, on its edge, which was toward the side of the ephod inward.

kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, nĩ ũndũ wa ũguo, mahĩtia ma Jakubu nĩmakahoroherio mathire, namo maciaro maya nĩmo makoonekana nĩ ũndũ wa kwehererio mehia make: Atĩ mahiga mothe ma kĩgongona akaamatua ta coka ũhehenjetwo ũgatuĩka tũcunjĩ, na gũtirĩ itugĩ cia Ashera kana igongona cia gũcinĩra ũbumba igaatigwo irũgamĩte.
english: Therefore by this the iniquity of Jacob will be forgiven, and this is all the fruit of taking away his sin: that he makes all the stones of the altar as chalk stones that are beaten in pieces, so that the Asherah poles and the incense altars shall rise no more.

kikuyu: No mũndũ ũcio ndaarĩ wa rũciaro rwa Lawi, no nĩamũkĩrire gĩcunjĩ gĩa ikũmi kuuma kũrĩ Iburahĩmu, na akĩrathima ũcio werĩirwo ciĩranĩro.
english: but he whose genealogy is not counted from them has accepted tithes from Abraham, and has blessed him who has the promises.

kikuyu: na Jeremia aarĩ wa ikũmi, na Makabanai aarĩ wa ikũmi na ũmwe.
english: Jeremiah the tenth, and Machbannai the eleventh.

kikuyu: na mĩtĩ ya matawa ya thahabu therie hamwe na matawa mayo ma gwakanaga marĩ mbere ya harĩa haamũre o ta ũrĩa gwathanĩtwo;
english: and the lamp stands with their lamps to burn according to the ordinance before the inner sanctuary, of pure gold;

kikuyu: Hadadi arĩ kũu Misiri akĩigua atĩ Daudi nĩahurũkĩte hamwe na maithe make, na atĩ Joabu mũnene wa ita o nake nĩarĩkĩtie gũkua. Hĩndĩ ĩyo Hadadi akĩĩra Firaũni atĩrĩ, “Njĩtĩkĩria thiĩ, nĩguo njooke bũrũri witũ.”
english: When Hadad heard in Egypt that David slept with his fathers, and that Joab the captain of the army was dead, Hadad said to Pharaoh, “Let me depart, that I may go to my own country.”

kikuyu: a rĩrĩ, Samũeli agĩkua, nao andũ othe a Isiraeli makĩũngana makĩmũrĩrĩra; makĩmũthika mũciĩ gwake kũu Rama. Nake Daudi agĩikũrũka agĩthiĩ Werũ wa Maoni.
english: amuel died; and all Israel gathered themselves together and mourned for him, and buried him at his house at Ramah. Then David arose and went down to the wilderness of Paran.

kikuyu: nake akiuga atĩrĩ, “Ũũ nĩguo Jehova ekuuga: Enjai mĩtaro ĩiyũre gĩtuamba gĩkĩ.
english: He said, “Yahweh says, ‘Make this valley full of trenches.’

kikuyu: “Nake mwathi wayo akĩmĩcookeria atĩrĩ, ‘Wee ndungata ĩno njaganu na ngũũtaru! Ũguo nĩ wooĩ atĩ ngethaga kũrĩa itahaandĩte, na ngongania kũrĩa itaahurĩte mbeũ?
english: “But his lord answered him, ‘You wicked and slothful servant. You knew that I reap where I didn’t sow, and gather where I didn’t scatter.

kikuyu: Ningĩ magĩthondeka ngathĩka mĩrongo ĩtano cia thahabu na magĩcihũthĩra kũohithania itambaya icio cia gũcuurio cia mĩena yeerĩ, nĩ ũndũ ũcio hema ĩyo nyamũre ĩgĩtuĩka kĩndũ kĩmwe.
english: He made fifty clasps of gold, and coupled the curtains to one another with the clasps: so the tabernacle was a unit.

kikuyu: Wee Mwathani, nĩngũkũgooca, gatagatĩ-inĩ ka ndũrĩrĩ; nĩngũkũinĩra ngũgooce ndĩ gatagatĩ-inĩ ka andũ.
english: I will give thanks to you, Lord, among the peoples. I will sing praises to you among the nations.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio rĩrĩa Ahija aaiguire mũkinyo wa magũrũ make arĩ mũrango-inĩ, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Toonya, mũtumia wa Jeroboamu. Ũretua tiwe nĩkĩ? Nĩndũmĩtwo kũrĩ we na ũhoro ũtarĩ mwega.
english: So when Ahijah heard the sound of her feet as she came in at the door, he said, “Come in, Jeroboam’s wife! Why do you pretend to be another? For I am sent to you with heavy news.

kikuyu: njamba ciao cia ita igĩthiĩ ũtukũ wothe nginya Bethi-Shani. Makĩruta mwĩrĩ wa Saũlũ na ya ariũ ake atatũ kuuma rũthingo-inĩ rwa Bethi-Shani, na magĩthiĩ nayo Jabeshi, magĩcooka makĩmĩcinĩra kuo.
english: all the valiant men arose, went all night, and took the body of Saul and the bodies of his sons from the wall of Beth Shan; and they came to Jabesh and burned them there.

kikuyu: Nĩmũgaikara mwĩohete iremba mĩtwe na mwĩkĩrĩte iraatũ magũrũ. Mũtigacakaya kana mũrĩre, no nĩmũkahwererekera tondũ wa mehia manyu na mũcaayage thĩinĩ wanyu.
english: Your turbans will be on your heads, and your sandals on your feet. You won’t mourn or weep; but you will pine away in

kikuyu: ee Jehova, nĩwe ndĩrakaĩra; hiũha ũũke kũrĩ niĩ. Igua mũgambo wakwa ngĩgũkaĩra.
english: ahweh, I have called on you. Come to me quickly! Listen to my voice when I call to you.

kikuyu: Atĩrĩrĩ, wee mwanake ũyũ, kenagĩra ũnini waku, na ũreke ngoro yaku ĩgũkenagie matukũ-inĩ ma wĩthĩ waku. Thiiaga na mĩthiĩre ĩrĩa wĩyendeire na ngoro yaku, o na ĩrĩa yonagwo ĩrĩ mĩega nĩ maitho maku, no ũmenye atĩrĩ, harĩ maũndũ macio mothe, Ngai nĩagagũciirithia.
english: Rejoice, young man, in your youth, and let your heart cheer you in the days of your youth, and walk in the ways of your heart, and in the sight of your eyes; but know that for all these things God will bring you into judgment.

kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa mũtumia ũmwe Mũsamaria okire gũtaha maaĩ-rĩ, Jesũ akĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “No ũũhe maaĩ ma kũnyua?”
english: A woman of Samaria came to draw water. Jesus said to her, “Give me a drink.”

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa aahingũrire Irima-rĩtarĩ-Gĩturi-rĩ, rĩkiuma ndogo yahaanaga ta ndogo ya icua inene. Narĩo riũa na matu igĩĩkĩrwo nduma nĩ ndogo ĩyo yoimaga Irima-rĩu-rĩtarĩ-Gĩturi.
english: He opened the pit of the abyss, and smoke went up out of the pit, like the smoke from a† burning furnace. The sun and the air were darkened because of the smoke from the pit.

kikuyu: Mwaka wa mĩrongo ĩtatũ na ĩnana wa Asa, mũthamaki wa Juda, Ahabu mũrũ wa Omuri agĩtuĩka mũthamaki wa Isiraeli, nake agĩthamakĩra Isiraeli arĩ Samaria mĩaka mĩrongo ĩĩrĩ na ĩĩrĩ.
english: In the thirty-eighth year of Asa king of Judah, Ahab the son of Omri began to reign over Israel. Ahab the son of Omri reigned over Israel in Samaria twenty-two years.

kikuyu: Thamaki cia iria-inĩ, na nyoni cia rĩera-inĩ, na nyamũ cia gĩthaka, na kanyamũ o gothe karĩa gathiiagĩra thĩ, o na andũ othe arĩa marĩ gũkũ thĩ nĩmakainaina maanyona. Irĩma nĩikangʼaũranio, na hurũrũka nĩikaragana, o na rũthingo o ruothe nĩrũkamomoka.
english: so that the fish of the sea, the birds of the sky, the animals of the field, all creeping things who creep on the earth, and all the men who are on the surface of the earth will shake at my presence. Then the mountains will be thrown down, the steep places will fall, and every wall will fall to the ground.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo o na Kristũ ndekenirie we mwene, no o ta ũrĩa kwandĩkĩtwo atĩrĩ: “Irumi cia arĩa makũrumaga nĩ niĩ ciagwĩrĩire.”
english: For even Christ didn’t please himself. But, as it is written, “The reproaches of those who reproached you fell on me.”*

kikuyu: Ndungata ciake ĩkĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Ũreka ũguo nĩkĩ? Rĩrĩa mwana ararĩ muoyo nĩũrehingĩte na ũkarĩra, no mwana akua-rĩ, wokĩra na warĩa irio!”
english: Then his servants said to him, “What is this that you have done? You fasted and wept for the child while he was alive, but when the child was dead, you rose up and ate bread.”

kikuyu: na akĩmatũma mathiĩ makahunjie ũhoro wa ũthamaki wa Ngai na mahonagie andũ arĩa arũaru.
english: He sent them out to preach God’s Kingdom and to heal the sick.

kikuyu: Daudi akĩĩra Gadi atĩrĩ, “Rĩu ndĩ na thĩĩna mũnene mũno. Nĩtũrekwo tũgwĩre moko-inĩ ma Jehova, nĩgũkorwo tha ciake nĩ nyingĩ; no ndũkareke ngwe moko-inĩ ma andũ.”
english: David said to Gad, “I am in distress. Let us fall now into Yahweh’s hand, for his mercies are great. Let me not fall into man’s hand.”

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo ciĩhoke Ngai na ciage kũriganĩrwo nĩ ciĩko ciake, no kũrũmia irũmagie maathani make.
english: that they might set their hope in God, and not forget God’s deeds, but keep his commandments,

kikuyu: Ririkana kiugo gĩaku kũrĩ ndungata yaku, nĩgũkorwo nĩũũheete kĩĩrĩgĩrĩro.
english: Remember your word to your servant, because you gave me hope.

kikuyu: Nĩnjũũĩ mũndũ thĩinĩ wa Kristũ, ũrĩa woirwo mĩaka ikũmi na ĩna mĩthiru, agĩtwarwo igũrũ rĩa gatatũ. Kana oirwo arĩ na mwĩrĩ kana atarĩ naguo, niĩ ndiũĩ, Ngai nĩwe ũũĩ.
english: I know a man in Christ who was caught up into the third heaven fourteen years ago—whether in the body, I don’t know, or whether out of the body, I don’t know; God knows.

kikuyu: Ithanwa nĩrĩigĩtwo itina- inĩ cia mĩtĩ, na mũtĩ o wothe ũtaciaraga maciaro mega ũrĩtemagwo ũgaikio mwaki-inĩ.”
english: Even now the ax also lies at the root of the trees. Every tree therefore that doesn’t produce good fruit is cut down and thrown into the fire.”

kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, o mĩaka ĩtatũ yathira, mũrutage icunjĩ cia ikũmi cia uumithio wanyu wothe wa mwaka ũcio, mũgaaciga kũu matũũra-inĩ manyu,
english: At the end of every three years you shall bring all the tithe of your increase in the same year, and shall store it within your gates.

kikuyu: Nao andũ-a-nja makĩriũkĩrio andũ ao arĩa maakuĩte, magĩcooka muoyo. Andũ angĩ nao nĩmaregire kũrekererio, makĩnyariirwo nĩgeetha makaariũkio magĩe na muoyo mwega makĩria.
english: Women received their dead by resurrection.* Others were tortured, not accepting their deliverance, that they might obtain a better resurrection.

kikuyu: Nĩnduĩkĩte ta kĩndũ gĩa kũgegania harĩ andũ aingĩ, no nĩwe rĩũrĩro rĩakwa rĩa hinya.
english: I am a marvel to many, but you are my strong refuge.

kikuyu: Arĩriai itũũra rĩa Jerusalemu na ũhooreri, na mwanĩrĩre, mũrĩĩre atĩrĩ, wĩra warĩo mũritũ nĩũthirĩte, na nĩrĩrekeirwo mehia marĩo, o na ningĩ atĩ nĩrĩherithĩtio nĩ guoko kwa Jehova maita meerĩ nĩ ũndũ wa mehia marĩo mothe.
english: “Speak comfortably to Jerusalem, and call out to her that her warfare is accomplished, that her iniquity is pardoned, that she has received of Yahweh’s hand double for all her sins.”

kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, angĩkorwo wagi witũ wa ũthingu nĩũtũmaga ũthingu wa Ngai ũguũrio mũno makĩria- rĩ, tũkiuge atĩa? No tuuge atĩ Ngai ti wa kĩhooto rĩrĩa egũtũkinyĩria mangʼũrĩ make? (Ndĩraaria o ta ũrĩa mũndũ wa gũkũ thĩ angĩaria.)
english: But if our unrighteousness commends the righteousness of God, what will we say? Is God unrighteous who inflicts wrath? I speak like men do.

kikuyu: ee Jehova, nĩ harĩwe njũrĩire; ndũkanareke njonorithio; nĩ ũndũ wa ũthingu waku-rĩ, ndeithũra.
english: n you, Yahweh, I take refuge. Let me never be disappointed. Deliver me in your righteousness.

kikuyu: O na kaana kamenyekaga nĩ ũndũ wa ciĩko ciako, gakamenyeka kana mĩtugo yako nĩ mĩtheru, na kana nĩ mĩagĩrĩru.
english: Even a child makes himself known by his doings, whether his work is pure, and whether it is right.

kikuyu: Na handũ hangĩ oigĩte atĩrĩ, “Wee ũrĩ mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai nginya tene, o ta ũrĩa Melikisedeki aatariĩ.”
english: As he says also in another place, “You are a priest forever, after the order of Melchizedek.”*

kikuyu: Niĩ ndĩmũbatithagia na maaĩ, nowe arĩmũbatithagia na Roho Mũtheru.” Kũbatithio na Kũgerio kwa Jesũ
english: I baptized you in§ water, but he will baptize you in the Holy Spirit.”

kikuyu: No Paũlũ na Baranaba magĩtigwo kũu Antiokia, kũrĩa o, na andũ angĩ aingĩ maarutanaga na makahunjia kiugo kĩa Mwathani. Paũlũ na Baranaba Gũkararania
english: But Paul and Barnabas stayed in Antioch, teaching and preaching the word of the Lord, with many others also.

kikuyu: Nake akĩnjĩĩra atĩrĩ, “Mũrũ wa mũndũ, nĩũrona ũrĩa mareeka; maũndũ marĩ magigi biũ marĩa andũ a nyũmba ya Isiraeli marekĩra haha, maũndũ ma kũndindĩka kũraya na handũ-hakwa-harĩa- haamũre? No wee-rĩ, nĩũkuona maũndũ o na marĩ magigi makĩria.”
english: He said to me, “Son of man, do you see what they do? Even the great abominations that the house of Israel commit here, that I should go far off from my sanctuary? But you will again see yet other great abominations.”

kikuyu: Wee nĩwe ũmenyagĩrĩra thĩ na ũkamiurĩria mbura; ũtũmaga ĩgĩe na bũthi mũingĩ. Rũũĩ rwa Ngai rũiyũrĩte maaĩ ma gũkũrĩria andũ irio, nĩgũkorwo ũguo nĩguo wathĩrĩirie gwĩkagwo.
english: You visit the earth, and water it. You greatly enrich it. The river of God is full of water. You provide them grain, for so you have ordained it.

kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, ithuĩ arĩa twĩtĩkĩtie nĩ ithuĩ tũtoonyaga kĩhurũko-inĩ kĩu, o ta ũrĩa Ngai oigire atĩrĩ, “Nĩ ũndũ ũcio ngĩĩhĩta na mwĩhĩtwa ndakarĩte, ngiuga atĩrĩ, ‘Matirĩ hĩndĩ magaatoonya kĩhurũko-inĩ gĩakwa.’ ” O na gũtuĩka wĩra wake nĩwathirĩte kuuma o rĩrĩa thĩ yombirwo.
english: For we who have believed do enter into that rest, even as he has said, “As I swore in my wrath, they will not enter into my rest;”* although the works were finished from the foundation of the world.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo Gatũrũme ũrĩa ũrĩ gatagatĩ ga gĩtĩ kĩa ũnene nĩwe ũgaatuĩka mũrĩithi wao; nĩakamatongoria, amakinyie ithima-inĩ cia maaĩ ma muoyo. Nake Ngai nĩakamagiria maithori mothe mathire maitho-inĩ mao.”
english: for the Lamb who is in the middle of the throne shepherds them and leads them to springs of life-giving waters. And God will wipe away every tear from their eyes.”

kikuyu: na Gedori, na Ahio, na Zekaria, na Mikilothu.
english: Gedor, Ahio, Zechariah, and Mikloth.

kikuyu: “Githĩ Jehova ndaaririkanire na agĩĩciiria ũhoro wa ũbumba ũcio wacinagĩrwo matũũra-inĩ macio ma Juda na njĩra-inĩ cia Jerusalemu nĩ inyuĩ, na maithe manyu, na athamaki anyu, na anene anyu, o na andũ othe a bũrũri ũcio?
english: “The incense that you burned in the cities of Judah, and in the streets of Jerusalem, you and your fathers, your kings and your princes, and the people of the land, didn’t Yahweh remember them, and didn’t it come into his mind?

kikuyu: hĩndĩ ĩyo nĩũgakena nĩ ũndũ wa Jehova, na nĩngatũma ũtũũgĩrio mũno gũkũ thĩ, na ngũheage igai rĩa thoguo Jakubu, ũrĩrĩĩage.” Nĩgũkorwo nĩ kanua ka Jehova kaarĩtie ũhoro ũcio.
english: then you will delight yourself in Yahweh, and I will make you to ride on the and I will feed you with the heritage of Jacob your father;” for Yahweh’s mouth has spoken it.

kikuyu: Ndĩmatũmaga mookĩrĩre rũrĩrĩ rũrĩa rũtooĩ Ngai, ngamatũma mookĩrĩre andũ arĩa mandakaragia, nĩguo matunyane indo, na mahurie ndaho, na mamarangĩrĩrie ta ndoro ya njĩra-inĩ.
english: I will send him against a profane nation, and against the people who anger me I will give him a command to take the plunder and to take the prey, and to tread them down like the mire of the streets.

kikuyu: Amosi agĩcookeria Amazia atĩrĩ, “Niĩ-rĩ, ndiarĩ mũnabii kana mũrũ wa mũnabii, no ndaarĩ mũrĩithi wa mahiũ na ningĩ mũmenyereri wa mĩkũyũ.
english: Then Amos answered Amaziah, “I was no prophet, neither was I a prophet’s son, but I was a herdsman, and a farmer of sycamore figs;

kikuyu: Mwĩrahei nĩ ũndũ wa rĩĩtwa rĩake itheru; ngoro cia arĩa marongoragia Jehova nĩikene.
english: Glory in his holy name. Let the heart of those who seek Yahweh rejoice.

kikuyu: nĩguo atũme Kristũ, o we ũrĩa wamũrĩtwo nĩ ũndũ wanyu, nake nĩwe Jesũ.
english: and that he may send Christ Jesus, who was ordained for you before,

kikuyu: Ningĩ akĩaria ndũmĩrĩri yake, akiuga atĩrĩ: “Ĩno nĩyo ndũmĩrĩri ya Balamu mũrũ wa Beori, ndũmĩrĩri ya mũndũ ũrĩa riitho rĩake rĩonaga wega,
english: He took up his parable, and said, “Balaam the son of Beor says, the man whose eyes are open says;

kikuyu: Wahehenjire Rahabu agĩtuĩka o ta ũmwe wa arĩa moragĩtwo; wahurunjire thũ ciaku na guoko gwaku kũrĩ hinya.
english: You have broken Rahab in pieces, like one of the slain. You have scattered your enemies with your mighty arm.

kikuyu: (Na rĩrĩ, Musa aarĩ mũndũ mwĩnyiihia mũno, mwĩnyiihia gũkĩra andũ arĩa angĩ othe gũkũ thĩ).
english: Now the man Moses was very humble, more than all the men who were on the surface of the earth.

kikuyu: Mũtingĩhota kũnyuĩra gĩkombe kĩa Mwathani, na mũnyuĩre gĩkombe kĩa ndaimono o nakĩo; mũtingĩhota kũgwatanĩra metha-inĩ ya Mwathani, na mũgwatanĩre metha-inĩ ya ndaimono.
english: You can’t both drink the cup of the Lord and the cup of demons. You can’t both partake of the table of the Lord and of the table of demons.

kikuyu: no gũkorwo mũthamaki nĩegwĩtĩkĩra kũhonokia ndungata yake kuuma guoko-inĩ kwa mũndũ ũrĩa ũrageria gũtũniina niĩ hamwe na mũriũ wakwa, matweherie kuuma kũrĩ igai rĩrĩa twaheirwo nĩ Ngai.’
english: For the king will hear, to deliver his servant out of the hand of the man who would destroy me and my son together out of the inheritance of God.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo o ta ũrĩa mbura yuraga, narĩo ira rĩkaharũrũka kuuma igũrũ, na iticookaga kuo itaihũgĩtie thĩ na igatũma mĩmera ĩthundũke na ĩciare, nĩguo mũhandi aheo mbeũ na ũrĩa wendaga kũrĩa aheo irio ciake,
english: For as the rain comes down and the snow from the sky, and doesn’t return there, but waters the earth, and makes it grow and bud, and gives seed to the sower and bread to the eater;

kikuyu: Maya nĩmo maarĩ marĩĩtwa ma ariũ a Lawi kũringana na maandĩko mao: Gerishoni, na Kohathu, na Merari. Lawi aatũũrire muoyo mĩaka 137.
english: These are the names of the sons of Levi according to their generations: Gershon, and Kohath, and Merari; and the years of the life of Levi were one hundred thirty-seven years.

kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, ehia maroniinwo kuuma thĩ, na andũ arĩa aaganu mathire biũ. Wee ngoro yakwa, gooca Jehova.
english: Let sinners be consumed out of the earth. Let the wicked be no more. Bless Yahweh, my soul. Praise Yah!

kikuyu: Mũndũ oimaga nda ya nyina arĩ njaga, na o ũguo okaga, noguo athiiaga. Ndarĩ kĩndũ o nakĩ kiumanĩte na wĩra wake agaakuua na moko make.
english: As he came out of his mother’s womb, naked shall he go again as he came, and shall take nothing for his labor, which he may carry away in his hand.

kikuyu: Ũrĩa ngũmwĩra nduma-inĩ-rĩ, wariei mũthenya barigici, na ũrĩa warĩtio na mĩheehũ matũ-inĩ manyu-rĩ, wanĩrĩrei mũrĩ nyũmba-igũrũ.
english: What I tell you in the darkness, speak in the light; and what you hear whispered in the ear, proclaim on the housetops.

kikuyu: Ũndũ ũcio moigire nĩwakenirie gĩkundi kĩu gĩothe. Nao magĩthuura Stefano, mũndũ waiyũrĩtwo nĩ wĩtĩkio na Roho Mũtheru, na Filipu, na Porokoro, na Nikanora, na Timoni, na Paramena, na Nikola, mũndũ wa kuuma Antiokia ũrĩa wagarũrũkĩte agatuĩka Mũyahudi.
english: These words pleased the whole multitude. They chose Stephen, a man full of faith and of the Holy Spirit, Philip, Prochorus, Nicanor, Timon, Parmenas, and Nicolaus, a proselyte of Antioch,

kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, mũndũ-wa-nja ũcio nĩarĩkĩtie kuoya andũ acio eerĩ na akamahitha. Nake akiuga atĩrĩ, “Ĩĩ nĩguo, andũ acio nĩmegũkĩte gwakwa, no ndinamenya kũrĩa mekuumĩte.
english: The woman took the two men and hid them. Then she said, “Yes, the men came to me, but I didn’t know where they came from.

kikuyu: Oyai ithandũkũ rĩa Jehova mũrĩigĩrĩre igũrũ rĩa ngaari, na mũige gathandũkũ mwena-inĩ warĩo na mũgekĩre indo icio cia tha- habu icio mũramĩcookeria irĩ cia iruta rĩa mahĩtia. Rekei rĩthiĩ,
english: and take Yahweh’s ark and lay it on the cart. Put the jewels of gold, which you return him for a trespass offering, in a box by its side; and send it away, that it may go.

kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo wendo waku ũkoragwo ũrĩ mbere yakwa, na ngeragĩra njĩra-inĩ yaku ya ũhoro wa ma.
english: For your loving kindness is before my eyes. I have walked in your truth.

kikuyu: Nowe akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Ndĩ na irio cia kũrĩa, iria mũtarĩ ũndũ wacio mũũĩ.”
english: But he said to them, “I have food to eat that you don’t know about.”

kikuyu: Mũkaarĩĩaga nyama cia ariũ na aarĩ anyu.
english: You will eat the flesh of your sons, and you will eat the flesh of your daughters.

kikuyu: Ningĩ mũraika ũcio akĩnjĩĩra atĩrĩ, “Andĩka atĩrĩ: ‘Kũrathimwo-rĩ, nĩ arĩa metĩtwo iruga-inĩ rĩa ũhiki wa Gatũrũme!’ ” Agĩcooka akiuga atĩrĩ, “Icio nĩ ciugo cia ma cia Ngai.”
english: He said to me, “Write, ‘Blessed are those who are invited to the wedding supper of the Lamb.’ ” He said to me, “These are true words of God.”

kikuyu: Nake agĩkĩrĩrĩria kũhooya, arĩ na ruo rũnene ngoro-inĩ, nayo thithino yake yahaanaga ta matata manene ma thakame magĩtaata thĩ.
english: Being in agony, he prayed more earnestly. His sweat became like great drops of blood falling down on the ground.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo mũndũ ũrĩa wĩgaathagĩrĩria tiwe wonagwo arĩ mwega, no nĩ ũrĩa Mwathani agathagĩrĩria.
english: For it isn’t he who commends himself who is approved, but whom the Lord commends.

kikuyu: Jesũ akĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Wĩtagwo atĩa?” Nake akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Legioni,” nĩgũkorwo aarĩ na ndaimono nyingĩ.
english: Jesus asked him, “What is your name?” He said, “Legion,” for many demons had entered into him.

kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Ngai eerĩire Iburahĩmu kĩĩranĩro gĩake, eehĩtire akĩĩgwetaga we mwene, nĩgũkorwo gũtiarĩ na ũngĩ mũnene kũrĩ we wa kwĩhĩta nake,
english: For when God made a promise to Abraham, since he could swear by no one greater, he swore by himself,

kikuyu: o rĩrĩ, Ngai nĩaririkanire Nuhu o hamwe na nyamũ ciothe cia gĩthaka o na mahiũ marĩa mothe aarĩ namo thĩinĩ wa thabina, na agĩtũma rũhuho rũhurutane gũkũ thĩ, namo maaĩ makĩambĩrĩria kũhũa.
english: od remembered Noah, all the animals, and all the livestock that were with him in the ship; and God made a wind to pass over the earth. The waters subsided.

kikuyu: “Mũirĩtu gathirange nĩakagĩa nda na nĩagaciara kahĩĩ, nao magaagatua Imanueli” ũguo naguo ũgĩtaũrwo nĩ kuuga, “Ngai hamwe na ithuĩ.”
english: “Behold, the virgin shall be with child, and shall give birth to a son. They shall call his name Immanuel,” which is, being interpreted, “God with us.”*

kikuyu: Andũ aingĩ nĩmagatherio, mathirwo nĩ gĩko merũhio, taarĩ gũkumuthwo makumuthĩtwo magathera, no arĩa aaganu nĩmagakĩrĩrĩria kwagana, na gũtirĩ o na ũmwe wao ũkaamenya gĩtũmi kĩa maũndũ macio, no arĩa oogĩ nĩmakamamenya.
english: Many will purify themselves, and make themselves white, and be refined, but the wicked will do wickedly; and none of the wicked will understand, but those who are wise will understand.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo mũkĩnjookeria atĩrĩ, “Nĩtũhĩtĩirie Jehova. Nĩtũkwambata tũkarũe, o ta ũrĩa Jehova Ngai witũ aatwathĩte.” Nĩ ũndũ ũcio inyuĩ inyuothe o mũndũ o mũndũ akĩĩoha indo ciake cia mbaara, mwĩciirĩtie atĩ nĩ ũndũ mũhũthũ kwambata mũthiĩ bũrũri ũcio ũrĩ irĩma.
english: Then you answered and said to me, “We have sinned against Yahweh. We will go up and fight, according to all that Yahweh our God commanded us.” Every man of you put on his weapons of war, and presumed to go up into the hill country.

kikuyu: Nake Nathani akĩĩra Daudi ciugo ciothe cia ũguũrio ũcio. Ihooya rĩa Daudi
english: Nathan spoke to David all these words, and according to all this vision.

kikuyu: Ti-itherũ, hĩndĩ ĩyo nĩũkona gĩkeno thĩinĩ wa ũcio Mwene-Hinya-Wothe, na nĩũgatiirĩra Ngai ũthiũ waku.
english: For then you will delight yourself in the Almighty, and will lift up your face to God.

kikuyu: Kanua gakwa kaaragia ũhoro wa ma, nĩgũkorwo mĩromo yakwa nĩĩthũire waganu mũno ta kĩndũ kĩrĩ magigi.
english: For my mouth speaks truth. Wickedness is an abomination to my lips.

kikuyu: Ningĩ Jehova akĩũria Shaitani atĩrĩ, “Nĩũcũũranĩtie ũhoro wa ndungata yakwa Ayubu? Ũkoona atĩ kũu thĩ gũtirĩ mũndũ ũhaana take; ndarĩ ũcuuke na nĩ mũrũngĩrĩru, mũndũ mwĩtigĩri-Ngai na ũtheemaga maũndũ marĩa mooru. Ningĩ no arũmĩtie wĩhokeku wake, o na gũtuĩka nĩwaningĩrĩirie ndĩmwanange hatarĩ gĩtũmi.”
english: Yahweh said to Satan, “Have you considered my servant Job? For there is no one like him in the earth, a blameless and an upright man, one who fears God, and turns away from evil. He still maintains his integrity, although you incited me against him, to ruin him without cause.”

kikuyu: a rĩrĩ, Jesũ aarĩkia gwatha arutwo acio ake ikũmi na eerĩ-rĩ, akiuma kũu agĩthiĩ kũrutana na kũhunjia matũũra-inĩ ma Galili.
english: hen Jesus had finished directing his twelve disciples, he departed from there to teach and preach in their cities.

kikuyu: Makĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Mwathi witũ, nĩtũkũririkana atĩ rĩrĩa mũheenania ũcio aarĩ muoyo. nĩoigire atĩrĩ, ‘Thuutha wa matukũ matatũ nĩngariũka.’
english: saying, “Sir, we remember what that deceiver said while he was still alive: ‘After three days I will rise again.’

kikuyu: Mũngĩgaatiganĩria Jehova na mũtungatĩre ngai cia kũngĩ, nĩakamũgarũrũka na amũrehere mwanangĩko amũniine, o na amwĩkĩte maũndũ mega.”
english: If you forsake Yahweh, and serve foreign gods, then he will turn and do you evil, and consume you, after he has done you good.”

kikuyu: Andũ manjũũragia atĩrĩ, “Kaĩ ndũmĩrĩri ya Jehova ĩkĩrĩ kũ? Nĩĩkĩhingio rĩu!”
english: Behold, they ask me, “Where is Yahweh’s word? Let it be fulfilled now.”

kikuyu: “ ‘Gũtirĩ hĩndĩ ĩngĩ andũ a Isiraeli makaagĩa na thũ mariganĩtie nacio, nĩguo ituĩke congʼe ũrĩ ruo, na mĩigua mĩũgĩ. Hĩndĩ ĩyo nĩmakamenya atĩ niĩ nĩ niĩ Mwathani Jehova.
english: “ ‘ “There will no longer be a pricking brier to the house of Israel, nor a hurting thorn of any that are around them that scorned them. Then they will know that I am the Lord Yahweh.”

kikuyu: Geria mũno wĩonanagie harĩ Ngai wĩ mwĩtĩkĩrĩku, na wĩ mũruti-wĩra ũtarĩ ũndũ ũngĩmũconora, na ũrutanage ũhoro ũrĩa wa ma ũrũngĩrĩirie.
english: Give diligence to present yourself approved by God, a workman who doesn’t need to be ashamed, properly handling the Word of Truth.

kikuyu: Ngoro yayo yũmĩte ta ihiga, ĩkooma ta ihiga rĩa gũthĩa rĩa na thĩ.
english: His heart is as firm as a stone, yes, firm as the lower millstone.

kikuyu: Nyonia kĩmenyithia kĩa wega waku, nĩguo thũ ciakwa ikĩone iconorithio, nĩgũkorwo Wee, Wee Jehova, nĩũndeithĩtie na ũkaahooreria.
english: Show me a sign of your goodness, that those who hate me may see it, and be shamed, because you, Yahweh, have helped me, and comforted me.

kikuyu: O na rĩrĩ, nĩthuurĩte Oholiabu mũrũ wa Ahisamaku, wa mũhĩrĩga wa Dani, nĩguo ateithagie Bezaleli wĩra. Ningĩ nĩheete mabundi mothe ũũgĩ wa gũthondeka indo ciothe iria ngwathĩte:
english: Behold, I myself have appointed with him Oholiab, the son of Ahisamach, of the tribe of Dan; and in the heart of all who are wise-hearted I have put wisdom, that they may make all that I have commanded you:

kikuyu: Rĩrĩa yathundũra mathangũ, mũmĩonaga na mũkamenya atĩ ihinda rĩa riũa rĩrĩ hakuhĩ.
english: When they are already budding, you see it and know by your own selves that the summer is already near.

kikuyu: Angĩkorwo mũndũ nĩahũũtiĩ-rĩ, agĩrĩirwo nĩ kũrĩa irio gwake mũciĩ, nĩgeetha rĩrĩa muongana gũtikagĩe ũhoro wa gũtuĩrwo ciira. Nĩngaheana ũhoro wa maũndũ macio mangĩ rĩrĩa ngooka.
english: But if anyone is hungry, let him eat at home, lest your coming together be for judgment. The rest I will set in order whenever I come.

kikuyu: iugo kĩa Jehova nĩkĩanginyĩrĩire, ngĩĩrwo atĩrĩ,
english: ahweh’s word came to me, saying,

kikuyu: Wee Jehova, ĩka ndungata yaku kũringana na kiugo gĩaku.
english: You have treated your servant well, according to your word, Yahweh.

kikuyu: nake Zadoku mũrũ wa Ahitubu* na Ahimeleku mũrũ wa Abiatharu maarĩ athĩnjĩri-Ngai; nake Seraia aarĩ mwandĩki.
english: Zadok the son of Ahitub and Ahimelech the son of Abiathar were priests, Seraiah was scribe,

kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, mũthamaki ũngĩ ũtooĩ ũhoro wa Jusufu akĩambĩrĩria gũthamaka kũu bũrũri wa Misiri.
english: Now there arose a new king over Egypt, who didn’t know Joseph.

kikuyu: Lea aarĩ na maitho mataarĩ na hinya, no Rakeli oombĩtwo arĩ kĩĩrorerwa na aarĩ mũthaka.
english: Leah’s eyes were weak, but Rachel was beautiful in form and attractive.

kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Jehova aaiguire ũrĩa mwoigire, akĩrakara na akĩĩhĩta, akiuga atĩrĩ:
english: Yahweh heard the voice of your words and was angry, and swore, saying,

kikuyu: ‘Githĩ ti gwetha mareetha na makagayana indo cia ndaho: mũirĩtu ũmwe kana eerĩ harĩ o mũndũ, na nguo cia marangi maingĩ itahĩirwo Sisera, nguo cia marangi maingĩ iria ngʼemie, nguo iria ngʼemie mũno cia ngingo yakwa, icio ciothe nĩ cia gũtahwo?’
english: ‘Have they not found, have they not divided the plunder? A lady, two ladies to every man; to Sisera a plunder of dyed garments, a plunder of dyed garments embroidered, of dyed garments embroidered on both sides, on the necks of the plunder?’

kikuyu: Maatũũrĩte mamuumaga thuutha tondũ nĩamagegetie hĩndĩ ndaaya na ũragũri wake.
english: They listened to him because for a long time he had amazed them with his sorceries.

kikuyu: Rĩrĩa maatoonyire mũtitũ nĩmoonire ũũkĩ ũgĩtaata, no gũtirĩ mũndũ o na ũmwe waũcamire na kanua nĩ ũndũ nĩmetigagĩra mwĩhĩtwa ũcio.
english: When the people had come to the forest, behold, honey was dripping, but no one put his hand to his mouth, for the people feared the oath.

kikuyu: na tũtũũra tũnini tũrĩa twarigiicĩirie matũũra macio o nginya Baalathu. Macio nĩmo maarĩ ũtũũro wao. Nao nĩmaigaga maandĩko ma njiarwa.
english: and all their villages that were around the same cities, as far as Baal. These were their settlements, and they kept their genealogy.

kikuyu: Mahũthagĩra moko mao meerĩ gwĩka ũũru; mũtongoria etagia iheo na hinya, nake mũciirithania aamũkagĩra mahaki; arĩa marĩ hinya nĩo moigaga kĩrĩa mekwenda: othe makagĩa ndundu ya gwĩka ũũru.
english: Their hands are on that which is evil to do it diligently. The ruler and judge ask for a bribe. The powerful man dictates the evil desire of his soul. Thus they conspire together.

kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, ũmwe wa ndungata cia Nabali akĩĩra Abigaili mũtumia wa Nabali atĩrĩ, “Daudi nĩaratũmĩte ndungata ciake kuuma werũ-inĩ irehere mwathi witũ ngeithi, nake araciruma.
english: But one of the young men told Abigail, Nabal’s wife, saying, “Behold, David sent messengers out of the wilderness to greet our master; and he insulted them.

kikuyu: Andũ arĩa meyoneire ũndũ ũcio makĩĩra andũ acio angĩ ũrĩa mũndũ ũcio warĩ na ndaimono aahonetio.
english: Those who saw it told them how he who had been possessed by demons was healed.

kikuyu: Nao othe maarĩ ta andũ ikũmi na eerĩ.
english: They were about twelve men in all.

kikuyu: ingĩ Jehova akĩĩra Musa atĩrĩ,
english: ahweh spoke to Moses, saying,

kikuyu: Mathagu ma makerubi maya maarĩ na ũraihu wa mĩkono mĩrongo ĩĩrĩ. Maicũhĩtio marũgamĩte na magũrũ, na mangʼetheire nyũmba nene.
english: The wings of these cherubim spread themselves out twenty cubits. They stood on their feet, and their faces were toward the house.
