kikuyu: ĩkiuna thuuna ya gacũmbĩrĩ kau, na ĩkĩmĩtwara bũrũri wa onjoria, nayo ĩkĩmĩhaanda thĩinĩ wa itũũra inene rĩa onjoria.
english: He cropped off the topmost of its young twigs, and carried it to a land of traffic. He planted it in a city of merchants.

kikuyu: “Mũrũ wa mũndũ, ĩra andũ a nyũmba ya Isiraeli atĩrĩ, ‘Inyuĩ mũroiga atĩrĩ: “Mahĩtia na mehia maitũ nĩmatũritũhĩire, na ithuĩ tũkahwererekera nĩ ũndũ wamo. Twakĩhota atĩa gũtũũra muoyo?” ’
english: “You, son of man, tell the house of Israel: ‘You say this, “Our transgressions and our sins are on us, and we pine away in them. How then can we live?” ’

kikuyu: Hiũria-i rũhiũ rwa kũgetha, nĩgũkorwo magetha nĩ makinyu. Ũkai, rangĩrĩriai thabibũ, nĩgũkorwo kĩhihĩro kĩa ndibei nĩkĩiyũru, na mĩtũngi ĩkaiyũra, ĩgaitĩkĩrĩra thĩ: ũguo noguo waganu wao ũnenehete!”
english: Put in the sickle; for the harvest is ripe. Come, tread, for the wine press is full, the vats overflow, for their wickedness is great.”

kikuyu: Nĩnderutĩire na kĩyo kwĩruta, na gũtuĩria na ũũgĩ maũndũ mothe marĩa mekagwo gũkũ thĩ. Kaĩ Ngai nĩakuuithĩtie andũ mũrigo mũritũ-ĩ!
english: I applied my heart to seek and to search out by wisdom concerning all that is done under the sky. It is a heavy burden that God‡ has given to the sons of men to be afflicted with.

kikuyu: Nake Jehoshafatu nĩaakire marikabu nyingĩ cia wonjoria, cia gũthiĩ kũgĩĩra thahabu Ofiri, no itiigana gũthiĩ, tondũ nĩcioinĩkangĩire kũu Ezioni-Geberi.
english: Jehoshaphat made ships of Tarshish to go to Ophir for gold, but they didn’t go, for the ships wrecked at Ezion Geber.

kikuyu: Nake mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai nĩakoya thakame ĩmwe na kĩara gĩake, na amĩhake hĩa cia kĩgongona kĩa iruta rĩa njino, nayo thakame ĩyo ĩngĩ amĩite gĩtina-inĩ gĩa kĩgongona.
english: The priest shall take some of its blood with his finger, and put it on the horns of the altar of burnt offering; and the rest of its blood he shall pour out at the base of the altar.

kikuyu: Ngoro ciao nĩ ta riiko rĩa kũrugĩra mĩgate; mathiiaga harĩ we marĩ na waara. Thuti ciao ciakanaga kahora ũtukũ wothe; nakuo gwakĩa rũciinĩ igaakana ta mwaki wa rũrĩrĩmbĩ.
english: For they have prepared their heart like an oven, while they lie in wait. Their anger smolders all night. In the morning it burns as a flaming fire.

kikuyu: njogu ĩrĩa yumĩte nda yake na ciana iria agaciara. Nĩgũkorwo no ende gũcirĩa na hitho hĩndĩ ĩyo ya kũrigiicĩrio na ya thĩĩna ũrĩa thũ ciaku igaakũrehere kũu matũũra-inĩ manene manyu.
english: toward her young one who comes out from between her feet, and toward her children whom she bears; for she will eat them secretly for lack of all things in the siege and in the distress with which your enemy will distress you in your gates.

kikuyu: Ningĩ o makĩrĩĩaga-rĩ, Jesũ akĩoya mũgate, agĩcookia ngaatho, agĩcooka akĩwenyũranga, akĩhe arutwo ake, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Oyai; ũyũ nĩguo mwĩrĩ wakwa.”
english: As they were eating, Jesus took bread, and when he had blessed it, he broke it and gave to them, and said, “Take, eat. This is my body.”

kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Pilato aiguire ũguo-rĩ, akiumia Jesũ nja, na agĩikarĩra gĩtĩ kĩa mũtuanĩri ciira handũ hoĩkaine ta “Harĩa-haare-Mahiga” (na heetagwo na Kĩhibirania Gabatha).
english: When Pilate therefore heard these words, he brought Jesus out and sat down on the judgment seat at a place called “The Pavement”, but in Hebrew, “Gabbatha.”

kikuyu: esũ aarĩkia kuuga maũndũ macio, agĩtiira maitho akĩrora na igũrũ, akĩhooya, akiuga atĩrĩ: “Baba hĩndĩ nĩ nginyu. Goocithia Mũrũguo, nĩguo nake Mũrũguo akũgoocithie.
english: esus said these things, then lifting up his eyes to heaven, he said, “Father, the time has come. Glorify your Son, that your Son may also glorify you;

kikuyu: Jehova nĩwe Mũrungu, na nĩatũmĩte ũtheri wake ũtwarĩre. Ambĩrĩriai gũkũngũiya, mũrĩ na mathĩgĩ moko, mũthiĩ nginya hĩa-inĩ cia kĩgongona.
english: Yahweh is God, and he has given us light. Bind the sacrifice with cords, even to the horns of the altar.

kikuyu: Mũndũ mũũgĩ oonaga ũgwati ũgĩũka akehitha, no arĩa matarĩ ũũgĩ mathiiaga o na mbere, nao magatoonya thĩĩna-inĩ.
english: A prudent man sees danger and takes refuge; but the simple pass on, and suffer for it.

kikuyu: mwakĩmeterera o nginya hĩndĩ ĩrĩa makaagimara? No mũikare mũtahikĩte mũmetereire?‡ Aca, aarĩ akwa. Niĩ ndĩ na ruo kũmũkĩra, nĩgũkorwo guoko kwa Jehova nĩgũtambũrũkĩte gũkanjũkĩrĩra!”
english: would you then wait until they were grown? Would you then refrain from having husbands? No, my daughters, for it grieves me seriously for your sakes, for Yahweh’s hand has gone out against me.”

kikuyu: Githĩ Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe ndatuĩte atĩ wĩra wa andũ no ngũ cia gwakĩrĩria mwaki, na atĩ ndũrĩrĩ ciĩnogagia o tũhũ?
english: Behold, isn’t it from Yahweh of Armies that the peoples labor for the fire, and the nations weary themselves for vanity?

kikuyu: Momanagĩrĩria magĩthugunda mĩbango mĩũru, meranaga ũrĩa mekũhitha mĩtego yao; mooragia atĩrĩ, “Nũũ ũngĩmĩona?”
english: They encourage themselves in evil plans. They talk about laying snares secretly. They say, “Who will see them?”

kikuyu: Tondũ wendo waku nĩ mwega gũkĩra muoyo guo mwene-rĩ, mĩromo yakwa nĩĩrĩkũgoocaga.
english: Because your loving kindness is better than life, my lips shall praise you.

kikuyu: Ndarĩ mũndũ o na ũmwe eetĩkĩririe amanyariire; nĩ ũndũ wao akĩrũithia athamaki, akĩmeera atĩrĩ:
english: He allowed no one to do them wrong. Yes, he reproved kings for their sakes,

kikuyu: Tondũ Ngai nĩateithĩtie Alawii arĩa maakuuaga ithandũkũ rĩa kĩrĩkanĩro kĩa Jehova-rĩ, ndegwa mũgwanja na ndũrũme mũgwanja nĩciarutirwo igongona.
english: When God helped the Levites who bore the ark of Yahweh’s covenant, they sacrificed seven bulls and seven rams.

kikuyu: Nĩakagĩa na kĩrĩkanĩro kĩrũmu na andũ aingĩ handũ ha kiumia kĩmwe. Gatagatĩ-inĩ ga kiumia kĩu nĩagatũma magongona na maruta matige kũrutwo. Na handũ ha mo, mwena ũmwe wa hekarũ-rĩ, nĩakarũgamia kĩndũ kĩrĩ magigi na thaahu, o nginya ithirĩro rĩrĩa rĩtuĩtwo rĩgaakinya, nake atũũre aitagĩrĩrio mangʼũrĩ.’ ”
english: He will make a firm covenant with many for one week. In the middle of the week he will cause the sacrifice and the offering to cease. On the wing of abominations will come one who makes desolate; and even to the decreed full end, wrath will be poured out on the desolate.”

kikuyu: Wee Mwathani-rĩ, nĩwe wanjiirĩrĩire; nawe ũgĩgĩkũũra muoyo wakwa.
english: Lord, you have pleaded the causes of my soul. You have redeemed my life.

kikuyu: “Rĩrĩa andũ aya, kana mũnabii, o na kana mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai mangĩkũũria atĩrĩ, ‘Ndũmĩrĩri nditũ kuuma kũrĩ Jehova nĩ ĩrĩkũ?’ ũkameera atĩrĩ, ‘Ndũmĩrĩri nditũ ya Jehova nĩ inyuĩ, na nĩngamũtiganĩria, ũguo nĩguo Jehova ekuuga.’
english: “When this people, or the prophet, or a priest, asks you, saying, ‘What is the message from Yahweh?’ Then you shall tell them, ‘ “What message? I will cast you off,” says Yahweh.’

kikuyu: “Niĩ nĩ niĩ mũrĩithi ũrĩa mwega; nĩnjũũĩ ngʼondu ciakwa, nacio ngʼondu ciakwa nĩcinjũũĩ,
english: I am the good shepherd. I know my own, and I’m known by my own;

kikuyu: Arĩa othe mangũũragĩra maitho mangenerere nĩ ũndũ wa mĩnyamaro yakwa maroconorithio na manyiitwo nĩ kĩrigiicano; arĩa othe metũũgagĩria igũrũ rĩakwa maroconorithio na maagithio gĩtĩĩo.
english: Let them be disappointed and confounded together who rejoice at my calamity. Let them be clothed with shame and dishonor who magnify themselves against me.

kikuyu: Makĩrĩĩta Dani o ta ithe wao Dani ũrĩa waciarĩtwo nĩ Isiraeli o na gũtuĩka itũũra rĩu inene rĩetagwo Laishi mbere ĩyo.
english: They called the name of the city Dan, after the name of Dan their father, who was born to Israel; however the name of the city used to be Laish.

kikuyu: Nake mũtumia ũcio akĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Wee mwathi wakwa, nĩndakũũrĩtie ũhe kahĩĩ? Githĩ ndiakwĩrire, ‘Ndũkarahũre mwĩhoko wakwa’?”
english: Then she said, “Did I ask you for a son, my lord? Didn’t I say, ‘Do not deceive me’?”

kikuyu: Jehoahazu aarĩ wa mĩaka mĩrongo ĩĩrĩ na ĩtatũ rĩrĩa aatuĩkire mũthamaki, na agĩthamaka Jerusalemu mĩeri ĩtatũ. Nyina eetagwo Hamutali mwarĩ wa Jeremia, na oimĩte Libina.
english: Jehoahaz was twenty-three years old when he began to reign; and he reigned three months in Jerusalem. His mother’s name was Hamutal the daughter of Jeremiah of Libnah.

kikuyu: Ningĩ Ngai agĩathana, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Nĩkũgĩe imũrĩ wariĩ-inĩ wa matu mairũ cia kũgayũkania mũthenya na ũtukũ, na irekwo ituĩke imenyithania cia imera, na mĩthenya na mĩaka.
english: God said, “Let there be lights in the expanse of the sky to divide the day from the night; and let them be for signs to mark seasons, days, and years;

kikuyu: Kũrĩ hĩndĩ niĩ ndaarĩ muoyo itanamenya watho; no hĩndĩ ĩrĩa rĩathani rĩokire, wĩhia ũkĩgĩa muoyo na niĩ ngĩkua.
english: I was alive apart from the law once, but when the commandment came, sin revived and I died.

kikuyu: Mĩkuru yothe nĩĩgathikwo yambatĩre, nacio irĩma ciothe na tũrĩma ciaraganio; nakuo kũrĩa gũtarĩ kũigananu nĩgũkaigananio, nakuo kũrĩa gũtarĩ kwaraganu kwaraganio wega.
english: Every valley shall be exalted, and every mountain and hill shall be made low. The uneven shall be made level, and the rough places a plain.

kikuyu: ĩ ũndũ ũcio andũ acio atatũ nĩmatigire gũcookeria Ayubu, nĩ ũndũ eeyonaga arĩ mũthingu.
english: o these three men ceased to answer Job, because he was righteous in his own eyes.

kikuyu: Hezekia akĩhũgũra ũthiũ wake, akĩrora rũthingo-inĩ, na akĩhooya Jehova, akiuga atĩrĩ,
english: Then he turned his face to the wall, and prayed to Yahweh, saying,

kikuyu: Nĩmakĩte kũndũ gũtũũgĩru gwa Tofethi, kũu Gĩtuamba-inĩ kĩa Beni-Hinomu, nĩguo macinagĩre aanake na airĩtu ao na mwaki kuo, ũndũ niĩ itaathanire wĩkagwo, o na ũndũ ũtarĩ wagĩa meciiria-inĩ makwa.
english: They have built the high places of Topheth, which is in the valley of the son of Hinnom, to burn their sons and their daughters in the fire, which I didn’t command, nor did it come into my mind.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo gũtigacooka kũgĩa cioneki cia maheeni, kana ũragũri wa kũheenanĩrĩria gatagatĩ-inĩ ka andũ a Isiraeli.
english: For there will be no more any false vision nor flattering divination within the house of Israel.

kikuyu: ĩkĩ nĩkĩo kĩrathimo kĩrĩa Musa mũndũ wa Ngai aarathimire andũ a Isiraeli atanakua.
english: his is the blessing with which Moses the man of God blessed the children of Israel before his death.

kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, endagai thũ cianyu, na mũciĩkage wega, o na mũkombithagie mũtekwĩhoka gũcookerio kĩndũ. Hĩndĩ ĩyo irĩhi rĩanyu nĩrĩkaneneha, na inyuĩ nĩmũgatuĩka ariũ a Ũrĩa-ũrĩ-Igũrũ-Mũno, nĩgũkorwo we nĩaiguagĩra arĩa matarĩ ngaatho tha o na arĩa aaganu.
english: But love your enemies, and do good, and lend, expecting nothing back; and your reward will be great, and you will be children of the Most High; for he is kind toward the unthankful and evil.

kikuyu: aĩ itũũra rĩĩrĩ inene rĩa andũ aremi, na rĩgwatĩtio thaahu, o na rĩiyũrĩte maũndũ ma kũhinyanĩrĩria rĩrĩ na haaro-ĩ!
english: oe to her who is rebellious and polluted, the oppressing city!

kikuyu: Esiteri ndoimbũrĩte rũrĩrĩ rwake kana kĩhumo kĩa nyũmba yao, tondũ Moridekai nĩamũtaarĩte akamwĩra ndageke ũguo.
english: Esther had not made known her people nor her relatives, because Mordecai had instructed her that she should not make it known.

kikuyu: “No inyuĩ nĩmwaheire Anaziri ndibei manyue, na mũgĩatha anabii matikanarathe ũndũ.
english: “But you gave the Nazirites wine to drink, and commanded the prophets, saying, ‘Don’t prophesy!’

kikuyu: Arahũka, Wee Jehova, mangʼethere, ũmahoote; ndeithũra na rũhiũ rwaku rwa njora kuuma kũrĩ andũ arĩa aaganu.
english: Arise, Yahweh, confront him. Cast him down. Deliver my soul from the wicked by your sword,

kikuyu: kuuma mũhĩrĩga wa Asheri, ikĩheo Mishali, na Abidoni
english: Out of the tribe of Asher, Mishal with its pasture lands, Abdon with its pasture lands,

kikuyu: Acio nĩwe marĩathĩkagĩra na marutage wĩra wothe wa Hema ĩyo, no matikanakuhĩrĩrie indo cia handũ hau haamũre kana kĩgongona, kana wee hamwe nao mũkue.
english: They shall keep your commands and the duty of the whole Tent; only they shall not come near to the vessels of the sanctuary and to the altar, that they not die, neither they nor you.

kikuyu: na ningĩ wĩre andũ arĩa mohetwo atĩrĩ, ‘Thiĩi, nĩmuohorwo,’ nao arĩa maikarĩte nduma-inĩ ũmeere atĩrĩ, ‘Umĩrai ũtheri-inĩ!’ “Makaarĩithagio mĩkĩra-inĩ ya njĩra, na moonage gĩa kũrĩa tũrĩma-inĩ tuothe tũrĩa tũtarĩ kĩndũ.
english: saying to those who are bound, ‘Come out!’; to those who are in darkness, ‘Show yourselves!’ “They shall feed along the paths, and their pasture shall be on all treeless heights.

kikuyu: Jehova nĩonete ruo rũnene rwa andũ othe a Isiraeli, arĩa maarĩ ngombo na arĩa mataarĩ ngombo, akĩona ũrĩa maathĩĩnĩkĩte, na gũtiarĩ mũndũ o na ũmwe wa kũmateithia.
english: For Yahweh saw the affliction of Israel, that it was very bitter for all, slave and free; and there was no helper for Israel.

kikuyu: hinda rĩu Jesũ nĩatuĩkanĩirie mĩgũnda-inĩ ya ngano mũthenya wa Thabatũ, na arutwo ake maarĩ ahũtu, na makĩambĩrĩria gũtua ngiracia ngano, magĩcirigitha, makĩrĩa ngano.
english: t that time, Jesus went on the Sabbath day through the grain fields. His disciples were hungry and began to pluck heads of grain and to eat.

kikuyu: Kĩgera kĩrutagwo kuuma tĩĩri-inĩ, nakĩo gĩcango kĩrutagwo mahiga-inĩ gĩgatwekio.
english: Iron is taken out of the earth, and copper is smelted out of the ore.

kikuyu: Ũthuure mĩaka ĩtatũ ya ngʼaragu, kana mĩeri ĩtatũ ya kũniinwo nĩ thũ ciaku igũthingatĩte na hiũ ciao cia njora, kana mĩthenya ĩtatũ ya rũhiũ rwa njora rwa Jehova, naguo nĩ atĩ kũgĩe na mĩthenya ya mũthiro bũrũri-inĩ, rĩrĩa mũraika wa Jehova akaaniinana Isiraeli guothe.’ Na rĩrĩ, tua itua ũnjĩĩre ũrĩa ngũthiĩ gũcookeria ũrĩa ũndũmĩte.”
english: either three years of famine; or three months to be consumed before your foes, while the sword of your enemies overtakes you; or else three days of the sword of Yahweh, even pestilence in the land, and Yahweh’s angel destroying throughout all the borders of Israel. Now therefore consider what answer I shall return to him who sent me.’ ”

kikuyu: Ahehenjaga arĩa marĩ ũhoti atekũũria mũndũ na akarũgamia andũ angĩ ithenya rĩao.
english: He breaks mighty men in pieces in ways past finding out, and sets others in their place.

kikuyu: Mũtiindaga mũkĩhũũra inanda cianyu cia mũgeeto o ta Daudi, na mũgethondekera inanda ingĩ cia kũina nacio.
english: who strum on the strings of a harp, who invent for themselves instruments of music, like David;

kikuyu: Nĩ kwarĩ na ariũ angĩ atano, nao nĩ: Hashuba, na Oheli, na Berekia, na Hasadia, na Jushabu-Hesedi.
english: and Hashubah, Ohel, Berechiah, Hasadiah, and Jushab Hesed, five.

kikuyu: ĩrĩa waikara thĩ kũrĩĩanĩra na mwathi wa bũrũri-rĩ,
english: hen you sit to eat with a ruler, consider diligently what is before you;

kikuyu: “Ti-itherũ Jehova, athamaki a Ashuri nĩmanangĩte biũ ndũrĩrĩ na mabũrũri macio.
english: Truly, Yahweh, the kings of Assyria have laid waste the nations and their lands,

kikuyu: Nyamũ cia mĩthemba yothe, na nyoni, na nyamũ iria itambaga thĩ, na nyamũ cia iria-inĩ rĩrĩa inene ciothe nĩihooreragio na cianahoorerio nĩ mũndũ,
english: For every kind of animal, bird, creeping thing, and sea creature is tamed, and has been tamed by mankind;

kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, hĩndĩ o ĩyo kũu thunagogi yao, nĩ kwarĩ na mũndũ warĩ na ngoma thũku, nake akĩanĩrĩra, akiuga atĩrĩ,
english: Immediately there was in their synagogue a man with an unclean spirit, and he cried out,

kikuyu: “Arĩria andũ a Isiraeli, ũmeere atĩrĩ: ‘Rĩrĩa mũgatoonya bũrũri ũrĩa ndĩramũtwara,
english: “Speak to the children of Israel, and tell them, ‘When you come into the land where I bring you,

kikuyu: Na mũtikae gũteta, ta ũrĩa amwe ao meekire, nao makĩũragwo nĩ mũraika ũrĩa mũniinani.
english: Don’t grumble, as some of them also grumbled, and perished by the destroyer.

kikuyu: Thĩ na merirĩria mayo nĩithiraga, no mũndũ ũrĩa wĩkaga ũrĩa Ngai endaga atũũraga nginya tene. Kwĩmenyerera Mũmena-Kristũ
english: The world is passing away with its lusts, but he who does God’s will remains forever.

kikuyu: Mũriũ wa Uzi aarĩ: Izirahia. Ariũ a Izirahia maarĩ: Mikaeli, na Obadia, na Joeli, na Ishia. Othe atano maarĩ anene a andũ ao.
english: The son of Uzzi: Izrahiah. The sons of Izrahiah: Michael, Obadiah, Joel, and Isshiah, five; all of them chief men.

kikuyu: Nĩgeetha ngoro iringĩke, nao arĩa magwĩte maingĩhe, nĩnjigĩte rũhiũ rwa njora rwa kũũragana ihingo-inĩ ciao ciothe. Hĩ! Rũthondeketwo rũhenagie ta rũheni,
english: I have set the threatening sword against all their gates, that their heart may melt, and their stumblings be multiplied. Ah! It is made as lightning. It is pointed for slaughter.

kikuyu: wa mĩrongo ĩĩrĩ na ĩna wagũĩrĩire Romamuti-Ezeri, na ariũ ake, na andũ a nyũmba yao, maarĩ andũ 12.
english: for the twenty-fourth to Romamti-Ezer, his sons and his brothers, twelve.

kikuyu: nĩngaitĩrĩria nyũmba ya Hazaeli mwaki ũrĩa ũgaacina ciĩhitho iria nũmu cia Beni-Hadadi.
english: but I will send a fire into the house of Hazael, and it will devour the palaces of Ben Hadad.

kikuyu: “Thondeka gatanji ga thahabu therie, na ũgakurure ta ũrĩa mũhũũri ũkururagwo, ũkurure ciugo ici: MŨTHERU MWAMŨRĨRE JEHOVA.
english: “You shall make a plate of pure gold, and engrave on it, like the engravings of a signet, ‘HOLY TO YAHWEH.’  A span is the length from the tip of a man’s thumb to the tip of his little finger when his hand is stretched out (about half a cubit, or 9 inches, or 22.8 cm.)  or, lapis lazuli

kikuyu: no Jehova ndakamatiganĩria moko-inĩ ma andũ acio aaganu, kana areke matuĩrwo atĩ nĩ ehia rĩrĩa magaaciirithio.
english: Yahweh will not leave him in his hand, nor condemn him when he is judged.

kikuyu: Mũthenya wa kana, tũrĩ thĩinĩ wa nyũmba ya Ngai witũ, tũgĩthima betha na thahabu na indo iria nyamũre ikĩneanwo moko-inĩ ma Meremothu mũrũ wa Uria, ũrĩa mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai. Eleazaru mũrũ wa Finehasi aarĩ hamwe nake, o ũndũ ũmwe na Jozabadu mũrũ wa Jeshua, na Noadia mũrũ wa Binui, arĩa Alawii.
english: On the fourth day the silver and the gold and the vessels were weighed in the house of our God into the hand of Meremoth the son of Uriah the priest; and with him was Eleazar the son of Phinehas; and with them were Jozabad the son of Jeshua, and Noadiah the son of Binnui, the Levites.

kikuyu: O rĩngĩ andũ a Isiraeli makĩrĩrĩra Jehova, nake akĩmahe mũndũ wa kũmahonokia wetagwo Ehudu, warĩ wa kĩmotho; mũrũ wa Gera ũrĩa Mũbenjamini. Nao andũ a Isiraeli makĩmũtũma na irĩhi rĩa igooti * 3:13 Andũ a Amoni na a Moabi maarĩ njiaro cia Loti (Kĩam 19:38), nao Amaleki maarĩ njiaro cia Esaũ (Kĩam 36:12, 16). Andũ aya nĩo maahakanĩte na andũ a Isiraeli, na nĩo maarĩ thũ ciao iria njũru.
english: But when the children of Israel cried to Yahweh, Yahweh raised up a savior for them: Ehud the son of Gera, the Benjamite, a left-handed man. The children of Israel sent tribute by him to Eglon the king of Moab.

kikuyu: Inyuĩ mũtũũraga Lakishi,† ngaari cianyu cia ita ciohererei mbarathi. Nĩ inyuĩ mwatoonyirie Mwarĩ wa Zayuni mehia-inĩ, nĩgũkorwo ũremi wa Isiraeli woimanire na inyuĩ.
english: Harness the chariot to the swift steed, inhabitant of Lachish. She was the beginning of sin to the daughter of Zion; for the transgressions of Israel were found in you.

kikuyu: “Nĩtwaiguire akiuga atĩrĩ, ‘Nĩngatharia hekarũ ĩno yakĩtwo nĩ andũ, na njake ĩngĩ ĩtakĩtwo nĩ mũndũ na ihinda rĩa mĩthenya ĩtatũ.’ ”
english: “We heard him say, ‘I will destroy this temple that is made with hands, and in three days I will build another made without hands.’ ”

kikuyu: Nao andũ magĩthiĩ kwĩyandĩkithia o mũndũ itũũra-inĩ rĩake mwene.
english: All went to enroll themselves, everyone to his own city.

kikuyu: na agĩikara kũu werũ-inĩ matukũ mĩrongo ĩna, akĩgeragio nĩ Shaitani. Nake aikaranagia na nyamũ cia gĩthaka, nao araika makamũtungatagĩra. Jesũ gwĩta Arutwo ake a Mbere
english: He was there in the wilderness forty days, tempted by Satan. He was with the wild animals; and the angels were serving him.

kikuyu: Na nĩmũkarangĩrĩria arĩa aaganu matuĩke mũhu rungu rwa makinya manyu mũthenya ũrĩa ngeeka maũndũ macio,” ũguo nĩguo Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe ekuuga.
english: You shall tread down the wicked; for they will be ashes under the soles of your feet in the day that I make,” says Yahweh of Armies.

kikuyu: Gĩkeno kĩrĩa kiumanaga na kũhũũrwo gwa tũhembe nĩgĩtigĩte gũkenwo, narĩo inegene rĩa andũ arĩa arĩĩu nĩrĩthirĩte, ningĩ gĩkeno kĩrĩa gĩkenagwo gũkĩhũũrwo kĩnanda kĩa mũgeeto nĩgĩkirĩtio.
english: The mirth of tambourines ceases. The sound of those who rejoice ends. The joy of the harp ceases.

kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, itũũra-inĩ rĩu nĩ kwarĩ na mũndũ wetagwo Simoni, nake nĩaragũrĩte kwa ihinda na akagegia andũ othe a Samaria. Nake nĩetĩĩaga na ageetua atĩ aarĩ mũndũ mũnene mũno,
english: But there was a certain man, Simon by name, who used to practice sorcery in the city and amazed the people of Samaria, making himself out to be some great one,

kikuyu: ĩ ũndũ ũcio ngĩĩcũũrania ũhoro wa maũndũ macio mothe na ngĩona atĩ andũ arĩa athingu na arĩa oogĩ, o hamwe na mawĩra mao, marĩ moko-inĩ ma Ngai, no rĩrĩ, gũtirĩ mũndũ ũngĩmenya kana nĩ wendo, kana nĩ rũmena rũmwetereire.
english: or all this I laid to my heart, even to explore all this: that the righteous, and the wise, and their works, are in the hand of God; whether it is love or hatred, man doesn’t know it; all is before them.

kikuyu: Acio eerĩ, mũndũ ũcio na mũtumia wake, maarĩ njaga, na matiaconokaga.
english: The man and his wife were both naked, and they were not ashamed.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo Ngai ũyũ nĩwe Ngai witũ nginya tene na tene; egũtũũra atũtongoragia nginya hĩndĩ ĩtagathira.
english: For this God is our God forever and ever. He will be our guide even to death.

kikuyu: “Ũthamaki wa igũrũ ũhaanaine na mũthamaki warugire iruga nĩ ũndũ wa ũhiki wa mũriũ.
english: “The Kingdom of Heaven is like a certain king, who made a wedding feast for his son,

kikuyu: Nawe, ũrĩ ta mwathi wa ariũ a thoguo-rĩ, nĩndakũhe rũgongo rwa bũrũri ũrĩa ndaatunyire Aamori na rũhiũ rwakwa rwa njora na ũta wakwa.”
english: Moreover I have given to you one portion above your brothers, which I took out of the hand of the Amorite with my sword and with my bow.”

kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Daudi aacookire gĩikaro kĩa nyũmba yake ya ũthamaki kũu Jerusalemu-rĩ, akĩoya thuriya iria ikũmi aatigĩte imenyagĩrĩre nyũmba ya ũthamaki, makĩigwo nyũmba marangagĩrwo. Nĩamaheaga irio, no ndaakomire nao. Maatũũraga marĩ ahingĩre na matariĩ ta atumia a ndigwa nginya mũthenya wa gũkua.
english: David came to his house at Jerusalem; and the king took the ten women his concubines, whom he had left to keep the house, and put them in custody and provided them with sustenance, but didn’t go in to them. So they were shut up to the day of their death, living in widowhood.

kikuyu: Mũhaka ũcio ũgĩcooka ũkĩrigiicũka werekeire Rama, ũgĩthiĩ nginya itũũra inene rĩrĩa rĩirige na hinya rĩa Turo, ũgĩcooka ũkĩrigiicũka ũgĩkinya Hosa, na ũkiumĩrĩra iria rĩrĩa rĩrĩ gũkuhĩ na Akizibu,
english: The border turned to Ramah, to the fortified city of Tyre; and the border turned to Hosah. It ended at the sea by the region of Achzib;

kikuyu: Ta thikĩrĩria, Wee Jehova; igua ũrĩa aya aathitangi maroiga!
english: Give heed to me, Yahweh, and listen to the voice of those who contend with me.

kikuyu: Ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ inyuĩ nyenda mũno-rĩ, mũtikanaheenio.
english: Don’t be deceived, my beloved brothers.

kikuyu: No rĩu-rĩ, nĩũkoretwo nĩ thĩĩna, nawe ũkoorwo nĩ hinya; ũhũũrĩtwo, nawe ũkanyiitwo nĩ kĩmako.
english: But now it has come to you, and you It touches you, and you are troubled.

kikuyu: na thaani ĩmwe nene ya thahabu ya ũritũ wa cekeri ikũmi, ĩiyũrĩtio ũbumba;
english: one golden ladle of ten shekels, full of incense;

kikuyu: Gĩkuũ na Mwanangĩko itiiganagia, na o namo maitho ma mũndũ matiiganagia.
english: Sheol† and Abaddon are never satisfied; and a man’s eyes are never satisfied.

kikuyu: “Ithuĩ-rĩ, nĩtwĩhĩtie na tũgakũremera,
english: “We have transgressed and have rebelled. You have not pardoned.

kikuyu: No mũndũ-rĩ, o na arĩ na indo nyingĩ, ndatũũraga; akuuaga o ta ũrĩa nyamũ ĩkuuaga ĩgathira.
english: But man, despite his riches, doesn’t endure. He is like the animals that perish.

kikuyu: Na kuuma mũhĩrĩga wa Nafitali makĩheo Kedeshi kũu Galili, na Hamoni, na Kiriathaimu, hamwe na ũrĩithio wamo.
english: and out of the tribe of Naphtali, Kedesh in Galilee with its pasture lands, Hammon with its pasture lands, and Kiriathaim with its pasture lands.

kikuyu: ĩ mũndũ ũrĩa Baba aamũrire arĩ wake kũna na akĩmũtũma gũkũ thĩ, ũhoro wake nĩ atĩa? Mũragĩĩthitanga nĩkĩ atĩ nĩndaruma Ngai tondũ nĩndoiga, ‘Ndĩ Mũrũ wa Ngai’?
english: do you say of him whom the Father sanctified and sent into the world, ‘You blaspheme,’ because I said, ‘I am the Son of God’?

kikuyu: Nake akĩmooria atĩrĩ, “Inyuĩ na inyuĩ muugaga atĩa? Muugaga niĩ nĩ niĩ ũ?” Petero akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Wee nĩwe Kristũ.”
english: He said to them, “But who do you say that I am?” Peter answered, “You are the Christ.”

kikuyu: Ningĩ Alawii amwe a kuuma rũciaro rwa Kohathu na rwa Kora magĩũkĩra na igũrũ, magĩkumia Jehova Ngai wa Isiraeli maanĩrĩire na mũgambo mũnene mũno.*
english: The Levites, of the children of the Kohathites and of the children of the Korahites, stood up to praise Yahweh, the God of Israel, with an exceedingly loud voice.

kikuyu: Arahũka ũtũteithie; tũkũũre tondũ wa wendani waku ũtathiraga.
english: Rise up to help us. Redeem us for your loving kindness’ sake.

kikuyu: Mwarĩ ũcio wa bũrũri wa Misiri nĩagaconorithio, akaanenganĩrwo kũrĩ andũ a mwena wa gathigathini.”
english: The daughter of Egypt will be disappointed; she will be delivered into the hand of the people of the north.”

kikuyu: kuuma ariũ a Jusufu: kuuma Efiraimu, nĩ Elishama mũrũ wa Amihudu; kuuma Manase, nĩ Gamalieli mũrũ wa Pedazuru;
english: Of the children of Joseph: of Ephraim: Elishama the son of Ammihud; of Manasseh: Gamaliel the son of Pedahzur.

kikuyu: Mũthenya wa ĩtatũ Jusufu akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Tondũ ndĩ mwĩtigĩri Ngai-rĩ, ĩkai ũũ nĩguo mũtũũre muoyo:
english: Joseph said to them the third day, “Do this, and live, for I fear God.

kikuyu: Hau thĩinĩ haamũre, ũraihu waho warĩ mĩkono mĩrongo ĩĩrĩ,* na wariĩ wa mĩkono mĩrongo ĩĩrĩ, na mĩkono mĩrongo ĩĩrĩ kũraiha na igũrũ. Nake nĩagemirie mwena wa thĩinĩ na thahabu therie,† na ningĩ akĩgemia kĩgongona kĩu na mbaũ cia mĩtarakwa.
english: Within the inner sanctuary was twenty cubits in length, and twenty cubits in width, and twenty cubits in its height. He overlaid it with pure gold. He covered the altar with cedar.

kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, o na gũtuĩka Jehova nĩamũtũmĩire ndungata ciake ciothe cia anabii kaingĩ, kaingĩ, mũtiigana gũthikĩrĩria kana gũtega matũ manyu nĩguo mũmaigue.
english: Yahweh has sent to you all his servants the prophets, rising up early and sending them (but you have not listened or inclined your ear to hear),

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio mũthenya wa keerĩ magĩthiũrũrũka itũũra rĩu inene riita rĩmwe, magĩcooka kambĩ. Meekire ũguo mĩthenya ĩtandatũ.
english: The second day they marched around the city once, and returned into the camp. They did this six days.

kikuyu: nĩgeetha Jehova atũũrie kĩĩranĩro kĩrĩa aanjĩĩrĩire, akiuga ũũ: ‘Njiaro ciaku ingĩkaamenyerera mũtũũrĩre wacio, na ithiiage irĩ na wĩhokeku mbere yakwa na ngoro ciao ciothe, na meciiria macio mothe, gwaku gũtikaga mũndũ wa gũikarĩra gĩtĩ kĩa ũnene gĩa Isiraeli.’
english: Then Yahweh may establish his word which he spoke concerning me, saying, ‘If your children are careful of their way, to walk before me in truth with all their heart and with all their soul, there shall not fail you,’ he said, ‘a man on the throne of Israel.’

kikuyu: Na inyuĩ maithe-rĩ, mũtikarakaragie ciana cianyu, itikae gũkua ngoro.
english: Fathers, don’t provoke your children, so that they won’t be discouraged.

kikuyu: Rĩrĩa kĩgongona gĩaitĩrĩirio maguta, atongoria nĩmarehire maruta mao nĩguo kĩamũrwo, na makĩmaiga mbere ya kĩgongona kĩu.
english: The princes gave offerings for the dedication of the altar in the day that it was anointed. The princes gave their offerings before the altar.

kikuyu: Akĩĩra andũ a nyũmba yake atĩrĩ, “Cookanĩrĩriai mahiga.” Nĩ ũndũ ũcio makĩũngania mahiga makĩmaiganĩrĩra hĩba, magĩcooka makĩrĩanĩra irio hau hakuhĩ na hĩba ĩyo.
english: Jacob said to his relatives, “Gather stones.” They took stones, and made a heap. They ate there by the heap.

kikuyu: Jeria aarĩ na andũ a nyũmba yao 2,700 arĩa maarĩ ehoti na maarĩ atongoria a nyũmba, nake Mũthamaki Daudi akĩmatua arũgamĩrĩri a andũ a Rubeni, na a andũ a Gadi, na nuthu ya mũhĩrĩga wa Manase maũndũ-inĩ mothe marĩa maakonainie na Ngai, o na marĩa maakonainie na ũhoro wa mũthamaki.
english: His relatives, men of valor, were two thousand seven hundred, heads of fathers’ households, whom King David made overseers over the Reubenites, the Gadites, and the half-tribe of the Manassites, for every matter pertaining to God and for the affairs of the king.

kikuyu: Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe nĩakamarathima akiugaga atĩrĩ, “Kũrathimwo-rĩ, nĩ andũ akwa a Misiri, o na Aashuri arĩa ndeyũmbĩire, na Isiraeli igai rĩakwa.”
english: because Yahweh of Armies has blessed them, saying, “Blessed be Egypt my people, Assyria the work of my hands, and Israel my inheritance.”

kikuyu: Rĩu nĩkũbataranĩtie atĩ andũ arĩa mehokeirwo maũndũ no nginya monanie atĩ nĩ ehokeku.
english: Here, moreover, it is required of stewards that they be found faithful.

kikuyu: “Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, Jehova ekuuga ũũ: ‘Nĩngacooka itũũra rĩa Jerusalemu ndĩrĩiguĩre tha, nayo nyũmba yakwa yakwo kuo rĩngĩ. Naruo rũrigi rũa gũthima nĩrũgatambũrũkio Jerusalemu,’ ũguo nĩguo Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe ekuuga.
english: Therefore Yahweh says: “I have returned to Jerusalem with mercy. My house shall be built in it,” says Yahweh of Armies, “and a line shall be stretched out over Jerusalem.” ’

kikuyu: Nĩũkamũhooya, nake nĩagakũigua, nawe nĩũkahingia mĩĩhĩtwa yaku.
english: You will make your prayer to him, and he will hear you. You will pay your vows.

kikuyu: ndũkahiũhe kũũtwara igooti-inĩ, tondũ-rĩ, ũgeeka atĩa marigĩrĩrio-inĩ,
english: Don’t be hasty in bringing charges to court. What will you do in the end when your neighbor shames you?

kikuyu: Naguo riiri wa Jehova ũgĩtoonyahekarũũgereirekĩhingokĩrĩakĩerekeiremwena wa irathĩro.
english: Yahweh’s glory came into the house by the way of the gate which faces toward the east.

kikuyu: Namo maũndũ marĩa mangĩ makoniĩ wathani wake na mĩthiĩre yake yothe-rĩ, kuuma kĩambĩrĩria nginya mũthia-rĩ, maandĩkĩtwo ibuku-inĩ rĩa athamaki a Juda na Isiraeli.
english: Now the rest of his acts, and all his ways, first and last, behold, they are written in the book of the kings of Judah and Israel.

kikuyu: Nake agĩaka kĩgongona na mahiga macio rĩĩtwa-inĩ rĩa Jehova, na akĩenja mũtaro mwariĩ ũngĩiganĩra ibaba igĩrĩ* cia mbegũ, ũkĩrigiicĩria kĩgongona kĩu.
english: With the stones he built an altar in Yahweh’s name. He made a trench around the altar large enough to contain two seahs† of seed.

kikuyu: Githĩ to mũhaka Kristũ ambe anyariirwo na acooke atoonye riiri-inĩ wake?”
english: Didn’t the Christ have to suffer these things and to enter into his glory?”

kikuyu: Ũcooke ũgatumĩrĩre mĩhari ĩna ya tũhiga twa goro. Mũhari wa mbere hakorwo kahiga ka wakiki ũrĩa mũtune, na ka yakuti ĩrĩa ĩhaana ngoikoni, na ka baregethu;
english: You shall set in it settings of stones, four rows of stones: a row of ruby, topaz, and beryl shall be the first row;

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ-rĩ, angĩkorwo igai rĩngĩoneka nĩ ũndũ wa watho-rĩ, rĩtikiumanĩte na kĩĩranĩro; no Ngai, nĩ ũndũ wa wega wake, nĩaheire Iburahĩmu igai na ũndũ wa kĩĩranĩro.
english: For if the inheritance is of the law, it is no more of promise; but God has granted it to Abraham by promise.

kikuyu: Elija akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “O ta ũrĩa Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe ũrĩa ndungatagĩra atũũraga muoyo- rĩ, ti-itherũ nĩngwĩonithania niĩ mwene kũrĩ Ahabu ũmũthĩ.” Elija Kĩrĩma-inĩ gĩa Karimeli
english: Elijah said, “As Yahweh of Armies lives, before whom I stand, I will surely show myself to him today.”

kikuyu: na rĩrĩa rĩothe maruta ma njino mangĩarutĩirwo Jehova, mĩthenya ya Thabatũ, na ciathĩ cia Karũgamo ka Mweri, na ciathĩ iria ingĩ ciathanĩtwo. No nginya matungatage mbere ya Jehova mahinda mothe marĩ mũigana ũrĩa ũtuĩtwo, ningĩ na njĩra ĩrĩa maathĩtwo marutage nayo.
english: and to offer all burnt offerings to Yahweh on the Sabbaths, on the new moons, and on the set feasts, in number according to the ordinance concerning them, continually before Yahweh;

kikuyu: Maitho make nĩmeyonere mwanangĩko wake mwene; nĩarekwo akunde mangʼũrĩ ma Mwene-Hinya-Wothe.
english: Let his own eyes see his destruction. Let him drink of the wrath of the Almighty.

kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, nĩ gĩtuĩke ũira gatagatĩ gaitũ na inyuĩ na njiarwa iria igooka, atĩ tũrĩhooyagĩra Jehova hema-inĩ yake tũrĩ na maruta maitũ ma njino, na magongona na maruta ma ũiguano. Ningĩ matukũ ma thuutha njiaro cianyu itikeera njiaro ciitũ atĩrĩ, ‘Mũtirĩ na igai harĩ Jehova.’
english: but it will be a witness between us and you, and between our generations after us, that we may perform the service of Yahweh before him with our burnt offerings, with our sacrifices, and with our peace offerings;’ that your children may not tell our children in time to come, ‘You have no portion in Yahweh.’

kikuyu: Mahe, Wee Jehova; no nĩ kĩĩ ũkũmahe? Mahe nda iria ihunaga, na ũmahe nyondo iria itarĩ iria.
english: Give them—Yahweh what will you give? Give them a miscarrying womb and dry breasts.

kikuyu: Njiaro ciaku ikaingĩha ta rũkũngũ rwa thĩ, na nĩũgatambũrũka na mwena wa ithũĩro, na mwena wa irathĩro, na wa gathigathini, o na wa gũthini. Andũ a mĩhĩrĩga yothe ya thĩ nĩmakarathimwo nĩ ũndũ waku, na nĩ ũndũ wa rũciaro rwaku.
english: Your offspring will be as the dust of the earth, and you will spread abroad to the west, and to the east, and to the north, and to the south. In you and in your offspring, all the families of the earth will be blessed.

kikuyu: “Na rĩrĩ, rũgamai muone ũndũ ũyũ mũnene Jehova akiriĩ gwĩka maitho-inĩ manyu!
english: “Now therefore stand still and see this great thing, which Yahweh will do before your eyes.

kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, Daudi aarĩ mũrũ wa Mũefiratha wetagwo Jesii, woimĩte Bethilehemu ya Juda. Jesii aarĩ na ariũ anana, na matukũ-inĩ ma Saũlũ aarĩ mũndũ mũkũrũ, agatindĩkanga mĩaka.
english: Now David was the son of that Ephrathite of Bethlehem Judah, whose name was Jesse; and he had eight sons. The man was an elderly old man in the days of Saul.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo kũgiana gwitũ ti gwa kũgiana na arĩa marĩ na mwĩrĩ na thakame, no tũgianaga na aathani, na mothamaki, na maahinya ma gũkũ thĩ ĩno ĩrĩ nduma, o na mbũtũ cia maroho mooru marĩ kũrĩa igũrũ.
english: For our wrestling is not against flesh and blood, but against the principalities, against the powers, against the world’s rulers of the darkness of this age, and against the spiritual forces of wickedness in the heavenly places.

kikuyu: Nao makĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Atangĩrĩ muuni watho-rĩ, tũtingĩmũrehe kũrĩ we.”
english: They answered him, “If this man weren’t an evildoer, we wouldn’t have delivered him up to you.”

kikuyu: Aarĩkia kuuga ũguo, agĩturia ndu hamwe nao othe makĩhooya.
english: When he had spoken these things, he knelt down and prayed with them all.

kikuyu: Nake mũthamaki wa Babuloni akĩmooragithĩria kũu Ribila, bũrũri wa Hamathu. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio andũ a Juda magĩtwarwo ithaamĩrio, kũraya na bũrũri wao.
english: The king of Babylon attacked them and put them to death at Riblah in the land of Hamath. So Judah was carried away captive out of his land.

kikuyu: Ngai akĩmũteithia kũhũũrana na Afilisti na Arabu arĩa maatũũraga Guri-Baali na Ameuni.
english: God helped him against the Philistines, and against the Arabians who lived in Gur Baal, and the Meunim.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio ngĩĩhĩta ndakarĩte, ngiuga atĩrĩ, “Matirĩ hĩndĩ magaatoonya kĩhurũko-inĩ gĩakwa.”
english: Therefore I swore in my wrath, “They won’t enter into my rest.”

kikuyu: No Ayahudi makĩigua ũiru; nĩ ũndũ ũcio magĩcaria andũ arĩa maarĩ mĩtugo mĩũru kuuma ndũnyũ, makĩmacookanĩrĩria gĩkundi, na makĩambĩrĩria kũruta ngũĩ kũu itũũra-inĩ. Nao makĩhanyũka kwa Jasoni gwetha Paũlũ na Sila nĩgeetha mamoimie nja kũrĩ kĩrĩndĩ.
english: But the unpersuaded Jews took along† some wicked men from the marketplace and gathering a crowd, set the city in an uproar. Assaulting the house of Jason, they sought to bring them out to the people.

kikuyu: Nake Gideoni akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “No rĩrĩ, mwathi wakwa, angĩkorwo Jehova arĩ hamwe na ithuĩ-rĩ, nĩ kĩĩ gĩtũmĩte maũndũ maya mothe matũkore? Makĩrĩ ha morirũ make mothe marĩa maithe maitũ maatwĩraga ũhoro wamo atĩrĩ, ‘Githĩ ti Jehova watwambatirie agĩtũruta bũrũri wa Misiri?’ No rĩu-rĩ, Jehova nĩatũtiganĩirie agatũneana guoko-inĩ kwa Midiani.”
english: Gideon said to him, “Oh, my lord, if Yahweh is with us, why then has all this happened to us? Where are all his wondrous works which our fathers told us of, saying, ‘Didn’t Yahweh bring us up from Egypt?’ But now Yahweh has cast us off, and delivered us into the hand of Midian.”

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio andũ othe magĩthiĩ Giligali na magĩkindĩra atĩ Saũlũ nĩwe mũthamaki marĩ mbere ya Jehova. Nao marĩ kũu makĩruta maruta ma ũiguano marĩ mbere ya Jehova, nake Saũlũ na andũ othe a Isiraeli magĩkũngũĩra na njĩra nene.
english: All the people went to Gilgal; and there they made Saul king before Yahweh in Gilgal. There they offered sacrifices of peace offerings before Yahweh; and there Saul and all the men of Israel rejoiced greatly.

kikuyu: Nĩahurunjĩte iheo ciake akahe andũ arĩa athĩĩni, naguo ũthingu wake ũtũũraga nginya tene; rũhĩa rwake nĩrũkambarario na igũrũ, aheo gĩtĩĩo.
english: He has dispersed, he has given to the poor. His righteousness endures forever. His horn will be exalted with honor.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, o na gwatuĩka nĩndamwandĩkĩire marũa-rĩ, ndiamaandĩkire nĩ ũndũ wa mũndũ ũrĩa wehĩtie, kana nĩ ũndũ wa ũrĩa wahĩtĩirio, no nĩgeetha mũhote kũmenya inyuĩ ene mũrĩ mbere ya Ngai ũrĩa mwĩheanĩte nĩ ũndũ witũ.
english: So although I wrote to you, I wrote not for his cause that did the wrong, nor for his cause that suffered the wrong, but that your earnest care for us might be revealed in you in the sight of God.

kikuyu: No mũndehere mũrũ wa thoguo ũrĩa mũnini biũ na noguo ngaamenya atĩ mũtirĩ athigaani, mũrĩ andũ ehokeku. Hĩndĩ ĩyo nĩngamũcookeria mũrũ wa thoguo, na nĩmũgetĩkĩrio kuonjorithagia bũrũri-inĩ ũyũ.’ ”
english: Bring your youngest brother to me. Then I will know that you are not spies, but that you are honest men. So I will deliver your brother to you, and you shall trade in the land.’ ”

kikuyu: Acio mahunjagia marĩ na ngoro njega-rĩ, mekaga ũguo marĩ na wendani, makĩmenyaga atĩ njigĩĩtwo gũkũ nĩ ũndũ wa kũrũĩrĩra Ũhoro-ũrĩa-Mwega.
english: The former insincerely preach Christ from selfish ambition, thinking that they add affliction to my chains;

kikuyu: Esiteri agĩcookia atĩrĩ, “Mũthamaki angĩona kwagĩrĩire-rĩ, ĩtĩkĩria Ayahudi arĩa marĩ Shushani makaahingia itua rĩĩrĩ o na rũciũ, na ũreke ciimba cia ariũ ikũmi a Hamani igaacuurio mĩtĩ-igũrũ.”
english: Then Esther said, “If it pleases the king, let it be granted to the Jews who are in Susa to do tomorrow also according to today’s decree, and let Haman’s ten sons be hanged on the gallows.”

kikuyu: Macio nĩmo maathani marĩa Jehova aaheire Musa Kĩrĩma-inĩ gĩa Sinai makoniĩ andũ a Isiraeli.
english: These are the commandments which Yahweh commanded Moses for the children of Israel on Mount Sinai.

kikuyu: No hĩndĩ o ĩyo Jesũ akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Ũmĩrĩriai! Nĩ niĩ. Tigai gwĩtigĩra.”
english: But immediately Jesus spoke to them, saying, “Cheer up! It is I! ‡ Don’t be afraid.”

kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Niĩ nĩ niĩ kũriũka na nĩ niĩ muoyo. Nake ũrĩa wothe ũnjĩtĩkĩtie, o na aakua, nĩagatũũra muoyo;
english: Jesus said to her, “I am the resurrection and the life. He who believes in me will still live, even if he dies.

kikuyu: nĩgeetha Alawii (arĩa matarĩ agaĩre gĩthaka kana igai rĩao ene), na andũ a kũngĩ, na ciana cia ngoriai, na atumia a ndigwa arĩa matũũraga matũũra-inĩ manyu, magookaga makarĩa na makahũũna, nĩguo Jehova Ngai wanyu amũrathimage wĩra-inĩ wothe wa moko manyu.
english: The Levite, because he has no portion nor inheritance with you, as well as the foreigner living among you, the fatherless, and the widow who are within your gates shall come, and shall eat and be satisfied; that Yahweh your God may bless you in all the work of your hand which you do.

kikuyu: Makaari macio maakĩtwo ta ũũ: maarĩ na icunjĩ cia mĩena inyiitithanĩtio na buremu.
english: The work of the bases was like this: they had panels; and there were panels

kikuyu: Nake mũrũ wa nyina Abisalomu akĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Kaĩ muuma na Amunoni, mũrũ wa thoguo? Rĩu ta gĩkire, mwarĩ wa maitũ, tondũ we nĩ mũrũ wa thoguo. Tiga kũigua ũũru ngoro nĩ ũndũ wa ũndũ ũcio.” Nake Tamaru agĩtũũra mũciĩ kwa mũrũ wa nyina Abisalomu, arĩ mũndũ-wa-nja ũrĩ na kĩeha kĩnene.
english: Absalom her brother said to her, “Has Amnon your brother been with you? But now hold your peace, my sister. He is your brother. Don’t take this thing to heart.” So Tamar remained desolate in her brother Absalom’s house.

kikuyu: Nayo ngumo ya maũndũ macio igĩkinyĩra andũ a kanitha ũrĩa warĩ kũu Jerusalemu; nao magĩtũma Baranaba athiĩ Antiokia.
english: The report concerning them came to the ears of the assembly which was in  The word for “brothers” here and where context allows may also be correctly translated “brothers and sisters” or “siblings.”  A Hellenist is someone who keeps Greek customs and culture.

kikuyu: Ũguo noguo, rĩrĩa mũkoona maũndũ macio magĩĩkĩka mũkaamenya ihinda rĩrĩ hakuhĩ, rĩrĩ o mũromo-inĩ.
english: even so you also, when you see these things coming to pass, know that it is near, at the doors.

kikuyu: Na tondũ Maandĩko marĩa matheru nĩmooigĩte o mbere atĩ Ngai nĩagatua andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ athingu na * 2:11 Antiokia rĩarĩ itũũra inene rĩa Suriata, na rĩarĩ rĩa gatatũ thuutha wa Roma na Alekisanderia thĩinĩ wa mabũrũri marĩa maathagwo nĩ Aroma.
english: The Scripture, foreseeing that God would justify the Gentiles by faith, preached the Good News beforehand to Abraham, saying, “In you all the nations will be blessed.”*

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa Ngai onire ũrĩa meekire, na ũrĩa maagarũrũkĩte magatiga mĩthiĩre yao mĩũru-rĩ, akĩmaiguĩra tha akĩaga kũmaniina ta ũrĩa oigĩte nĩekũmaniina.
english: God saw their works, that they turned from their evil way. God relented of the disaster which he said he would do to them, and he didn’t do it.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo mũndũ ũcio wahonetio na njĩra ya kĩama aarĩ na ũkũrũ wa makĩria ma mĩaka mĩrongo ĩna. Mahooya ma Etĩkia
english: For the man on whom this miracle of healing was performed was more than forty years old.

kikuyu: nĩguo ĩhũũnagie kũu gũtiganĩrie gũgakira ihooru, na ĩtũme kũmere nyeki?
english: to satisfy the waste and desolate ground, to cause the tender grass to grow?

kikuyu: Roho aheanaga muoyo; mwĩrĩ ndũrĩ kĩene. Ciugo iria ndamwarĩria nĩ roho na nĩcio muoyo.
english: It is the spirit who gives life. The flesh profits nothing. The words that I speak to you are spirit, and are life.

kikuyu: Jehoiada mũrũ wa Benaia na Abiatharu nĩo maacookire ithenya rĩa Ahithofeli. Joabu nĩwe warĩ mũnene wa mbũtũ cia ita cia mũthamaki.
english: After Ahithophel was Jehoiada the son of Benaiah, and Abiathar. Joab was the captain of the king’s army.

kikuyu: Ũkĩrai, tũthiĩ! Ũrĩa ũkũngunyanĩra nĩarooka.” Jesũ Kũnyiitwo
english: Arise! Let’s get going. Behold, he who betrays me is at hand.”

kikuyu: “ ‘Tondũ ũcio rũciũ kĩroko mũkeyumĩria inyuĩ ene o mũhĩrĩga, o mũhĩrĩga. Na rĩrĩ, mũhĩrĩga ũrĩa Jehova agaathuura nĩũkeyumĩria o mbarĩ, o mbarĩ; nayo mbarĩ ĩrĩa Jehova agaathuura nĩĩkeyumĩria o nyũmba, o nyũmba; nayo nyũmba ĩrĩa Jehova agaathuura nĩĩkeyumĩria o mũndũ, o mũndũ.
english: In the morning therefore you shall be brought near by your tribes. It shall be that the tribe which Yahweh selects shall come near by families. The family which Yahweh selects shall come near by households. The household which Yahweh selects shall come near man by man.

kikuyu: Jakubu agĩkoma na Rakeli o nake, na akĩenda Rakeli gũkĩra Lea. Na akĩrutĩra Labani wĩra mĩaka ĩngĩ mũgwanja. Ciana cia Jakubu
english: He went in also to Rachel, and he loved also Rachel more than Leah, and served with him seven more years.

kikuyu: Ndaamĩheire werũ ũtuĩke mũciĩ wayo, naguo bũrũri wa cumbĩ ũtuĩke gĩtũũro kĩayo.
english: whose home I have made the wilderness, and the salt land his dwelling place?

kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ, nĩtũkũmwatha thĩinĩ wa rĩĩtwa rĩa Mwathani Jesũ Kristũ, atĩ mũtigane na mũrũ kana mwarĩ wa Ithe witũ o wothe kĩgũũta, ũrĩa ũtaikaraga kũringana na ũrutani ũrĩa twamũrutire.
english: Now we command you, brothers, in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, that you withdraw yourselves from every brother who walks in rebellion and not after the tradition which they received from us.

kikuyu: ĩrĩa andũ othe a rũrĩrĩ rũu maarĩkirie kũringa Rũũĩ rwa Jorodani, Jehova akĩĩra Joshua atĩrĩ,
english: hen all the nation had completely crossed over the Jordan, Yahweh spoke to Joshua, saying,

kikuyu: Rĩrĩa aakinyire na akĩona ũira wa wega wa Ngai, agĩkena na akĩmomĩrĩria marĩ othe, atĩ maikarage maathĩkĩire Mwathani na ngoro ciao ciothe.
english: who, when he had come, and had seen the grace of God, was glad. He exhorted them all, that with purpose of heart they should remain near to the Lord.

kikuyu: Aca gũtitariĩ ta ũguo, ũhoro ũyũ nĩguo warĩtio nĩ mũnabii Joeli, akiuga atĩrĩ:
english: But this is what has been spoken through the prophet Joel:

kikuyu: Rĩrĩa mũgaakorwo mũkuhĩrĩirie gũthiĩ mbaara, mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai nĩagakuhĩrĩria mbũtũ cia ita na aciarĩrie.
english: It shall be, when you draw near to the battle, that the priest shall approach and speak to the people,

kikuyu: Inyuothe mũrĩ ciana cia Ngai nĩ ũndũ wa gwĩtĩkia Kristũ Jesũ,
english: For you are all children of God, through faith in Christ Jesus.

kikuyu: Daudi agĩikara bũrũri wa Afilisti mwaka ũmwe na mĩeri ĩna.
english: The number of the days that David lived in the country of the Philistines was a full year and four months.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, kĩmenye atĩ ti ũthingu waku ũtũmĩte Jehova Ngai waku akũhe bũrũri ũcio mwega ũwĩgwatĩre ũtuĩke waku, nĩgũkorwo inyuĩ mũrĩ andũ momĩtie ngingo. Njaũ ya Thahabu
english: Know therefore that Yahweh your God doesn’t give you this good land to possess for your righteousness, for you are a stiff-necked people.

kikuyu: No Afarisai na arutani a watho makĩregana na ũrĩa Ngai endaga meke, tondũ o matiabatithĩtio nĩ Johana.)
english: But the Pharisees and the lawyers rejected the counsel of God, not being baptized by him themselves.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ nĩeciiragia ũhoro wa mahĩtia make mothe marĩa aneeka, na akagarũrũka agatigana namo-rĩ, ti-itherũ nĩagatũũra muoyo; we ndagakua.
english: Because he considers, and turns away from all his transgressions that he has committed, he shall surely live. He shall not die.

kikuyu: nĩngũmũtegeire matũ biũ. No gũtirĩ o na ũmwe wanyu wonanirie mahĩtia ma Ayubu; gũtirĩ o na ũmwe wanyu ũmũcookeirie mĩario yake.
english: Yes, I gave you my full attention, but there was no one who convinced Job, or who answered his words, among you.

kikuyu: Nĩnyamũkĩrĩte indo nyingĩ mũno makĩria; nĩndeheirwo indo cia kũnjigana, nĩgũkorwo rĩu nĩnyamũkĩrĩte iheo iria mwanengerire Epafarodito andehere. Indo icio nĩ ta maruta marĩ na mũtararĩko mwega, marĩ igongona rĩĩtĩkĩrĩku rĩa gũkenia Ngai.*
english: But I have all things and abound. I am filled, having received from Epaphroditus the things that came from you, a sweet-smelling fragrance, an acceptable and well-pleasing sacrifice to God.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ wa ũguo magatuĩka a gũtuĩrwo ciira, tondũ nĩmathũkĩtie kĩĩranĩro kĩao kĩa mbere.
english: having condemnation, because they have rejected their first pledge.

kikuyu: “Riiri wa Lebanoni ũgaatuĩka waku, naguo nĩ wa mĩtĩ ya mĩthengera, mĩhuhu, na mĩkarakaba yothe hamwe, nĩguo ĩgemie handũ hakwa harĩa haamũre; na niĩ nĩngagoocithia handũ harĩa hakinyagwo nĩ magũrũ makwa.
english: “The glory of Lebanon shall come to you, the cypress tree, the pine, and the box tree together, to beautify the place of my sanctuary; and I will make the place of my feet glorious.

kikuyu: Ti atĩ nĩnjĩtĩkagĩra ũira wa mũndũ; no nĩndĩũgwetaga nĩgeetha mũhonokio.
english: But the testimony which I receive is not from man. However, I say these things that you may be saved.

kikuyu: Kĩrĩndĩ kĩnene kĩa andũ gĩkĩũngana, nakĩo gĩkĩhinga ithima ciothe na tũrũũĩ tũrĩa twathereragĩra bũrũri- inĩ. Makĩũria atĩrĩ, “Athamaki a Ashuri-rĩ, megũka makore maaĩ maingĩ ũũ nĩkĩ?”
english: Then many people gathered together and they stopped all the springs and the brook that flowed through the middle of the land, saying, “Why should the kings of Assyria come, and find abundant water?”

kikuyu: Ningĩ Jehova akĩĩra Musa atĩrĩ,
english: Yahweh spoke to Moses, saying,

kikuyu: Tondũ ũcio-rĩ, mũgaatuĩka a mbere gũtahwo; ndĩa cianyu na gũikara mwĩtambũrũkĩtie ithire. Jehova Nĩathũire Mwĩtĩĩo wa Isiraeli
english: Therefore they will now go captive with the first who go captive. The feasting and lounging will end.

kikuyu: Nake Abisalomu nĩaciarire aanake atatũ na mũirĩtu ũmwe. Nake mwarĩ eetagwo Tamaru, na aarĩ mũndũ-wa-nja mũthaka.
english: Three sons were born to Absalom, and one daughter, whose name was Tamar. She was a woman with a beautiful face.

kikuyu: Jehova ekuuga ũũ: “Nĩngacooka Zayuni, ndũũre Jerusalemu. Hĩndĩ ĩyo itũũra rĩa Jerusalemu rĩgeetagwo Itũũra Inene rĩa Ũhoro-wa-Ma, nakĩo kĩrĩma kĩa Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe gĩgeetagwo Kĩrĩma Gĩtheru.”
english: Yahweh says: “I have returned to Zion, and will dwell in the middle of Jerusalem. Jerusalem shall be called ‘The City of Truth;’ and the mountain of Yahweh of Armies, ‘The Holy Mountain.’ ”

kikuyu: Andũ acio a Dani magĩcookia atĩrĩ, “Tiga gũkararania na ithuĩ, kana ũtharĩkĩrwo nĩ andũ amwe arĩa me marakara, nawe hamwe na nyũmba yaku mũũrwo nĩ mĩoyo yanyu.”
english: The children of Dan said to him, “Don’t let your voice be heard among us, lest angry fellows fall on you, and you lose your life, with the lives of your household.”

kikuyu: a rĩrĩ, mwaka-inĩ wa mbere wa Kurusu mũthamaki wa Perisia, nĩguo kiugo kĩa Jehova kĩrĩa kĩarĩtio nĩ Jeremia kĩhiinge-rĩ, Jehova akĩarahũra ngoro ya Kurusu mũthamaki wa Perisia aanĩrĩrithie kũrĩa guothe aathamakaga, na andĩke atĩrĩ:
english: ow in the first year of Cyrus king of Persia, that Yahweh’s† word by Jeremiah’s mouth might be accomplished, Yahweh stirred up the spirit of Cyrus king of Persia, so that he made a proclamation throughout all his kingdom, and put it also in writing, saying,

kikuyu: Ndamũthaitha ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ, mũkirĩrĩrie ciugo ciakwa cia kũmũtaara nĩgũkorwo ndamwandĩkĩra o marũa makuhĩ.
english: But I exhort you, brothers, endure the word of exhortation, for I have written to you in few words.

kikuyu: ake Jusufu agĩatha mũnene wa nyũmba yake, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Iyũria makũnia ma andũ aya irio nyingĩ o ta ũrĩa mangĩhota gũkuua, na wĩkĩre betha ya mũndũ o mũndũ mũromo-inĩ wa ikũnia rĩake.
english: e commanded the steward of his house, saying, “Fill the men’s sacks with food, as much as they can carry, and put each man’s money in his sack’s mouth.

kikuyu: Na hĩndĩ ĩrĩa ndaarĩ na inyuĩ, ndabatario nĩ kĩndũ- rĩ, ndiatuĩkaga mũrigo harĩ mũndũ o na ũrĩkũ, nĩgũkorwo ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ moka kuuma Makedonia nĩmaheire indo cia kũigana mabataro makwa. Nĩndĩgirĩrĩirie ndigatuĩke mũrigo kũrĩ inyuĩ na njĩra o yothe, na nĩngũthiĩ na mbere gwĩka ũguo.
english: When I was present with you and was in need, I wasn’t a burden on anyone, for the brothers, when they came from Macedonia, supplied the measure of my need. In everything I kept myself from being burdensome to you, and I will continue to do so.

kikuyu: Mũthia-inĩ Pilato akĩmũneana kũrĩ o nĩguo akambwo mũtĩ-igũrũ. Jesũ Kwambwo Mũtĩ-Igũrũ Nacio thigari ikĩnyiita Jesũ.
english: So then he delivered him to them to be crucified. So they took Jesus and led him away.

kikuyu: Geithiai atongoria anyu othe na andũ othe, a Ngai. Andũ a kuuma Italia nĩmamũgeithia.
english: Greet all of your leaders and all the saints. The Italians greet you.

kikuyu: marĩa waheanire na tũnua twa anabii ndungata ciaku, rĩrĩa woigire atĩrĩ, ‘Bũrũri ũrĩa mũrathiĩ kwĩgwatĩra ũtuĩke wanyu nĩmũthaahie nĩ waganu wa andũ aguo. Nĩ ũndũ wa maũndũ mao me magigi, nĩmaũiyũrĩtie maũndũ mao mooru kuuma mwena ũmwe nginya ũrĩa ũngĩ.
english: which you have commanded by your servants the prophets, saying, ‘The land to which you go to possess is an unclean land through the uncleanness of the peoples of the lands, through their abominations, which have filled it from one end to another with their filthiness.

kikuyu: nake Eleasa nĩwe warĩ ithe wa Sisimai, nake Sisimai nĩwe warĩ ithe wa Shalumu,
english: and Eleasah became the father of Sismai, and Sismai became the father of Shallum,

kikuyu: Nake Ngai akĩarĩria Isiraeli na kĩoneki ũtukũ, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Jakubu! Jakubu!” Nake agĩcookia atĩrĩ, “Niĩ ũyũ haha.”
english: God spoke to Israel in the visions of the night, and said, “Jacob, Jacob!” He said, “Here I am.”

kikuyu: Rĩĩaga gĩcunjĩ kĩu ta kĩndũ kĩamũre mũno; mũndũ mũrũme o wothe wa nyũmba yaku nĩagakĩrĩĩaga. Wee no nginya ũtuage atĩ gĩcunjĩ kĩu nĩ kĩamũre.
english: You shall eat of it like the most holy things. Every male shall eat of it. It shall be holy to you.

kikuyu: Arahũka, ũkĩra ũngitĩre! Hũũranĩra, Wee Ngai wakwa na Mwathani wakwa.
english: Wake up! Rise up to defend me, my God! My Lord, contend for me!

kikuyu: Aahori tene maatũũraga Seiru, no njiaro cia Esaũ ikĩmarutũrũra, makiuma kuo. Njiaro cia Esaũ ikĩniina Aahori, ikĩmeeheria mbere yao, na magĩtũũra kuo ithenya rĩao, o ta ũrĩa andũ a Isiraeli meekire bũrũri-inĩ ũrĩa Jehova aamaheire megwatĩre ũtuĩke wao.)
english: The Horites also lived in Seir in the past, but the children of Esau succeeded them. They destroyed them from before them, and lived in their place, as Israel did to the land of his possession, which Yahweh gave to them.)

kikuyu: Umbũrai ũhoro wa ũrĩa gũgaikara, na mũũheane, na andũ marekwo maciire marĩ hamwe. Nũũ waheanire ũhoro ũyũ kuuma o tene? Ningĩ nũũ waumbũrĩte matukũ-inĩ macio ma tene mũno? Githĩ ti niĩ, niĩ Jehova? Gũtirĩ Ngai ũngĩ tiga niĩ, Mũrungu mũthingu na Mũhonokia; ti-itherũ gũtirĩ ũngĩ tiga niĩ.
english: Declare and present it. Yes, let them take counsel together. Who has shown this from ancient time? Who has declared it of old? Haven’t I, Yahweh? There is no other God besides me, a just God and a Savior. There is no one besides me.

kikuyu: Niĩ nĩ niĩ ndombire thĩ na andũ arĩa marĩ kuo o na nyamũ iria irĩ kuo na hinya wakwa mũnene na ndambũrũkĩtie guoko gwakwa, na ndĩmĩheaga o mũndũ ũrĩa ingĩenda kũhe.
english: ‘I have made the earth, the men, and the animals that are on the surface of the earth by my great power and by my outstretched arm. I give it to whom it seems right to me.

kikuyu: Wee Ngai wakwa, ndirikana, ũnjĩke wega nĩ ũndũ wa maũndũ marĩa mothe njĩkĩire andũ aya.
english: Remember me, my God, for all the good that I have done for this people.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo mũngĩtũũra kũringana na ũrĩa mwĩrĩ ũyũ wĩhagia wendaga-rĩ, nĩmũgakua, no mũngĩũraga ciĩko icio njũru cia mwĩrĩ na hinya wa Roho-rĩ, nĩmũgatũũra muoyo,
english: For if you live after the flesh, you must die; but if by the Spirit you put to death the deeds of the body, you will live.

kikuyu: a rĩrĩ, mĩaka ĩĩrĩ mĩgima yathira-rĩ, Firaũni akĩroota kĩroto: Akĩona arũgamĩte rũteere-inĩ rwa Rũũĩ rwa Nili;
english: t the end of two full years, Pharaoh dreamed, and behold, he stood by the river.

kikuyu: Mũtigetĩkĩre Hezekia amũringĩrĩrie kwĩhoka Jehova hĩndĩ ĩrĩa ekuuga atĩrĩ, ‘Ti-itherũ Jehova nĩegũtũhonokia; itũũra rĩĩrĩ inene rĩtikaaneanwo guoko-inĩ kwa mũthamaki wa Ashuri.’
english: Don’t let Hezekiah make you trust in Yahweh, saying, “Yahweh will surely deliver us. This city won’t be given into the hand of the king of Assyria.” ’

kikuyu: No mahiũ mothe na indo iria twatahire kuuma matũũra macio mao manene nĩtwacioire tũgĩthiĩ nacio.
english: But all the livestock, and the plunder of the cities, we took for plunder for ourselves.

kikuyu: Ithirĩro rĩa maũndũ mothe nĩrĩkuhĩrĩirie. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio ikaragai mũrĩ na ũũgĩ wa ngoro, na mũikarage mũterigĩtwo nĩguo mũhotage kũhooya.
english: But the end of all things is near. Therefore be of sound mind, self-controlled, and sober in prayer.

kikuyu: Mwĩmenyererei mũtigekage ciĩko cianyu cia ũthingu mbere ya andũ nĩguo mamuone. Mũngĩĩka ũguo-rĩ, mũtirĩ kĩheo mũkaaheo nĩ Ithe wanyu ũrĩa ũrĩ igũrũ.
english: Be careful that you don’t do your charitable giving† before men, to be seen by them, or else you have no reward from your Father who is in heaven.

kikuyu: No nginya marũmie ũhoro-ũrĩa-wa-ma wa wĩtĩkio ũrĩa mũrikĩru marĩ na thamiri itarĩ na mwĩcuuko.
english: holding the mystery of the faith in a pure conscience.

kikuyu: Akiuga atĩrĩ, “Wee Mwathani, angĩkorwo nĩnjĩtĩkĩrĩkĩte maitho-inĩ maku, ndagũthaitha Mwathani twarana na ithuĩ. O na gũtuĩka aya nĩ andũ momĩtie ngingo-rĩ, tuohere waganu witũ o na mehia maitũ, na ũtũme tũtuĩke igai rĩaku.”
english: He said, “If now I have found favor in your sight, Lord, please let the Lord go among us, even though this is a stiff-necked people; pardon our iniquity and our sin, and take us for your inheritance.”

kikuyu: O na ningĩ matiigana kwĩrira nĩ ũndũ wa mehia ma ũragani wao, kana ũrogi wao, kana ũmaraya wao, o na kana ũici wao.
english: They didn’t repent of their murders, their sorceries,‡ their sexual immorality, or their thefts.

kikuyu: na hau nĩho aakĩte kĩgongona ihinda rĩa mbere. Na Aburamu agĩkaĩra rĩĩtwa rĩa Jehova ho.
english: to the place of the altar, which he had made there at the first. There Abram called on Yahweh’s name.

kikuyu: “Niĩ-rĩ, nĩnangĩte kĩhihĩro kĩa ndibei ndĩ nyiki; gũtirĩ mũndũ wa kuuma kũrĩ ndũrĩrĩ o na ũmwe twarĩ nake. Ndamakinyĩrĩirie ndĩ na marakara, na ngĩmarangĩrĩria thĩ ndĩ na mangʼũrĩ; nayo thakame yao ĩkĩminjũkĩra nguo ciakwa, nacio ciothe ikĩgĩa na marooro mayo.
english: “I have trodden the wine press alone. Of the peoples, no one was with me. Yes, I trod them in my anger and trampled them in my wrath. Their lifeblood is sprinkled on my garments, and I have stained all my clothing.

kikuyu: Nĩndĩrĩkũrutaga na ngakuonereria njĩra ĩrĩa wagĩrĩirwo nĩ kũgera; ndĩrĩgũtaaraga na ngũmenyagĩrĩre.
english: I will instruct you and teach you in the way which you shall go. I will counsel you with my eye on you.

kikuyu: Nao arutwo makĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Angĩkorwo ũguo nĩguo kũrĩ gatagatĩ ka mũthuuri na mũtumia-rĩ, nĩ kaba kwaga kũhikania.”
english: His disciples said to him, “If this is the case of the man with his wife, it is not expedient to marry.”

kikuyu: Gũtirĩ mũndũ ũkiraga ageciiria, gũtirĩ mũndũ ũrĩ na ũũgĩ kana ũmenyo oige atĩrĩ, “Nuthu ya ngũ ndahũthĩrire na gwakia mwaki; o na ngĩrugĩra mĩgate makara-inĩ macio, na ngĩcooka ngĩhĩhia nyama na ngĩrĩa. Ti-itherũ no ngĩthondeke kĩndũ kĩrĩ magigi kuuma kũrĩ ngũ iria itigaire? Ti-itherũ no nyinamĩrĩre gĩcunjĩ kĩa mũtĩ?”
english: No one thinks, neither is there knowledge nor understanding to say, “I have burned part of it in the fire. Yes, I have also baked bread on its coals. I have roasted meat and eaten it. Shall I make the rest of it into an abomination? Shall I bow down to a tree trunk?”

kikuyu: Ngoro yakwa ĩiganĩire ta ĩrĩĩte irio iria njega mũno; nako kanua gakwa nĩkarĩkũgoocaga, mĩromo yakwa ĩgakũinĩra.
english: My soul shall be satisfied as with the richest food. My mouth shall praise you with joyful lips,

kikuyu: Mũthenya wa ĩnana wakinya, magĩũka nĩguo maruithie kaana kau, nao meendaga gũgatua Zakaria o ta ũrĩa ithe eetagwo,
english: On the eighth day, they came to circumcise the child; and they would have called him Zacharias, after the name of his father.

kikuyu: Ndũrĩrĩ iria mũgaatunya bũrũri-rĩ, nĩithikagĩrĩria arogi na andũ arĩa maragũraga. No inyuĩ, Jehova Ngai wanyu ndamwĩtĩkĩrĩtie gwĩka ũguo.
english: For these nations that you shall dispossess listen to those who practice sorcery and to diviners; but as for you, Yahweh your God has not allowed you so to do.

kikuyu: Mũthamaki Solomoni akĩĩrwo atĩ Joabu orĩire hema-inĩ ya Jehova na aarĩ hakuhĩ na kĩgongona. Hĩndĩ ĩyo Solomoni agĩatha Benaia mũrũ wa Jehoiada atĩrĩ, “Thiĩ, ũmũũrage!”
english: King Solomon was told, “Joab has fled to Yahweh’s Tent; and behold, he is by the altar.” Then Solomon sent Benaiah the son of Jehoiada, saying, “Go, fall on him.”

kikuyu: “Mũrũ wa mũndũ, erekeria ũthiũ waku harĩ Gogu wa kũu bũrũri wa Magogu, ũcio mũnene wa anene a Mesheki na Tubali; mũrathĩre ũhoro wa kũmũũkĩrĩra,
english: “Son of man, set your face toward Gog, of the land of Magog, the prince of Rosh, Meshech, and Tubal, and prophesy against him,

kikuyu: nao andũ a Moabi magĩĩtigĩra mũno nĩ ũndũ nĩ kwarĩ na andũ aingĩ mũno. Ti-itherũ, andũ a Moabi maiyũrĩtwo nĩ guoya nĩ ũndũ wa andũ a Isiraeli.
english: Moab was very afraid of the people, because they were many. Moab was distressed because of the children of Israel.

kikuyu: Akĩũria Jesũ ciũria nyingĩ, nowe Jesũ ndaigana kũmũcookeria o na kĩmwe.
english: He questioned him with many words, but he gave no answers.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Solomoni agĩikarĩra gĩtĩ gĩa ũthamaki kĩa Jehova, na agĩtuĩka mũthamaki ithenya rĩa ithe Daudi. Nake akĩgaacĩra na agĩathĩkĩrwo Isiraeli guothe.
english: Then Solomon sat on the throne of Yahweh as king instead of David his father, and prospered; and all Israel obeyed him.

kikuyu: Kenani aakinyia mĩaka mĩrongo mũgwanja-rĩ, akĩgĩa na mũriũ wetagwo Mahalaleli.
english: Kenan lived seventy years, then became the father of Mahalalel.

kikuyu: Nake Jusufu akĩmaruta maru-inĩ ma Isiraeli, akĩinamĩrĩria ũthiũ akĩũturumithia thĩ.
english: Joseph brought them out from between his knees, and he bowed himself with his face to the earth.

kikuyu: Nake Zimuri, ũmwe wa anene ake, ũrĩa waathaga nuthu ya ngaari ciake cia ita, agĩciirĩra ũrĩa ekũmũũkĩrĩra. Hĩndĩ ĩyo Ela aarĩ Tiriza, akĩnyua njoohi mũciĩ kwa Ariza ũrĩa warĩ mũrori wa nyũmba ya ũthamaki kũu Tiriza.
english: His servant Zimri, captain of half his chariots, conspired against him. Now he was in Tirzah, drinking himself drunk in the house of Arza, who was over the household in Tirzah;

kikuyu: Nĩmakũũranirie ũhoro-ũrĩa-wa-ma wa Ngai na maheeni, makĩhooya na magĩtungatĩra indo iria ciombirwo handũ ha gũtungatĩra Mũciũmbi, o ũcio wa kũgoocagwo nginya tene. Ameni.
english: who exchanged the truth of God for a lie, and worshiped and served the creature rather than the Creator, who is blessed forever. Amen.

kikuyu: Nĩnjũũĩ ciĩko ciaku, ngamenya atĩ wee ndũrĩ mũhoro na ndũrĩ mũhiũ. Kũngĩrĩ wega korwo waheha kana ũhiũhe!
english: “I know your works, that you are neither cold nor hot. I wish you were cold or hot.

kikuyu: Tondũ mũcithinĩkanagia na rwere, o na kĩande, nacio ngʼondu iria itarĩ na hinya mũgacitiindĩka na hĩa cianyu o nginya mũgaciingata,
english: Because you thrust with side and with shoulder, and push all the diseased with your horns, until you have scattered them abroad,

kikuyu: “Mũndũ angĩgĩa na ihĩa ngoothi-inĩ yake, narĩo ihĩa rĩu rĩgĩe na kĩronda gĩtunĩhe kana kĩerũhe- rĩ,
english: “Or when the body has a burn from fire on its skin, and the raw flesh of the burn becomes a bright spot, reddish-white, or white,

kikuyu: Nao andũ a Misiri nĩmakamenya atĩ niĩ nĩ niĩ Jehova, rĩrĩa ngataambũrũkia guoko gwakwa njũkĩrĩre bũrũri wa Misiri na ndute andũ a Isiraeli moime kuo.”
english: The Egyptians shall know that I am Yahweh when I stretch out my hand on Egypt, and bring the children of Israel out from among them.”

kikuyu: No ũngĩhikania-rĩ, wee ndwĩhĩtie; o na mũirĩtu angĩhika-rĩ, o nake ndehĩtie. No andũ arĩa marahika kana makahikania-rĩ, nĩmarĩonaga mathĩĩna maingĩ mũtũũrĩre-inĩ ũyũ, na nĩmo ndĩrenda kũmũrigĩrĩria.
english: But if you marry, you have not sinned. If a virgin marries, she has not sinned. Yet such will have oppression in the flesh, and I want to spare you.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwokĩhumokĩaũnabiigĩtioimanirenakwendakwamũndũ, noandũnĩmaririeũhorouumĩte kũrĩ Ngai, o ta ũrĩa Roho Mũtheru aamatongoririe.
english: For no prophecy ever came by the will of man, but holy men of God spoke, being moved by the Holy Spirit.

kikuyu: No mũthamaki akiuga atĩrĩ, “Tũnyiitanĩire ũndũ ũrĩkũ niĩ na inyuĩ, inyuĩ ariũ a Zeruia? Angĩkorwo ararumana tondũ Jehova nĩamwĩrĩte atĩrĩ, ‘Ruma Daudi,’ nũũ ũngĩmũũria atĩrĩ, ‘Ũgwĩka ũguo nĩkĩ?’ ”
english: The king said, “What have I to do with you, you sons of Zeruiah? Because he curses, and because Yahweh has said to him, ‘Curse David,’ who then shall say, ‘Why have you done so?’ ”

kikuyu: Mangĩgakwĩra atĩrĩ, “Ũka ũthiĩ hamwe na ithuĩ; reke tuoherie mũndũ tũite thakame yake, reke tũcemaceeme mũndũ o na ũtehĩtie;
english: If they say, “Come with us. Let’s lie in wait for blood. Let’s lurk secretly for the innocent without cause.

kikuyu: Gwĩka ũrĩa kwagĩrĩire na ũrĩa ũrĩ kĩhooto nĩ gwĩtĩkagĩrwo nĩ Jehova gũkĩra kũruta igongona.
english: To do righteousness and justice is more acceptable to Yahweh than sacrifice.

kikuyu: Mahũthĩro ma Solomoni ma o mũthenya maarĩ maya: kori mĩrongo ĩtatũ† cia mũtu ũrĩa mũhinyu mũno, na kori mĩrongo ĩtandatũ‡ cia mũtu,
english: Solomon’s provision for one day was thirty cors† of fine flour, sixty measures of meal,

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo o ta ũrĩa andũ othe makuaga nĩ ũndũ wa Adamu-rĩ, no taguo andũ othe magaacookio muoyo thĩinĩ wa Kristũ.
english: For as in Adam all die, so also in Christ all will be made alive.

kikuyu: No nginya rũũru rwakwa rũrĩĩage kĩrĩa mũrangĩrĩirie, na rũnyuuage kĩrĩa munjugĩte na makinya manyu?”
english: As for my sheep, they eat that which you have trodden with your feet, and they drink that which you have fouled with your feet.’

kikuyu: Ũhoro wa gũciarwo kwa Jesũ Kristũ watariĩ ũũ: Nyina Mariamu nĩ oorĩtio nĩ Jusufu atuĩke mũtumia wake, no matanaikarania-rĩ, nĩamenyekire atĩ arĩ na nda ya Roho Mũtheru.
english: Now the birth of Jesus Christ was like this: After his mother, Mary, was engaged to Joseph, before they came together, she was found pregnant by the Holy Spirit.

kikuyu: we na andũ ake othe makĩambata Kĩrĩma-inĩ gĩa Zalimuna. Akĩoya ithanwa, agĩtema honge, na agĩciigĩrĩra ciande. Agĩatha andũ arĩa maarĩ nake, akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Narua! Ĩkai ũguo muona ndeka!”
english: Abimelech went up to Mount Zalmon, he and all the people who were with him; and Abimelech took an ax in his hand, and cut down a bough from the trees, and took it up, and laid it on his shoulder. Then he said to the people who were with him, “What you have seen me do, make haste, and do as I have done!”

kikuyu: Akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Reke hakorwo harĩ na kĩrĩĩko giitũ nawe o ta ũrĩa haarĩ kĩrĩĩko gatagatĩ ka baba na thoguo. Ta kĩone, nĩndagũtũmĩra betha na thahabu. Rĩu tharia kĩrĩĩko kĩanyu na Baasha, mũthamaki wa Isiraeli nĩguo atigane na niĩ.”
english: “Let there be a treaty between me and you, as there was between my father and your father. Behold, I have sent you silver and gold. Go, break your treaty with Baasha king of Israel, that he may depart from me.”

kikuyu: Nao andũ arĩa maahĩtũkagĩra hau makamũrumaga, makĩinagia mĩtwe yao,
english: Those who passed by blasphemed him, wagging their heads

kikuyu: Nake akiuga o rĩngĩ atĩrĩ, “Nĩtũthiĩ tũgerie gũkinya Gibea kana Rama na tũrarĩrĩre itũũra rĩmwe rĩamo.”
english: He said to his servant, “Come and let’s draw near to one of these places; and we will lodge in Gibeah, or in Ramah.”

kikuyu: Ngoro cia andũ nĩirakaĩra Mwathani. Atĩrĩrĩ, wee rũthingo rũrũ rwa Mwarĩ wa Zayuni, reke maithori maku manyũrũrũke ta rũũĩ mũthenya na ũtukũ; ndũkahurũke, kana ũreke maitho maku mahurũke.
english: Their heart cried to the Lord. O wall of the daughter of Zion, let tears run down like a river day and night. Give yourself no relief. Don’t let your eyes rest.

kikuyu: No mũige imaramari igĩrĩ imũngʼetheire, mũciĩre irute ũira wa atĩ nĩarumĩte Ngai na akaruma mũthamaki. Mũcooke mũmuumie nja ya itũũra mũmũhũre na mahiga nyuguto nginya akue.”
english: Set two men, wicked fellows, before him, and let them testify against him, saying, ‘You cursed God and the king!’ Then carry him out, and stone him to death.”

kikuyu: Solomoni aathamakĩire Isiraeli rĩothe arĩ Jerusalemu mĩaka mĩrongo ĩna.
english: The time that Solomon reigned in Jerusalem over all Israel was forty years.

kikuyu: (Andũ a Isiraeli nĩmathiire rũgendo moimĩte ithima-inĩ cia andũ a Jaakani, magĩkinya Mosera. Kũu nĩkuo Harũni aakuĩrĩire, na nokuo aathikirwo; nake mũriũ Eliazaru agĩtuĩka mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai ithenya rĩake.
english: (The children of Israel traveled from Beeroth Bene Jaakan to Moserah. There Aaron died, and there he was buried; and Eleazar his son ministered in the priest’s office in his place.

kikuyu: Mũtwe ũmwe wa nyamũ ĩyo wonekaga ta waringĩtwo iringa rĩa kĩũrago, no iringa rĩu rĩa kĩũrago nĩrĩahonete. Nayo thĩ yothe nĩyagegete na ĩkĩrũmĩrĩra nyamũ ĩyo.
english: One of his heads looked like it had been wounded fatally. His fatal wound was healed, and the whole earth marveled at the beast.

kikuyu: Nowe Mariamu akĩiga maũndũ macio mothe ngoro-inĩ yake, na agĩikara akĩmeciiragia.
english: But Mary kept all these sayings, pondering them in her heart.

kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, o na gũtariĩ ũguo, hĩndĩ ĩyo marĩ bũrũri-inĩ wa thũ ciao, ndikamarega kana ndĩmathũũre biũ ũndũ ũngĩtũma ndĩmaniine biũ, thũkie kĩrĩkanĩro gĩakwa nao. Niĩ nĩ niĩ Jehova Ngai wao.
english: Yet for all that, when they are in the land of their enemies, I will not reject them, neither will I abhor them, to destroy them utterly and to break my covenant with them; for I am Yahweh their God.

kikuyu: Hamwe na ũguo-rĩ, thondekera handũ ha gũikara, tondũ ndĩ na mwĩhoko atĩ nĩndĩtĩkĩrio njũke kũrĩ inyuĩ nĩ ũndũ wa mahooya marĩa mũhooyagĩra.
english: Also, prepare a guest room for me, for I hope that through your prayers I will be restored to you.

kikuyu: Indo iria aarutire ciarĩ thaani ĩmwe ya betha ya ũritũ wa cekeri igana rĩmwe rĩa mĩrongo ĩtatũ,* na mbakũri ĩmwe ya betha ya kũminjaminjĩria ya ũritũ wa cekeri mĩrongo mũgwanja,† cierĩ kũringana na gĩthimo gĩa cekeri ya harĩa haamũre, na o ĩmwe yaiyũrĩtio mũtu ũrĩa mũhinyu mũno ũtukanĩtio na maguta, ũrĩ iruta rĩa mũtu;
english: and his offering was: one silver platter, the weight of which was one hundred thirty shekels,† one silver bowl of seventy shekels, according to the shekel of the sanctuary,

kikuyu: NĩũndũũcionĩnduĩteatĩnĩngwakahekarũĩĩtanĩtionaRĩĩtwarĩaJehova Ngai wakwa, o ta ũrĩa Jehova eerire baba Daudi rĩrĩa oigire atĩrĩ, ‘Mũriũ waku ũrĩa ngaikarĩria gĩtĩ kĩa ũnene handũ haku-rĩ, nĩwe ũgaaka hekarũ ĩĩtanĩtio na Rĩĩtwa rĩakwa.’
english: Behold, I intend to build a house for the name of Yahweh my God, as Yahweh spoke to David my father, saying, ‘Your son, whom I will set on your throne in your place shall build the house for my name.’

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, Musa na Eleazaru ũrĩa mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai makĩarĩria andũ a Isiraeli kũu werũ-inĩ wa Moabi hakuhĩ na Rũũĩ rwa Jorodani mũrĩmo wa Jeriko, makĩmeera atĩrĩ,
english: Moses and Eleazar the priest spoke with them in the plains of Moab by the Jordan at Jericho, saying,

kikuyu: Inyuĩ nĩmũũĩ atĩ ithuĩ ti ndeto cia kwĩyendereria twaragia, o na kana tũkahithĩrĩra ũhoro wa gũkorokera indo; Ngai nĩwe mũira witũ.
english: For neither were we at any time found using words of flattery, as you know, nor a cloak of covetousness (God is witness),

kikuyu: Na o mũhari-inĩ nĩhaigwo ũbani ũrĩa mũtherie ũtuĩke gĩcunjĩ gĩa kĩririkano ũrũgamĩrĩire mĩgate ĩyo, na ũtuĩke iruta rĩa njino rĩrutĩirwo Jehova na mwaki.
english: You shall put pure frankincense on each row, that it may be to the bread for a memorial, even an offering made by fire to Yahweh.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo nĩmũkamenya atĩ niĩ ndĩ thĩinĩ wa Isiraeli, na atĩ niĩ nĩ niĩ Jehova Ngai wanyu, na atĩ gũtirĩ ũngĩ; andũ akwa matigacooka gũconorithio rĩngĩ. Mũthenya wa Jehova
english: You will know that I am among Israel, and that I am Yahweh, your God, and there is no one else; and my people will never again be

kikuyu: O na ũngĩnjĩĩra atĩrĩ, “Ithuĩ twĩhokete Jehova Ngai witũ”, githĩ tiwe wehereirio kũndũ kũrĩa gũtũũgĩru, na igongona iria ciarĩ kuo nĩ Hezekia, akĩĩra andũ a Juda na Jerusalemu atĩrĩ, “No nginya mũhooyagĩre mbere ya kĩgongona gĩkĩ kĩrĩ Jerusalemu?”
english: But if you tell me, ‘We trust in Yahweh our God,’ isn’t that he whose high places and whose altars Hezekiah has taken away, and has said to Judah and to Jerusalem, ‘You shall worship before this altar in Jerusalem’?

kikuyu: Atĩrĩrĩ, ngururĩte mũhiano waku hĩ-inĩ ciakwa, nacio thingo ciaku ikoragwo mbere yakwa hĩndĩ ciothe.
english: Behold, I have engraved you on the palms of my hands. Your walls are continually before me.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo o na nĩnyonete kĩgongona kĩandĩkĩtwo maandĩko maya: KŨRĨ NGAI ĨRĨA ĨTOĨO, rĩrĩa ngoretwo ngĩceera na ngarora wega indo cianyu iria nyamũre cia kũhooywo. Na rĩrĩ, kĩrĩa mũhooyaga mũtooĩ nĩkĩo ngũmũhe ũhoro wakĩo.
english: For as I passed along and observed the objects of your worship, I also found an altar with this inscription: ‘TO AN UNKNOWN GOD.’ What therefore you worship in ignorance, I announce to you.

kikuyu: tũgũrage athĩĩni na betha, nao arĩa abatari tũkamagũra na nyamũga igĩrĩ, o na twendagie magira hamwe na ngano.”
english: that we may buy the poor for silver, and the needy for a pair of sandals, and sell the sweepings with the wheat?’ ”

kikuyu: Jokitani nĩwe warĩ ithe wa Alimodadi, na Shelefu, na Hazarimavethu, na Jera,
english: Joktan became the father of Almodad, Sheleph, Hazarmaveth, Jerah,

kikuyu: Na niĩ ngĩcooka ngĩũria atĩrĩ, “Mwathani, nĩ nginya rĩ?” Nake akĩnjookeria atĩrĩ: “Nĩ nginya hĩndĩ ĩrĩa matũũra marĩa manene magaakorwo maanangĩtwo na matarĩ na wa kũmatũũra, nacio nyũmba ikorwo ĩtiganĩirio, na mĩgũnda ĩkaanangwo na ĩgathũkio biũ,
english: Then I said, “Lord, how long?” He answered, “Until cities are waste without inhabitant, houses without man, the land becomes utterly waste,

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio ngĩĩhũgũrĩra Jehova Ngai, ngĩmũhooya, ngĩmũthaithaga ndĩhingĩte kũrĩa irio, na ndĩhumbĩte nguo ya ikũnia, na ngehurĩria mũhu mũtwe.
english: I set my face to the Lord God, to seek by prayer and petitions, with fasting and sackcloth and ashes.

kikuyu: Akĩmĩikia Irima rĩu-Rĩtarĩ- Gĩturi, akĩmĩhingĩrĩria kuo, na akĩrĩcinĩrĩra mũhũri, nĩguo amĩgirĩrĩrie ndĩkanacooke kũheenia andũ a ndũrĩrĩ rĩngĩ nginya mĩaka ĩyo ngiri ĩmwe ĩthire. Thuutha wa ũguo nĩĩkarekererio ihinda inini.
english: and cast him into the abyss, and shut it and sealed it over him, that he should deceive the nations no more until the thousand years were finished. After this, he must be freed for a short time.

kikuyu: Naniĩnĩngamatambũrũkĩriaguokondĩmookĩrĩre, nandũme bũrũri wanangĩke ũtiganĩrio, kuuma werũ-inĩ ũcio nginya Dibila, kũndũ guothe kũrĩa matũũrĩte. Hĩndĩ ĩyo nĩmakamenya atĩ niĩ nĩ niĩ Jehova.’ ”
english: I will stretch out my hand on them and make the land desolate and waste, from the wilderness toward Diblah, throughout all their habitations. Then they will know that I am Yahweh.’ ”

kikuyu: nao nĩ atharamara, na andũ a gwaka, na aaki a mahiga. Na ningĩ ũtigĩrĩre nĩmagũra mbaũ na mahiga maicũhie ma gũcookereria hekarũ.
english: to the carpenters, and to the builders, and to the masons, and for buying timber and cut stone to repair the house.

kikuyu: Ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ na ma, amwe marũngiĩ haha matigacama gĩkuũ matonete Mũrũ wa Mũndũ agĩũka na ũthamaki wake.”
english: Most certainly I tell you, there are some standing here who will in no way taste of death until they see the Son of Man coming in his Kingdom.”

kikuyu: Gũtirĩ hĩndĩ ĩngĩ mũndũ mũkĩĩgu agaacooka kũgĩa igweta,
english: The fool will no longer be called noble, nor the scoundrel be highly respected.

kikuyu: Ngĩmenya atĩ rĩathani o rĩu rĩarĩ rĩa gũtũma mũndũ atũũre muoyo-rĩ, norĩo rĩarehire gĩkuũ.
english: The commandment which was for life, this I found to be for death;

kikuyu: ĩrimũ kĩĩraga na ngoro atĩrĩ, “Gũtirĩ Ngai.” Nao amaramari, ciĩko ciao irĩ magigi; gũtirĩ mũndũ o na ũmwe wĩkaga wega.
english: he fool has said in his heart, “There is no God.” They are corrupt, and have done abominable iniquity. There is no one who does good.

kikuyu: Menyerera wathĩkĩre mawatho maya mothe ndĩragwatha, nĩgeetha maũndũ maku na ma ciana ciaku iria igooka thuutha waku maagagĩrĩre hĩndĩ ciothe, tondũ ũrĩkoragwo ũgĩĩka wega na ũrĩa kwagĩrĩire maitho-inĩ ma Jehova Ngai waku.
english: Observe and hear all these words which I command you, that it may go well with you and with your children after you forever, when you do that which is good and right in Yahweh your God’s eyes.

kikuyu: No makĩmũringĩrĩria o nginya agĩconoka, akĩremwo nĩ kũrega. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio akiuga atĩrĩ, “Matũmei.” Nao magĩtũma andũ mĩrongo ĩtano, arĩa maamwethire mĩthenya ĩtatũ, no matiigana kũmuona.
english: When they urged him until he was ashamed, he said, “Send them.” Therefore they sent fifty men; and they searched for three days, but didn’t find him.

kikuyu: Mũthenya ũmwe nĩndathiire mũciĩ gwa Shemaia mũrũ wa Delaia, mũrũ wa Mehetabeli, ũrĩa wahingĩirwo gwake mũciĩ. Akĩnjĩĩra atĩrĩ, “Nĩtũcemanie nyũmba ya Ngai, hekarũ thĩinĩ, na tũhinge mĩrango ya hekarũ, tondũ kũrĩ na andũ maroka gũkũũraga, megũka gũkũũraga ũtukũ.”
english: I went to the house of Shemaiah the son of Delaiah the son of Mehetabel, who was shut in at his home; and he said, “Let us meet together in God’s house, within the temple, and let’s shut the doors of the temple; for they will come to kill you. Yes, in the night they will come to kill you.”

kikuyu: “Wee Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe, Ngai wa Isiraeli, nĩũguũrĩirie ndungata yaku ũndũ ũyũ, ũkoiga atĩrĩ, ‘Nĩngagwakĩra nyũmba.’ Nĩ ũndũ ũcio ndungata yaku nĩĩgĩĩte na ũmĩrĩru wa kũhooya ihooya rĩrĩ.
english: For you, Yahweh of Armies, the God of Israel, have revealed to your servant, saying, ‘I will build you a house.’ Therefore your servant has found in his heart to pray this prayer to you.

kikuyu: Narĩu-rĩ, harĩũhorowakwendananaariũnaaarĩaIthewitũ-rĩ, tũtibatairekũmwandĩkĩranĩũndũ inyuĩ ene nĩmũrutĩtwo kwendanaga nĩ Ngai.
english: But concerning brotherly love, you have no need that one write to you. For you yourselves are taught by God to love one another,

kikuyu: Nja ĩyo ĩkorwo na ũraihu wa mĩkono igana,‡ na mĩkono mĩrongo ĩtano kwarama,§ na § 26:33 Mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai ũrĩa mũnene nowe wiki wetĩkĩrĩtio gũtoonya Harĩa-Hatheru-Mũno, na eekaga ũguo riita rĩmwe o mwaka hĩndĩ ĩrĩa ekũhoroheria andũ mehia mao. * 27:1 nĩ ta mita ĩmwe na robo (1:25) † 27:1 nĩ ta mita igĩrĩ na robo (2:25) ‡ 27:9
english: The length of the court shall be one hundred cubits, and the width fifty throughout, and the height five cubits, of fine twined linen, and their sockets of bronze.

kikuyu: Kaĩ mũtararirikana atĩ rĩrĩa ndarĩ hamwe na inyuĩ nĩndamwĩraga maũndũ macio?
english: Don’t you remember that when I was still with you, I told you these things?

kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Amazia aacookire oimĩte kũũraga andũ a Edomu-rĩ, nĩainũkire na ngai cia andũ a Seiru. Agĩcirũgamia ta ngai ciake mwene, agĩciinamĩrĩra na agĩcirutĩra magongona ma njino.
english: Now after Amaziah had come from the slaughter of the Edomites, he brought the gods of the children of Seir, and set them up to be his gods, and bowed down himself before them and burned incense to them.

kikuyu: Jehova agĩcemania na Balamu, agĩĩkĩra ndũmĩrĩri kanua gake, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Cooka kũrĩ Balaki ũmũhe ndũmĩrĩri ĩno.”
english: Yahweh met Balaam, and put a word in his mouth, and said, “Return to Balak, and say this.”
