kikuyu: Nĩ niĩ Johana, Ndĩratũmĩra makanitha marĩa mũgwanja marĩ kũu bũrũri wa Asia* ũhoro ũyũ: Atĩrĩrĩ, wega na thayũ irogĩa na inyuĩ kuuma kũrĩ ũrĩa ũrĩ ho, na nowe warĩ ho, na nowe ũgooka, na kuuma kũrĩ maroho mũgwanja marĩa maikaraga mbere ya gĩtĩ gĩake kĩa ũnene,
english: John, to the seven assemblies that are in Asia: Grace to you and peace from God, who is and who was and who is to come; and from the seven Spirits who are before his throne;

kikuyu: Nĩ kaba gũtũũra werũ-inĩ, gũkĩra gũtũũra na mũtumia wa haaro na kũrakara narua.
english: It is better to dwell in a desert land, than with a contentious and fretful woman.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio magĩatha andũ a Benjamini, makĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Thiĩi mwĩhithe kũu mĩgũnda-inĩ ya mĩthabibũ
english: They commanded the children of Benjamin, saying, “Go and lie in wait in the vineyards,

kikuyu: Nĩwe ũgaacookererwo nĩ riiri wothe wa nyũmba ya ithe ta ũcuurĩtio harĩ we: ciana ciake na iria ikeyongerera thĩinĩ wa ciana icio, o indo ciothe ciayo iria cionekaga ta itarĩ kĩene, kuuma mbakũri nginya mĩthemba yothe ya ndigithũ.”
english: They will hang on him all the glory of his father’s house, the offspring and the issue, every small vessel, from the cups even to all the pitchers.

kikuyu: Harũni akĩmacookeria akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Rutai icũhĩ cia thahabu iria irĩ matũ ma atumia anyu, na aanake na airĩtu anyu mũndehere.”
english: Aaron said to them, “Take off the golden rings, which are in the ears of your wives, of your sons, and of your daughters, and bring them to me.”

kikuyu: “ ‘Mũndũ mũrũme angĩhikia mũndũ-wa-nja na ahikie nyina, ũcio nĩ waganu. Mũndũ mũrũme ũcio na andũ-a-nja acio eerĩ no nginya macinwo na mwaki, nĩgeetha gũtikanakorwo na waganu thĩinĩ wanyu.
english: “ ‘If a man takes a wife and her mother, it is wickedness. They shall be burned with fire, both he and they, that there may be no wickedness among you.

kikuyu: Nao nĩmakarehe ariũ a thoguo othe, kuuma ndũrĩrĩ-inĩ ciothe, marehwo makuuĩtwo nĩ mbarathi, na ngaari cia ita, na makaari ma kũguucio, na mahaicĩte nyũmbũ na ngamĩĩra, moke kĩrĩma-inĩ gĩakwa kĩrĩa kĩamũre kũu Jerusalemu, matuĩke ta igongona harĩ Jehova,” ũguo nĩguo Jehova ekuuga. “Nao magaacirehe o ta ũrĩa andũ a Isiraeli marehaga ngano yao ya maruta kũu hekarũ-inĩ ya Jehova ĩkuuĩtwo na indo iria itarĩ thaahu.
english: They shall bring all your brothers out of all the nations for an offering to Yahweh, on horses, in chariots, in litters, on mules, and on camels, to my holy mountain Jerusalem, says Yahweh, as the children of Israel bring their offering in a clean vessel into Yahweh’s house.

kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, rĩu ũhaandĩtwo werũ-inĩ wa mũthanga, bũrũri ũrĩa mũũmũ, ũrĩ nyoota.
english: Now it is planted in the wilderness, in a dry and thirsty land.

kikuyu: Ti-itherũ nĩegũgũteithũra gĩkerenge-inĩ kĩa mũtegi, o na kuuma mũrimũ-inĩ ũrĩa mũũru wa kũũraga.
english: For he will deliver you from the snare of the fowler, and from the deadly pestilence.

kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, andũ a Misiri makuona mekuuga atĩrĩ, ‘Ũyũ nĩ mũtumia wake,’ tondũ ũcio manjũrage no wee makũreke ũtũũre muoyo, makuoe.
english: It will happen that when the Egyptians see you, they will say, ‘This is his wife.’ They will kill me, but they will save you alive.

kikuyu: na ũthondekithie magĩe na mũtararĩko mwega wa ũbumba; wĩra ũcio ũrutwo nĩ mũthondeki mũũgĩ wa maguta. Makorwo mekĩrĩtwo cumbĩ, na marĩ matheru na makaamũrwo.
english: You shall make incense of it, a perfume after the art of the perfumer, seasoned with salt, pure and holy.

kikuyu: Andũ mangĩkaarega kũmũnyiita ũgeni-rĩ, mũkaaribariba magũrũ manyu rũkũngũ rũitĩke mũkiuma itũũra rĩao arĩ ũira wa kũmatuĩra ciira.”
english: As many as don’t receive you, when you depart from that city, shake off even the dust from your feet for a testimony against them.”

kikuyu: Azeli aarĩ na ariũ atandatũ, na maya nĩmo marĩĩtwa mao: Azirikamu, na Bokeru, na Ishumaeli, na Shearia, na Obadia, na Hanani. Acio nĩo maarĩ ariũ a Azeli.
english: Azel had six sons, whose names are these: Azrikam, Bocheru, Ishmael, Sheariah, Obadiah, and Hanan. All these were the sons of Azel.

kikuyu: Nĩndĩhĩtĩte ngĩgwetaga ũtheru wakwa, rĩmwe rĩa kũigana, na ndirĩ hĩndĩ ngaaheenia Daudi,
english: Once I have sworn by my holiness, I will not lie to David.

kikuyu: Ta thikĩrĩria! Niĩ nĩngwĩkĩra roho wa mũthemba mũna thĩinĩ wake, nĩguo rĩrĩa arĩigua ũhoro mũna-rĩ, acooke bũrũri wake, na arĩ kũu ndũme ooragwo na rũhiũ rwa njora.’ ”
english: Behold, I will put a spirit in him, and he will hear news, and will return to his own land. I will cause him to fall by the sword in his own land.” ’ ”

kikuyu: “Nake Juda aheo gĩcigo kĩmwe; nakĩo gĩkaahakana na rũgongo rwa Rubeni kuuma mwena wa irathĩro nginya mwena wa ithũĩro.
english: “By the border of Reuben, from the east side to the west side, Judah, one portion.

kikuyu: Nao maanagĩrĩra na mũgambo mũnene makoiga atĩrĩ: “Kũhonokania nĩ kwa Ngai witũ, ũrĩa ũikarĩire gĩtĩ kĩa ũnene, na gwa Gatũrũme.”
english: They cried with a loud voice, saying, “Salvation be to our God, who sits on the throne, and to the Lamb!”

kikuyu: agĩtũmanĩra Balamu mũrũ wa Beori, ũrĩa warĩ Pethori, hakuhĩ na Rũũĩ rwa Farati, kũu bũrũri wao. Balaki akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ: “Harĩ na andũ mokĩte moimĩte bũrũri wa Misiri; nao maiyũrĩte bũrũri ũyũ wothe, na maikarĩte o hakuhĩ na niĩ.
english: He sent messengers to Balaam the son of Beor, to Pethor, which is by the River, to the land of the children of his people, to call him, saying, “Behold, there is a people who came out of Egypt. Behold, they cover the surface of the earth, and they are staying opposite me.

kikuyu: Imakania imuumagĩrĩra na mĩena yothe, na ikamũtengʼeragia harĩa hothe aagerera.
english: Terrors will make him afraid on every side, and will chase him at his heels.

kikuyu: Ikerubi rĩa keerĩ o narĩo rĩarĩ rĩa mĩkono ikũmi; makerubi macio meerĩ nĩmaiganaine na makahaanana.
english: The other cherub was ten cubits. Both the cherubim were of one measure and one form.

kikuyu: Nũũ ũngĩthikĩrĩria ũguo mũroiga? Igai rĩa mũndũ ũrĩa watigirwo akĩrora indo rĩkũiganana na rĩa ũrĩa waikũrũkire agĩthiĩ mbaara-inĩ. Othe mekũgayana o ũndũ ũmwe.”
english: Who will listen to you in this matter? For as his share is who goes down to the battle, so shall his share be who stays with the baggage. They shall share alike.”

kikuyu: Nao andũ a Shupimu na a Hupimu maarĩ a rũciaro rwa Iru, nao Ahushimu maarĩ a rũciaro rwa Aheri. Njiaro cia Nafitali
english: So were Shuppim, Huppim, the sons of Ir, Hushim, and the sons of Aher.

kikuyu: makĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Wee Kristũ, tũrathĩre ũhoro ũtwĩre, nũũ wakũgũtha!” Petero Gũkaana Jesũ
english: saying, “Prophesy to us, you Christ! Who hit you?”

kikuyu: Mũthenya wa mbere Jona akĩambĩrĩria gũtoonya itũũra rĩu. Akĩanĩrĩra, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Gwatigia mĩthenya mĩrongo ĩna Nineve kũharaganio.”
english: Jonah began to enter into the city a day’s journey, and he cried out, and said, “In forty days, Nineveh will be overthrown!”

kikuyu: No inyuĩ nĩmwandiganĩirie na mũgĩtungatĩra ngai ingĩ, tondũ ũcio ndigacooka kũmũhonokia o na rĩ.
english: Yet you have forsaken me and served other gods. Therefore I will save you no more.

kikuyu: Adonija na ageni othe arĩa maarĩ hamwe nake makĩigua mũrurumo ũcio rĩrĩa maarĩ hakuhĩ kũrĩĩkia iruga rĩao. Rĩrĩa maiguire mũgambo wa karumbeta, Joabu akĩũria atĩrĩ, “Inegene rĩu rĩothe rĩrĩ itũũra-inĩ nĩ rĩa kĩĩ?”
english: Adonijah and all the guests who were with him heard it as they had finished eating. When Joab heard the sound of the trumpet, he said, “Why is this noise of the city being in an uproar?”

kikuyu: Andũ a Shiloni maarĩ: Asaia ũrĩa warĩ irigithathi, na ariũ ake.
english: Of the Shilonites: Asaiah the firstborn and his sons.

kikuyu: Nĩngũũkĩra thiĩ ngacangacange itũũra-inĩ inene, hungure njĩra-inĩ ciarĩo na nja ciarĩo; thiĩ ngeethe ũcio ngoro yakwa yendete. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio ngĩmũcaria, no ndiigana kũmuona.
english: I will get up now, and go about the city; in the streets and in the squares I will seek him whom my soul loves. I sought him, but I didn’t find him.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo mageetaga itũũra rĩa Jerusalemu Gĩtĩ kĩa Ũthamaki kĩa Jehova, nacio ndũrĩrĩ ciothe nĩikongana kũu itũũra rĩa Jerusalemu nĩ ũndũ wa gũtĩĩa rĩĩtwa rĩa Jehova. Matigacooka kũrũmĩrĩra ũremi wa ũũru wa ngoro ciao.
english: At that time they will call Jerusalem ‘Yahweh’s Throne;’ and all the nations will be gathered to it, to Yahweh’s name, to Jerusalem. They will no longer walk after the stubbornness of their evil heart.

kikuyu: “Mwĩyakĩrei nyũmba na mũcitũũre; haandai mĩgũnda mũcooke mũrĩe maciaro mayo.
english: “Build houses and dwell in them. Plant gardens and eat their fruit.

kikuyu: Kĩrĩa kũrĩ no kũrumana, na kũheenania, na ũragani, na ũici, na ũtharia; maũndũ macio makaingĩha mũno, na ũiti wa thakame ũkarũmĩrĩra ũiti wa thakame.
english: There is cursing, lying, murder, stealing, and committing adultery; they break boundaries, and bloodshed causes bloodshed.

kikuyu: Andũ arĩa aaganu macangacangaga marĩ na mwĩtĩĩo, rĩrĩa ũndũ ũrĩa mũũru ũragathĩrĩrio nĩ andũ.
english: The wicked walk on every side, when what is vile is exalted among the sons of men.

kikuyu: Akĩiga mũtĩ wa kũigĩrĩrwo matawa thĩinĩ wa Hema-ya-Gũtũnganwo ũngʼetheire metha mwena wa gũthini wa hema ĩyo nyamũre,
english: He put the lamp stand in the Tent of Meeting, opposite the table, on the south side of the tabernacle.

kikuyu: Nĩwahaandĩtwo tĩĩri-inĩ mwega handũ harĩ na maaĩ maingĩ nĩguo ũrute honge, na ũciare maciaro, na ũtuĩke mũthabibũ ũrĩ na riiri.’
english: It was planted in a good soil by many waters, that it might produce branches and that it might bear fruit, that it might be a good vine.” ’

kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, aigua ta ekumwo nĩ ngoro nĩ kuona mũrũ wa nyina Benjamini-rĩ, Jusufu agĩkiuma na ihenya agĩthiĩ gũcaria handũ angĩrĩrĩra. Nake agĩthiĩ kanyũmba gake ga thĩinĩ akĩrĩrĩra kuo.
english: Joseph hurried, for his heart yearned over his brother; and he sought a place to weep. He entered into his room, and wept there.

kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, Baruku mũrũ wa Neria nĩwe ũkũringĩrĩirie ũtũũkĩrĩre, nĩguo tũneanwo kũrĩ andũ a Babuloni, nĩguo matũũrage, kana matũtahe, matũtware Babuloni.”
english: but Baruch the son of Neriah has turned you against us, to deliver us into the hand of the Chaldeans, that they may put us to death or carry us away captive to Babylon.”

kikuyu: Mũtirĩ hĩndĩ mũgaacooka gũkũ bũrũri ũyũ mwĩriragĩria gũgaacooka.”
english: But to the land to which their soul longs to return, there they will not return.”

kikuyu: Rũciinĩ Daudi akĩandĩkĩra Joabu marũa, akĩmanengera Uria atware.
english: In the morning, David wrote a letter to Joab and sent it by the hand of Uriah.

kikuyu: “Atĩrĩrĩ, nĩngamũrehithĩria nyoka irĩ thumu gatagatĩ kanyu, ndĩmũrehithĩrie nduĩra iria itangĩhoorereka, nacio nĩikamũrũma,” ũguo nĩguo Jehova ekuuga.
english: “For, behold, I will send serpents, adders among you, which will not be charmed; and they will bite you,” says Yahweh.

kikuyu: ĩ ũndũ ũcio-rĩ, nĩtwagĩrĩirwo kũrũmbũiya mũno ũhoro ũrĩa twanaigua, nĩguo tũtikae gũtũgũgio tweherio kuuma harĩ guo.
english: herefore we ought to pay greater attention to the things that were heard, lest perhaps we drift away.

kikuyu: Kũrigĩrĩria-rĩ, ikundi cia kambĩ ya Dani ikiumagara irĩ thuutha wa icio ingĩ ciothe, ikiumagara ĩrũmĩrĩire bendera yao. Ahiezeri mũrũ wa Amishadai nĩwe warĩ mũtongoria wao.
english: The standard of the camp of the children of Dan, which was the rear guard of all the camps, set forward according to their armies. Ahiezer the son of Ammishaddai was over his army.

kikuyu: “Andũ maathikagĩrĩria marĩ na kĩĩrĩgĩrĩro, meetagĩrĩra kĩrĩra gĩakwa makirĩte.
english: “Men listened to me, waited, and kept silence for my counsel.

kikuyu: Mwathi wakwa aroaga kũrũmbũiya mũndũ ũcio mwaganu ti Nabali. Ahaana o ta rĩĩtwa rĩake, rĩĩtwa rĩake riugĩte mũndũ mũkĩĩgu, na akoragwo arĩ o mũkĩĩgu. No niĩ ndungata yaku-rĩ, ndionire andũ acio mwathi wakwa aatũmĩte.
english: Please don’t let my lord pay attention to this worthless fellow, Nabal, for as his name is, so is he. Nabal§ is his name, and folly is with him; but I, your servant, didn’t see my lord’s young men whom you sent.

kikuyu: Inyuĩ mwakĩrĩ anini gũkĩra makanitha marĩa mangĩ nakĩ, tiga o gũtuĩka atĩ niĩ ndiigana gũtuĩka mũrigo harĩ inyuĩ? Ndekerai ihĩtia rĩu!
english: For what is there in which you were made inferior to the rest of the assemblies, unless it is that I myself was not a burden to you? Forgive me this wrong!

kikuyu: Ĩĩgũtũũra ĩtahaga na wabu wayo, ĩkaniinaga ndũrĩrĩ ingĩ ĩtegũciguĩra tha?
english: Will he therefore continually empty his net, and kill the nations without mercy?

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio hegũkorwo na mbaũ inyanya, na itina ikũmi na ithathatũ cia betha, o igĩrĩ igaikarĩrwo nĩ rũbaũ rũmwe.
english: There shall be eight boards, and their sockets of silver, sixteen sockets; two sockets under one board, and two sockets under another board.

kikuyu: Mũraathirĩkia nĩkĩ, mũgatũma ndaakare nĩ ũndũ wa kĩrĩa mũthondekete na moko manyu na ũndũ wa gũcinĩra ngai ingĩ ũbumba gũkũ bũrũri wa Misiri, kũrĩa mũũkĩte gũtũũra? Nĩmũkwĩniina na mũtuĩke kĩndũ gĩa kũrumagwo na gĩa kũnyararwo thĩinĩ wa ndũrĩrĩ ciothe cia thĩ.
english: in that you provoke me to anger with the works of your hands, burning incense to other gods in the land of Egypt where you have gone to live, that you may be cut off, and that you may be a curse and a reproach among all the nations of the earth?

kikuyu: “Thondeka nja ya hema ĩrĩa nyamũre; mwena wa gũthini ũkorwo ũrĩ wa mĩkono igana‡ kũraiha na ũgĩe na itambaya cia gũcuurio cia gatani ũrĩa mwogothe wega,
english: “You shall make the court of the tabernacle: for the south side southward there shall be hangings for the court of fine twined linen one hundred cubits long for one side.

kikuyu: Mũhaka wa mwena wa irathĩro warĩ Iria rĩa Cumbĩ nginya mũthia wa Rũũĩ rwa Jorodani. Naguo mũhaka wa mwena wa gathigathini wambĩrĩirie gĩcongoco-inĩ kĩa iria mũthia-inĩ wa Rũũĩ rwa Jorodani,
english: The east border was the Salt Sea, even to the end of the Jordan. The border of the north quarter was from the bay of the sea at the end of the Jordan.

kikuyu: Ũguo noguo ũhoro wanyu ũtariĩ. Tiga mwaririe na ciugo ikũmenyeka na rũthiomi rwanyu-rĩ, nĩ mũndũ ũrĩkũ ũngĩmenya ũrĩa mũroiga? Mũngĩkorwo o mũkĩarĩria rĩera.
english: So also you, unless you uttered by the tongue words easy to understand, how would it be known what is spoken? For you would be speaking into the air.

kikuyu: Ningĩ nĩmatumire ndaamĩ cia ciande cia ebodi, nacio ikĩohererwo koine-inĩ igĩrĩ ciayo, nĩguo inyiitithanio nayo.
english: They made shoulder straps for it, joined together. It was joined together at the two ends.

kikuyu: Thoguo na maitũguo marocanjamũka nĩ ũndũ waku; mũtumia ũrĩa wagũciarire arokena!
english: Let your father and your mother be glad! Let her who bore you rejoice!

kikuyu: “Ĩra andũ a Isiraeli macooke na thuutha, na mambe hema gũkuhĩ na Pi-Hahirothu, gatagatĩ ka Migidoli na iria. Nĩmambe hema hũgũrũrũ-inĩ cia iria, kũngʼethanĩra na Baali-Zefoni.
english: “Speak to the children of Israel, that they turn back and encamp before Pihahiroth, between Migdol and the sea, before Baal Zephon. You shall encamp opposite it by the sea.

kikuyu: Mũtikanarĩĩre matũũra-inĩ manyu gĩcunjĩ gĩa ikũmi kĩa ngano yanyu, na ndibei ya mũhihano, na maguta, kana marigithathi ma ndũũru cianyu cia ngʼombe na cia ngʼondu, kana kĩndũ o gĩothe mwĩhĩtĩte kũrutaga, kana maruta manyu ma kwĩyendera, o na kana maruta ma mwanya.
english: You may not eat within your gates the tithe of your grain, or of your new wine, or of your oil, or the firstborn of your herd or of your flock, nor any of your vows which you vow, nor your free will offerings, nor the wave offering of your hand;

kikuyu: “Kaĩ andũ a Ashuri marĩ na haaro-ĩ! o acio rũthanju rwa marakara makwa, na thimbũ ya mangʼũrĩ makwa!
english: Alas Assyrian, the rod of my anger, the staff in whose hand is my indignation!

kikuyu: Makĩria ma ũguo-rĩ, ithuĩ no tũgĩkorwo tũrĩ aira a Ngai a maheeni, tondũ nĩtuumbũrĩte atĩ Ngai nĩariũkirie Kristũ kuuma kũrĩ arĩa akuũ. No rĩrĩ, ndaakĩmũriũkirie angĩkorwo nĩ ma atĩ arĩa akuũ matiriũkagio.
english: Yes, we are also found false witnesses of God, because we testified about God that he raised up Christ, whom he didn’t raise up if it is true that the dead are not raised.

kikuyu: Ti wega kũherithia mũndũ ũtekĩte mahĩtia, kana kũhũũra anene nĩ ũndũ wa ũrũngĩrĩru wao.
english: Also to punish the righteous is not good, nor to flog officials for their integrity.

kikuyu: Mwĩrĩ wakwa nĩũinainaga nĩgũgwĩtigĩra;
english: My flesh trembles for fear of you. I am afraid of your judgments. AYIN

kikuyu: Ĩra Shemaia ũcio Mũnehelami atĩrĩ,
english: Concerning Shemaiah the Nehelamite you shall speak, saying,

kikuyu: Aya nĩo maarĩ atongoria a nyũmba ciao: Ariũ a Rubeni ũrĩa irigithathi rĩa Isiraeli maarĩ Hanoku na Palu, na Hezironi, na Karimi. Acio nĩo maarĩ mbarĩ cia Rubeni.
english: These are the heads of their fathers’ houses. The sons of Reuben the firstborn of Israel: Hanoch, and Pallu, Hezron, and Carmi; these are the families of Reuben.

kikuyu: Musa agĩcookeria andũ acio atĩrĩ, “Tigai gwĩtigĩra. Mwĩhaandei wega, na nĩmũkuona ũhonokio ũrĩa Jehova ekũmũrehere ũmũthĩ. Andũ a Misiri aya mũrona ũmũthĩ, mũtikamona rĩngĩ.
english: Moses said to the people, “Don’t be afraid. Stand still, and see the salvation of Yahweh, which he will work for you today; for you will never again see the Egyptians whom you have seen today.

kikuyu: Kuuma mũhĩrĩga wa Asheri makĩheo Mashali, na Abidoni,
english: and out of the tribe of Asher, Mashal with its pasture lands, Abdon with its pasture lands,

kikuyu: Mũndũ o wothe angĩkũhooya kĩndũ mũhe, na mũndũ o wothe angĩoya kĩndũ gĩaku, ndũkamwĩtie.
english: Give to everyone who asks you, and don’t ask him who takes away your goods to give them back again.

kikuyu: weri wa gatatũ thuutha wa andũ a Isiraeli kuuma bũrũri wa Misiri, mũthenya o ũcio-rĩ, magĩkinya Werũ wa Sinai.
english: 80 Exodus 19:25 19 1 In the third month after the children of Israel had gone out of the land of Egypt, on that same day they came into the wilderness of Sinai.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Ũrĩa Mũtheru wa Isiraeli ekuuga atĩrĩ: “Tondũ nĩmũregete ndũmĩrĩri ĩno, mũkehoka ũhinyanĩrĩria, na mũgatua maheeni kĩndũ gĩa gwĩtiiranagia nakĩo,
english: Therefore the Holy One of Israel says, “Because you despise this word, and trust in oppression and perverseness, and rely on it,

kikuyu: Nake nĩekũmuonia nyũmba nene ya igũrũ ĩkĩrĩtwo indo na ĩgathondekwo wega. Haarĩriai irio cia Bathaka iitũ kuo.”
english: He will himself show you a large upper room furnished and ready. Get ready for us there.”

kikuyu: Matukũ-inĩ make-rĩ, Juda nĩakahonokio, nake Isiraeli atũũre arĩ mũgitĩre. Nake ageetagwo rĩĩtwa rĩrĩ: Jehova-Ũthingu-Witũ.”
english: In his days Judah will be saved, and Israel will dwell safely. This is his name by which he will be called: Yahweh our righteousness.

kikuyu: Akiuga atĩrĩ, “Mũngĩthikĩrĩria mũgambo wa Jehova Ngai wanyu wega, na mwĩke maũndũ marĩa magĩrĩire maitho-inĩ make, mũngĩrũmbũiya maathani make na mũmenyerere wathani wake wothe- rĩ, ndikamũrehera mũrimũ o na ũmwe wa ĩrĩa ndareheire andũ a Misiri, nĩgũkorwo niĩ nĩ niĩ Jehova, ũrĩa ũmũhonagia.”
english: He said, “If you will diligently listen to Yahweh your God’s voice, and will do that which is right in his eyes, and will pay attention to his commandments, and keep all his statutes, I will put none of the diseases on you which I have put on the Egyptians; for I am Yahweh who heals you.”

kikuyu: Ũtukũ ũmwe Eli, ũrĩa maitho make maambĩrĩirie kwaga hinya akaaga kuona wega, aakomete o harĩa aamenyerete.
english: At that time, when Eli was laid down in his place (now his eyes had begun to grow dim, so that he could not see),

kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, mwaka-inĩ wa keerĩ wa ũthamaki wa Peka mũrũ wa Remalia mũthamaki wa Isiraeli, Jothamu mũrũ wa Uzia mũthamaki wa Juda akĩambĩrĩria gũthamaka.
english: In the second year of Pekah the son of Remaliah king of Israel, Jotham the son of Uzziah king of Judah began to reign.

kikuyu: Matũũra macio na tũtũũra twamo nĩmo maarĩ igai rĩa mũhĩrĩga wa Isakaru, kũringana na ũrĩa mbarĩ ciao ciatariĩ. Igai rĩa Asheri
english: This is the inheritance of the tribe of the children of Issachar according to their families, the cities with their villages.

kikuyu: Ndekereria, nĩguo ndĩmaniine na tharie rĩĩtwa rĩao gũkũ thĩ mũhuro wa riũa. Thuutha wa ũguo njooke ngũtue rũrĩrĩ rũrĩ hinya na rũrĩ na andũ aingĩ kũmakĩra.”
english: Leave me alone, that I may destroy them, and blot out their name from under the sky; and I will make of you a nation mightier and greater than they.”

kikuyu: Mũũmbi wa igũrũ na thĩ, na iria, na kĩrĩa gĩothe gĩtũũraga thĩinĩ wacio: ũcio nĩwe Jehova, ũrĩa ũtũũraga arĩ mwĩhokeku nginya tene.
english: who made heaven and earth, the sea, and all that is in them; who keeps truth forever;

kikuyu: Nake Pilato akĩmacookeria atĩrĩ, “Oyai arangĩri, mũthiĩ mũrangĩre mbĩrĩra ĩyo wega o ta ũrĩa mũngĩhota.”
english: Pilate said to them, “You have a guard. Go, make it as secure as you can.”

kikuyu: Ashuri ndangĩhota gũtũhonokia; tũtikũhaica mbarathi cia ita. Tũtirĩ hĩndĩ ĩngĩ tũgaacooka gwĩta kĩrĩa tũthondekete na moko maitũ ‘ngai ciitũ,’ nĩgũkorwo thĩinĩ waku ciana cia ngoriai nĩciiguagĩrwo tha.”
english: Assyria can’t save us. We won’t ride on horses; neither will we say any more to the work of our hands, ‘Our gods!’ for in you the fatherless finds mercy.”

kikuyu: Arahũka, arahũka! Wee Jerusalemu ũkĩra, o wee ũnyuĩrĩire gĩkombe kĩa mangʼũrĩ ma Jehova, ũnengereirio kuuma guoko-inĩ gwake, o wee ũnyuĩrĩire kuuma mbakũri ĩrĩa ĩtũmaga andũ matũgũũge na ũkamĩinĩkĩrĩria.
english: Awake, awake! Stand up, Jerusalem, you who have drunk from Yahweh’s hand the cup of his wrath. You have drunken the bowl of the cup of staggering, and drained it.

kikuyu: Tigai gũtuanagĩra ciira na ũrĩa mũndũ atariĩ, tuanagĩrai ciira na kĩhooto.” Jesũ nĩwe Kristũ?
english: Don’t judge according to appearance, but judge righteous judgment.”

kikuyu: No kuuma o kĩambĩrĩria, Ngai ‘ombire mũndũ mũrũme na mũndũ-wa-nja’.
english: But from the beginning of the creation, God made them male and female.*

kikuyu: Akorwo ti ũguo-rĩ, mwathiĩte kuona kĩ? Hihi nĩ mũndũ wehumbĩte nguo njega? Aca, arĩa mehumbaga nguo njega makoragwo thĩinĩ wa nyũmba cia ũthamaki.
english: But what did you go out to see? A man in soft clothing? Behold, those who wear soft clothing are in kings’ houses.

kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo nĩũgatũma mahũndũke moore rĩrĩa makoona ũgeetete ũta waku ũmarathe.
english: For you will make them turn their back, when you aim drawn bows at their face.

kikuyu: a rĩrĩ, Joramu mũrũ wa Ahabu agĩtuĩka mũthamaki wa Isiraeli kũu Samaria mwaka-inĩ wa ikũmi na ĩnana wa Jehoshafatu, mũthamaki wa Juda, nake agĩathana mĩaka ikũmi na ĩĩrĩ.
english: ow Jehoram the son of Ahab began to reign over Israel in Samaria in the eighteenth year of Jehoshaphat king of Judah, and reigned twelve years.

kikuyu: Njiguĩra tha, Wee Mwathani, nĩgũkorwo ngũkayagĩra mũthenya wothe.
english: Be merciful to me, Lord, for I call to you all day long.

kikuyu: Jesũ nĩaiguire atĩ nĩmaikĩtie mũndũ ũcio nja, na rĩrĩa aamuonire akĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Wee nĩwĩtĩkĩtie Mũrũ wa Mũndũ?”
english: Jesus heard that they had thrown him out, and finding him, he said, “Do you believe in the Son of God?”

kikuyu: Nao makĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “O nawe kaĩ uumĩte Galili? Tuĩria ũhoro, na nĩũkũmenya atĩ mũnabii ndangiuma Galili.” (Maandĩko mamwe ma tene matirĩ gacunjĩ gaka kuuma Johana 7:
english: Then the Jews said to him, “Now we know that you have a demon. Abraham died, as did the prophets; and you say, ‘If a man keeps my word, he will never taste of death.’

kikuyu: Kiugo kĩa Jehova nĩgĩakinyĩire Solomoni, akĩĩrwo atĩrĩ:
english: Yahweh’s word came to Solomon, saying,

kikuyu: ake Zofaru ũrĩa Mũnaamathi agĩcookia, akĩũria atĩrĩ:
english: hen Zophar, the Naamathite, answered,

kikuyu: Na nĩngũtũma kũgĩe ũthũ gatagatĩ gaku na mũndũ-wa-nja ũyũ, na gatagatĩ ka rũciaro rwaku na rwake; nake nĩagakũhehenja mũtwe, nawe nĩũkamũgũtha ndiira ya kũgũrũ gwake.”
english: I will put hostility between you and the woman, and between your offspring and her offspring. He will bruise your head, and you will bruise his heel.”

kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, thĩinĩ wa nyũmba ya ũthamaki kũu gĩikaro kĩrĩa kĩirigĩre gĩa Shushani, nĩ kwarĩ na Mũyahudi wa mũhĩrĩga wa Benjamini wetagwo Moridekai mũrũ wa Jairũ, mũrũ wa Shimei, mũrũ wa Kishu,
english: There was a certain Jew in the citadel of Susa whose name was Mordecai, the son of Jair, the son of Shimei, the son of Kish, a Benjamite,

kikuyu: mũndũ ũcio nĩakarĩha ithe wa mũirĩtu ũcio cekeri mĩrongo ĩtano cia betha.‡ Mũndũ ũcio no nginya ahikie mũirĩtu ũcio, nĩgũkorwo nĩamũthũkĩtie. Ndakanamũte matukũ mothe marĩa egũtũũra muoyo.
english: then the man who lay with her shall give to the lady’s father fifty shekels§ of silver. She shall be his wife, because he has humbled her. He may not put her away all his days.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo Jehova akĩĩra Musa atĩrĩ, “Ngũmuurĩria mĩgate kuuma igũrũ ta mbura. Andũ mariumaga nja o mũthenya makongania mĩgate ya kũmaigana o mũthenya ũcio. Ngũmageria na njĩra ĩyo nyone kana nĩmekũrũmĩrĩra ũrutani wakwa.
english: Then Yahweh said to Moses, “Behold, I will rain bread from the sky for you, and the people shall go out and gather a day’s portion every day, that I may test them, whether they will walk in my law or not.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo Maandĩko moigĩte atĩrĩ: “Nĩnjigĩte ihiga Zayuni, ihiga ithuure rĩa koine na rĩa goro, na mũndũ ũrĩa ũmwĩhokete ndagaaconorithio.”
english: Because it is contained in Scripture, “Behold,† I lay in Zion a chief cornerstone, chosen and precious. He who believes in him will not be disappointed.”*

kikuyu: ake Bilidadi ũrĩa Mũshuhi agĩcookia atĩrĩ:
english: hen Bildad the Shuhite answered,

kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo inyuothe arĩa mwabatithirio thĩinĩ wa Kristũ nĩmwĩhumbĩte Kristũ.
english: For as many of you as were baptized into Christ have put on Christ.

kikuyu: Nake Naomi akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Ta rora, mũtumia wa mũrũ wa nyina na mũthuuriguo nĩahũndũka acooke kũrĩ andũ ao, o na kũrĩ ngai ciake. Cooka mũthiĩ nake.”
english: She said, “Behold,‡ your sister-in-law has gone back to her people and to her god. Follow your sister-in-law.”

kikuyu: Thũ ciake nĩngacihumba thoni, no thũmbĩ ya ũnene ĩrĩa ĩrĩ mũtwe wake nĩĩgakenga na riiri.”
english: I will clothe his enemies with shame, but on himself, his crown will shine.”

kikuyu: Henũkia rũheni ũhurunje thũ ciaku; ikia mĩguĩ yaku ũcingatithie.
english: Throw out lightning, and scatter them. Send out your arrows, and rout them.

kikuyu: Tigai kuuga atĩrĩ, ‘Nĩtũgĩĩte na ũũgĩ; rekei Mũrungu amũkararie, no ti mũndũ.’
english: Beware lest you say, ‘We have found wisdom. God may refute him, not man;’

kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, mũndũ angĩkorwo ndekwenda kũhikia mũtumia wa mũrũ wa nyina, mũtumia ũcio nĩagathiĩ kũrĩ athuuri hau kĩhingo-inĩ gĩa itũũra, ameere atĩrĩ, “Mũrũ wa nyina na mũthuuri wakwa nĩaregete gũtũũria rĩĩtwa rĩa mũrũ wa nyina gũkũ Isiraeli. Nĩaregete kũhingia bata ũrĩa mũrũ wa nyina na mũthuuri wa mũtumia agĩrĩirwo nĩ kũhingia.”
english: If the man doesn’t want to take his brother’s wife, then his brother’s wife shall go up to the gate to the elders, and say, “My husband’s brother refuses to raise up to his brother a name in Israel. He will not perform the duty of a husband’s brother to me.”

kikuyu: Atĩrĩrĩ, reke mũthamaki mwathi wakwa athikĩrĩrie ũhoro wa ndungata yake. Angĩkorwo Jehova nĩwe ũkũringĩrĩirie ũnjũkĩrĩre-rĩ, aroĩtĩkĩra igongona. No angĩkorwo nĩ andũ mekĩte ũguo-rĩ, marogwatwo nĩ kĩrumi marĩ mbere ya Jehova! Nĩmanyingatĩte, njeherere gĩcunjĩ gĩakwa harĩ igai rĩa Jehova, na makoiga atĩrĩ, ‘Thiĩ ũgatungatĩre ngai ingĩ.’
english: Now therefore, please let my lord the king hear the words of his servant. If it is so that Yahweh has stirred you up against me, let him accept an offering. But if it is the children of men, they are cursed

kikuyu: o ta ũrĩa niĩ ngeragia gũkenia andũ othe na njĩra o yothe. Nĩgũkorwo ndicaragia ũndũ wa kwĩguna niĩ mwene, no njaragia ũrĩa ingĩguna andũ aingĩ, nĩguo mahonoke.
english: even as I also please all men in all things, not seeking my own profit, but the profit of the many, that they may be saved.

kikuyu: Jesũ akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Reke arĩa akuũ mathikage akuũ ao ene, no wee thiĩ ũkahunjie ũhoro wa ũthamaki wa Ngai.”
english: But Jesus said to him, “Leave the dead to bury their own dead, but you go and announce God’s Kingdom.”

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai angĩrora kĩndũ kĩu na akore ũgumu ũcio ũnyihanyiihĩte thuutha wa kĩndũ kĩu gũthambio-rĩ, nĩagatembũra nguo ĩyo eherie hau he na ũgumu kuuma nguo-inĩ ĩyo, kana rũũa-inĩ, kana ndigi-inĩ icio njogothe, kana taama-inĩ ũcio mũtume.
english: If the priest looks, and behold, the plague has faded after it is washed, then he shall tear it out of the garment, or out of the skin, or out of the warp, or out of the woof;

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio magĩtũma ithe wao anyue ndibei ũtukũ ũcio o naguo, nake mũirĩtu ũrĩa warĩ mũnini agĩtoonya agĩkoma nake. O rĩngĩ Loti ndaamenyire rĩrĩa mũirĩtu aakomire kana rĩrĩa ookĩrire.
english: They made their father drink wine that night also. The younger went and lay with him. He didn’t know when she lay down, nor when she got up.

kikuyu: Githĩ mũũmbi wa nyũngũ ndarĩ na kĩhooto gĩa kũhũthĩra rĩũmba o ũrĩa angĩenda; kĩenyũ kĩmwe kĩa rĩũmba rĩu athondeke indo imwe cia kũhũthĩrwo na njĩra ya gũtĩĩka na ingĩ nĩ ũndũ wa mahũthĩro ma ndũire?
english: Or hasn’t the potter a right over the clay, from the same lump to make one part a vessel for honor, and another for dishonor?

kikuyu: Atĩrĩrĩ, ti-itherũ Ngai nĩakaarĩria andũ aya na tũnua twa andũ maaragia na mwarĩrie wa andũ a kũngĩ na thiomi ngʼeni,
english: But he will speak to this nation with stammering lips and in another language,

kikuyu: “Nĩndahĩtũkia watho, atĩ kũrĩa guothe ũthamaki ũyũ wakwa wakinya-rĩ, andũ no nginya metigagĩre na matĩĩage Ngai wa Danieli. “Nĩgũkorwo nĩwe Ngai ũrĩa ũrĩ muoyo, na atũũraga nginya tene; ũthamaki wake ndũrĩ hĩndĩ ũkaaniinwo o na rĩ, naguo wathani wake ndũrĩ hĩndĩ ũgaathira.
english: “I make a decree that in all the dominion of my kingdom men tremble and fear before the God of Daniel. “For he is the living God, and steadfast forever. His kingdom is that which will not be destroyed. His dominion will be even to the end.

kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, hĩndĩ ĩrĩa Jehova aarĩirie Musa arĩ kũu bũrũri wa Misiri-rĩ,
english: On the day when Yahweh spoke to Moses in the land of Egypt,

kikuyu: “ ‘Ndũkanakome na tataguo, ũcio nĩ mũndũ wa nyũmba ĩmwe na nyũkwa.
english: “ ‘You shall not uncover the nakedness of your mother’s sister, for she is your mother’s near kinswoman.

kikuyu: 872 Isaia 43:13 43 Mũhonokia wa Isiraeli 1 No rĩrĩ, wee Jakubu, Jehova ũrĩa wakũũmbire, o we ũcio watũmire ũgĩe ho, wee Isiraeli, ekuuga atĩrĩ: “Tiga gwĩtigĩra, nĩgũkorwo nĩ niĩ ngũkũũrĩte; Ngwĩtĩte na rĩĩtwa rĩaku; wee ũrĩ wakwa.
english: ut now Yahweh who created you, Jacob, and he who formed you, Israel, says: “Don’t be afraid, for I have redeemed you. I have called you by your name. You are mine.

kikuyu: nga nĩtũraambĩrĩria gũcooka kwĩgaathĩrĩria? Kana hihi nĩtũbataire kũheo marũa ma gũtũ- gaathĩrĩria kũrĩ inyuĩ kana moimĩte kũrĩ inyuĩ ta andũ arĩa angĩ?
english: re we beginning again to commend ourselves? Or do we need, as do some, letters of commendation to you or from you?

kikuyu: Angĩkorwo no nginya ndĩĩrahe, ngwĩrahĩra maũndũ marĩa monanagia ũrĩa itarĩ na hinya.
english: If I must boast, I will boast of the things that concern my weakness.

kikuyu: Na hĩndĩ ĩyo akĩambĩrĩria kũhunjia thunagogi-inĩ atĩ Jesũ nĩ Mũrũ wa Ngai.
english: Immediately in the synagogues he proclaimed the Christ, that he is the Son of God.

kikuyu: Ũkai mũthikĩrĩrie inyuothe arĩa mwĩtigĩrĩte Ngai; rekei ndĩmũhe ũhoro wa ũrĩa anjĩkĩire.
english: Come and hear, all you who fear God. I will declare what he has done for my soul.

kikuyu: Mũtikanahooe ngai ĩngĩ o na ĩrĩkũ, nĩgũkorwo Jehova, ũrĩa rĩĩtwa rĩake arĩ Mũigua Ũiru, nĩ Ngai ũrĩ ũiru.
english: for you shall worship no other god; for Yahweh, whose name is Jealous, is a jealous God.

kikuyu: Ndiroiga ũguo nĩ ũndũ nĩmbataire, nĩgũkorwo nĩndĩrutĩte kũiganĩra o ũrĩa kũngĩkorwo kũhaana.
english: Not that I speak because of lack, for I have learned in whatever state I am, to be content in it.

kikuyu: Ningĩ Abisalomu akamwĩra atĩrĩ, “Atĩrĩrĩ mateta maku nĩ ma kĩhooto na nĩ magĩrĩire, no gũtirĩ mũndũ ũthuurĩtwo wa kũrũgamĩrĩra mũthamaki ũngĩgũthikĩrĩria.”
english: Absalom said to him, “Behold, your matters are good and right; but there is no man deputized by the king to hear you.”

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio rĩĩtwa rĩa Jehova nĩrĩkoimbũrwo kũu Zayuni, na nĩakagoocwo kũu Jerusalemu
english: that men may declare Yahweh’s name in Zion, and his praise in Jerusalem,

kikuyu: Wee mwathi wakwa-rĩ, kaĩ ũtarĩ waigua ũrĩa ndeekire rĩrĩa Jezebeli ooragaga anabii a Jehova, ũrĩa ndaahithire anabii a Jehova igana rĩmwe ngurunga-inĩ igĩrĩ, o ĩmwe anabii mĩrongo ĩtano, na ngamaheaga irio na maaĩ?
english: Wasn’t it told my lord what I did when Jezebel killed Yahweh’s prophets, how I hid one hundred men of Yahweh’s prophets with fifty to a cave, and fed them with bread and water?

kikuyu: O hĩndĩ ĩyo Joshua agĩthiĩ akĩniina andũ a Anaki kuuma bũrũri ũrĩa ũrĩ irĩma: kuuma Hebironi, na Debiri na Anabu, na kuuma bũrũri wothe ũrĩa wa irĩma wa Juda, na kuuma bũrũri wothe ũrĩa wa irĩma wa Isiraeli. Joshua akĩmaniina biũ hamwe na matũũra mao.
english: Joshua came at that time, and cut off the Anakim from the hill country, from Hebron, from Debir, from Anab, and from all the hill country of Judah, and from all the hill country of Israel. Joshua utterly destroyed them with their cities.

kikuyu: We nĩwe Mũrungu ũrĩa ũndĩhagĩria, agatooria ndũrĩrĩ, agaciiga rungu rwakwa,
english: even the God who executes vengeance for me, and subdues peoples under me.

kikuyu: Mũthenya wa mũgwanja-rĩ, thambiai nguo cianyu na inyuĩ nĩ mũgaathirwo nĩ thaahu. Thuutha ũcio no mũtoonye kambĩ.” Kũgayania Indo iria Ndahe
english: You shall wash your clothes on the seventh day, and you shall be clean. Afterward you shall come into the camp.”

kikuyu: ee Ngai, nĩtwagũcookeria ngaatho, twagũcookeria ngaatho, nĩgũkorwo Rĩĩtwa rĩaku rĩrĩ hakuhĩ; andũ maheanaga ũhoro wa ciĩko ciaku cia magegania.
english: e give thanks to you, God. We give thanks, for your Name is near. Men tell about your wondrous works.

kikuyu: Ningĩ Jehova akiuga atĩrĩ, “O ta ũguo ndungata yakwa Isaia athiĩte njaga na arĩ magũrũ matheri mĩaka ĩtatũ, arĩ rũũri na kĩmenyithia kĩa maũndũ mooru marĩa magaakora bũrũri wa Misiri na wa
english: Yahweh said, “As my servant Isaiah has walked naked and barefoot three years for a sign and a wonder concerning Egypt and concerning Ethiopia,

kikuyu: Mũhaka wao woimire Helefu o kũu mũtĩ-inĩ ũrĩa mũnene thĩinĩ wa Zaananimu, ũkĩhĩtũkĩra Adami-Nekebu na Jabineeli, ũgĩkinya Lakumu, na ũkĩrĩkĩrĩra Jorodani.
english: Their border was from Heleph, from the oak in Zaanannim, Adami-nekeb, and Jabneel, to Lakkum. It ended at the Jordan.

kikuyu: na Kibizaimu, na Bethi-Horoni hamwe na ithaka ciamo cia ũrĩithio; mothe maarĩ matũũra mana.
english: Kibzaim with its pasture lands, and Beth Horon with its pasture lands: four cities.

kikuyu: Na aarĩkia kwaria o ro ũguo-rĩ, ariũ a mũthamaki magĩũka makĩrĩraga na mũgambo mũnene. O nake mũthamaki na ndungata ciake ciothe makĩrĩra marĩ na ruo rũnene.
english: As soon as he had finished speaking, behold, the king’s sons came, and lifted up their voices and wept. The king also and all his servants wept bitterly.

kikuyu: Kuuma njiaro-inĩ cia Asheri: Arũme othe arĩa maakinyĩtie mĩaka mĩrongo ĩĩrĩ na makĩria, arĩa mangĩahotire gũthiĩ ita-inĩ, makĩandĩkwo o mũndũ o mũndũ na rĩĩtwa rĩake, kũringana na maandĩko ma mbarĩ ciao na nyũmba ciao.
english: Of the children of Asher, their generations, by their families, by their fathers’ houses, according to the number of the names, from twenty years old and upward, all who were able to go out to war:

kikuyu: Nĩndathaithire Mwathani mahinda matatũ anjehererie mũigua ũcio.
english: Concerning this thing, I begged the Lord three times that it might depart from me.

kikuyu: Akĩmataara na ciugo ingĩ nyingĩ, akĩmakaanagia; na akĩmathaitha, akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Mwĩhonokiei kuuma kũrĩ rũciaro rũrũ ruogomu.”
english: With many other words he testified and exhorted them, saying, “Save yourselves from this crooked generation!”

kikuyu: Solomoni aathamakĩire Isiraeli rĩothe arĩ Jerusalemu mĩaka mĩrongo ĩna.
english: Solomon reigned in Jerusalem over all Israel forty years.

kikuyu: Anga mũrĩ na maitho na mũtionaga, na mũrĩ na matũ na mũtiiguaga? Kaĩ mũtararirikana?
english: Having eyes, don’t you see? Having ears, don’t you hear? Don’t you remember?

kikuyu: Enjaga mungu ũgatũrĩkia rwaro rwa ihiga; maitho make makĩonaga igĩĩna ciaruo ciothe.
english: He cuts out channels among the rocks. His eye sees every precious thing.

kikuyu: Mũthoni-we wa Musa akĩmũcookeria, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Ũrĩa ũreka ti wega.
english: Moses’ father-in-law said to him, “The thing that you do is not good.

kikuyu: “ ‘Mũndũ o wothe ũngĩka ũndũ o na ũmwe wa maũndũ macio marĩ magigi, andũ ta acio no nginya maingatwo kuuma kũrĩ andũ ao.
english: “ ‘For whoever shall do any of these abominations, even the souls that do them shall be cut off from among their people.

kikuyu: “Ici nĩcio igaatuĩka njĩra cia kuuma itũũra rĩu inene: Kwambĩrĩria mwena wa gathigathini, ũrĩa ũrĩ na ũraihu wa mĩkono 4,500,
english: “These are the exits of the city: On the north side four thousand five hundred reeds by measure;

kikuyu: Ningĩ ngĩcooka ngĩigua ũmwe wa andũ arĩa atheru akĩaria, nake ũngĩ mũtheru akĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Kĩoneki gĩkĩ-rĩ, gĩgũikara ihinda rĩigana atĩa gĩgĩkahingio: kĩoneki kĩu gĩkoniĩ magongona ma o mũthenya, na ũremi ũcio wa kũrehe ihooru, na kũneanwo gwa gĩikaro kĩrĩa gĩtheru, na kũrangĩrĩrio thĩ kwa mbũtũ?”
english: Then I heard a holy one speaking; and another holy one said to that certain one who spoke, “How long will the vision about the continual burnt offering, and the disobedience that makes desolate, to give both the sanctuary and the army to be trodden under foot be?”

kikuyu: no nĩyo ndũmĩrĩri nditũ ĩkoniĩ Nineve. Rĩĩrĩ nĩrĩo ibuku rĩa kĩoneki kĩa Nahumu, ũrĩa Mũelikoshi. Jehova Kũrakarĩra Nineve
english: revelation about Nineveh. The book of the vision of Nahum the Elkoshite.

kikuyu: Ningĩ Firaũni agĩĩta Musa, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Thiĩi mũkahooe Jehova. O na atumia anyu na ciana no mathiĩ na inyuĩ; no rĩrĩ, mũtige ndũũru cianyu cia mbũri na cia ngʼombe.”
english: Pharaoh called to Moses, and said, “Go, serve Yahweh. Only let your flocks and your herds stay behind. Let your little ones also go with you.”

kikuyu: Narĩrĩamaikũrũkagakuumakĩrĩma-inĩ, Jesũakĩmaathamatikeeremũndũonaũrĩkũũrĩamonete, nginya rĩrĩa Mũrũ wa Mũndũ agaakorwo ariũkĩte akoima kũrĩ arĩa akuũ.
english: As they were coming down from the mountain, he commanded them that they should tell no one what things they had seen, until after the Son of Man had risen from the dead.

kikuyu: tĩrĩrĩ inyuĩ Agalatia aya akĩĩgu! Mũrogetwo nũũ? O inyuĩ muonirio wega Jesũ Kristũ arĩ mwambe mũtĩ igũrũ.
english: oolish Galatians, who has bewitched you not to obey the truth, before whose eyes Jesus Christ was openly portrayed among you as crucified?

kikuyu: Magĩita thakame ĩteehĩtie, thakame ya aanake na airĩtu ao, arĩa maarutire marĩ magongona kũrĩ mĩhianano ya Kaanani, naguo bũrũri ũgĩthaahio nĩ thakame yao.
english: They shed innocent blood, even the blood of their sons and of their daughters, whom they sacrificed to the idols of Canaan. The land was polluted with blood.

kikuyu: Mĩthingi yayo yahandirwo igũrũ rĩa kĩĩ, kana nũũ wahaandire ihiga rĩayo rĩa koine,
english: What were its foundations fastened on? Or who laid its cornerstone,

kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, Wee Jehova, ndũkandaihĩrĩrie mũno; Wee Hinya wakwa, hiũha ũũke ũndeithie.
english: But don’t be far off, Yahweh. You are my help. Hurry to help me!

kikuyu: Matukũ-inĩ macio-rĩ, Hezekia akĩrwara hakuhĩ akue. Akĩhooya Jehova, nake Jehova akĩmũigua na akĩmuonia kĩmenyithia kĩa mwanya.
english: In those days Hezekiah was terminally ill, and he prayed to Yahweh; and he spoke to him, and gave him a sign.

kikuyu: o na angĩhĩngwo, ndangĩgũa, nĩgũkorwo Jehova amũnyiitagĩrĩra na guoko gwake.
english: Though he stumble, he shall not fall, for Yahweh holds him up with his hand.

kikuyu: Mwĩtĩĩo wa Isiraeli nĩũrutaga ũira wa kũmũũkĩrĩra, no o na kũrĩ ũguo-rĩ, we ndacookagĩrĩra Jehova Ngai wake, kana akamũrongooria.
english: The pride of Israel testifies to his face; yet they haven’t returned to Yahweh their God, nor sought him, for all this.

kikuyu: tondũ mũthenya ũcio nĩmũkahoroherio, nĩguo mũtherio. Hĩndĩ ĩyo nĩmũgatherio kuuma kũrĩ mehia manyu mothe mũrĩ hau mbere ya Jehova.
english: for on this day shall atonement be made for you, to cleanse you. You shall be clean from all your sins before Yahweh.

kikuyu: ĩmwe ĩrĩ iruta rĩa kũhoroherio mehia na ĩyo ĩngĩ ĩrĩ iruta rĩa njino, o hamwe na iruta rĩa mũtu. Ũguo nĩguo mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai akaahoroheria mũndũ ũcio ũratherio hau mbere ya Jehova.”
english: of the kind he is able to afford, the one for a sin offering, and the other for a burnt offering, with the meal offering. The priest shall make atonement for him who is to be cleansed before Yahweh.”

kikuyu: Mũtĩ wa mũgwanja wagwĩrĩire mũhĩrĩga wa Dani, kũringana na ũrĩa mbarĩ ciao ciatariĩ.
english: The seventh lot came out for the tribe of the children of Dan according to their families.

kikuyu: “No ngũkwĩra atĩrĩ, ũhoro-inĩ ũyũ wee ndũrĩ na kĩhooto, nĩgũkorwo Ngai nĩ mũnene gũkĩra mũndũ.
english: “Behold, I will answer you. In this you are not just,

kikuyu: Naarĩ rũgendo-inĩ aakuhĩrĩria Dameski-rĩ, o rĩmwe ũtheri ũkiuma na igũrũ, ũkĩmwarĩra mĩena yothe.
english: As he traveled, he got close to Damascus, and suddenly a light from the sky shone around him.

kikuyu: Gĩkĩ nĩkĩo kĩhingo kĩa Jehova kĩrĩa andũ arĩa athingu mangĩtoonyera.
english: This is the gate of Yahweh; the righteous will enter into it.

kikuyu: O na kũrĩ ũguo Ayahudi matiigana gwĩtĩkia ũhoro wa mũndũ ũcio atĩ aarĩ mũtumumu na atĩ nĩacookete kuona, nginya rĩrĩa maatũmanĩire aciari ake.
english: The Jews therefore didn’t believe concerning him, that he had been blind and had received his sight, until they called the parents of him who had received his sight,

kikuyu: Nake Daudi akĩrakara tondũ mangʼũrĩ ma Jehova nĩmookĩrĩire Uza, na nginya ũmũthĩ handũ hau hetagwo Perezu-Uza.
english: David was displeased because Yahweh had broken out against Uzzah; and he called that place Perez Uzzah† to this day.

kikuyu: Ndarigiicĩirio nĩ ndũrĩrĩ ciothe, no tondũ wa kwĩhoka rĩĩtwa rĩa Jehova, ngĩciananga.
english: All the nations surrounded me, but in Yahweh’s name I cut them off.

kikuyu: “Inyuĩ andũ a nyũmba ya Isiraeli, githĩ to njĩke na inyuĩ o ta ũrĩa mũũmbi ũyũ wa nyũngũ ekaga?” Ũguo nĩguo Jehova ekuuga. “O ta rĩũmba rĩrĩ guoko-inĩ kwa mũũmbi-rĩ, ũguo nĩguo mũtariĩ mũrĩ guoko-inĩ gwakwa, inyuĩ andũ aya a nyũmba ya Isiraeli.
english: “House of Israel, can’t I do with you as this potter?” says Yahweh. “Behold, as the clay in the potter’s hand, so are you in my hand, house of Israel.

kikuyu: Andũ othe nĩmagĩte meciiria na makaaga ũũgĩ; nake mũturi wa thahabu o wothe nĩaconorithĩtio nĩ mĩhianano ĩyo yake. Nĩ ũndũ mĩhianano ĩyo yake ya gwĩturĩra nĩ ya maheeni; ndĩrĩ mĩhũmũ thĩinĩ wayo.
english: Every man has become brutish and without knowledge. Every goldsmith is disappointed by his engraved image; for his molten image is falsehood, and there is no breath in them.

kikuyu: na ũtũũre ũrũmĩtie wĩtĩkio, na ũrĩ na thamiri ĩtarĩ na ũcuuke. Andũ angĩ nĩmareganĩte na kũrũmĩrĩra thamiri ciao, nanĩũndũũciomagathũkiawĩtĩkiowao.
english: holding faith and a good conscience, which some having thrust away made a shipwreck concerning the faith,

kikuyu: Gũtirĩ ũndũ aameeraga atekwaria na ngerekano. No rĩrĩa maarĩ na arutwo ake oiki, nĩamataragĩria maũndũ mothe. Jesũ Kũhooreria Makũmbĩ ma Maaĩ
english: Without a parable he didn’t speak to them; but privately to his own disciples he explained everything.

kikuyu: Balaki akĩũria Balamu atĩrĩ, “Wanjĩka atĩa? Ngũrehire ũnumĩre thũ ciakwa, no ndũrĩ ũreka ũndũ o tiga kũmarathima!”
english: Balak said to Balaam, “What have you done to me? I took you to curse my enemies, and behold, you have blessed them altogether.”

kikuyu: Magaatambũrũkĩria moko mao kuo, o ta ũrĩa mũthambĩri atambũrũkagia moko agĩĩthambĩra. Ngai nĩwe ũkaaharũrũkia mwĩtĩĩo wao, o na marĩ na wara wa wĩra ũrĩa marutaga na moko mao.
english: He will spread out his hands in the middle of it, like one who swims spreads out hands to swim, but his pride will be humbled together with the craft of his hands.

kikuyu: Jehova arokũrĩha nĩ ũndũ wa ũguo wĩkĩte. Jehova Ngai wa Isiraeli, o ũrĩa wee ũũkĩte kwĩhitha mathagu-inĩ make, arokũrĩha na irĩhi inene.”
english: May Yahweh repay your work, and a full reward be given to you from Yahweh, the God of Israel, under whose wings you have come to take refuge.”

kikuyu: Wee Jehova, nĩ ũndũ wa rĩĩtwa rĩaku, tũũria muoyo wakwa; nĩ ũndũ wa ũthingu waku-rĩ, nduta kuuma mĩnyamaro-inĩ.
english: Revive me, Yahweh, for your name’s sake. In your righteousness, bring my soul out of trouble.

kikuyu: Ningĩ atwari a marikabu makĩĩrana atĩrĩ, “Ũkai tũcuuke mĩtĩ nĩguo tũmenye nũũ ũrehete mũtino ũyũ.” Magĩcuuka mĩtĩ naguo mũtĩ ũkĩgwĩra Jona.
english: They all said to each other, “Come! Let’s cast lots, that we may know who is responsible for this evil that is on us.” So they cast lots, and the lot fell on Jonah.

kikuyu: Na rĩu ũranjĩĩra thiĩ kũrĩ mwathi wakwa ngamwĩre atĩrĩ, ‘Elija arĩ gũkũ.’
english: Now you say, ‘Go, tell your lord, “Behold, Elijah is here.” ’

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Daudi na andũ ake makĩrooka gũũkĩra rũciinĩ tene nĩguo macooke bũrũri-inĩ wa Afilisti, nao Afilisti makĩambata magĩthiĩ Jezireeli.
english: So David rose up early, he and his men, to depart in the morning, to return into the land of the Philistines; and the Philistines went up to Jezreel.

kikuyu: “Angĩkorwo nĩwĩtuĩte mũndũ mũkĩĩgu, na ũgetũũgĩria, kana ũkorwo nĩũthugundĩte gwĩka ũũru-rĩ, humbĩra kanua gaku na rũhĩ!
english: “If you have done foolishly in lifting up yourself, or if you have thought evil, put your hand over your mouth.

kikuyu: Kuuma gĩkabũ- inĩ kĩa mĩgate ĩtarĩ mĩĩkĩre ndawa ya kũimbia kĩrĩa kĩrĩ mbere ya Jehova, oya mũgate ũmwe na keki ĩthondeketwo na maguta, na woe kamũgate kahũthũ.
english: and one loaf of bread, one cake of oiled bread, and one wafer out of the basket of unleavened bread that is before Yahweh.

kikuyu: Acio othe maarĩ a njiaro cia Obedi-Edomu; o hamwe na ariũ ao, na andũ a nyũmba yao maarĩ andũ ehoti, na maarĩ na hinya wa kũruta wĩra. Njiaro cia Obedi-Edomu othe maarĩ andũ 62.
english: All these were of the sons of Obed-Edom with their sons and their brothers, able men in strength for the service: sixty-two of Obed-Edom.

kikuyu: O mũndũ aagĩrĩirwo nĩkũheana kĩrĩa atuĩte na ngoro yake nĩekũheana, na ndakaheane atekwenda kana taarĩ kũringĩrĩrio, nĩgũkorwo Ngai endaga mũndũ ũrĩa ũheanaga akenete.
english: Let each man give according as he has determined in his heart, not grudgingly or under compulsion, for God loves a cheerful giver.

kikuyu: Ada agĩciarĩra Esaũ Elifazu, na Basemathu agĩciara Reueli,
english: Adah bore to Esau Eliphaz. Basemath bore Reuel.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ-rĩ, kuona atĩ Ngai ndaigana kũiguĩra araika arĩa mehirie tha, no nĩkũmaikia aamaikirie Jehanamu, akĩmohithia marima-inĩ ma nduma maikarage kuo metereire ituĩro-rĩ,
english: For if God didn’t spare angels when they sinned, but cast them down to Tartarus,‡ and committed them to pits of darkness to be reserved for judgment;

kikuyu: Ningĩ ũthondeke ngathĩka mĩrongo ĩtano cia thahabu, na ũcihũthĩre na kuohithania itambaya icio cia gũcuurio nĩguo hema ĩyo nyamũre ĩtuĩke kĩndũ kĩmwe.
english: You shall make fifty clasps of gold, and couple the curtains to one another with the clasps. The tabernacle shall be a unit.

kikuyu: Saũlũ akĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Mwanake ũyũ, ũrĩ mũriũ waũ?” Nake Daudi akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Niĩ ndĩ mũrũ wa ndungata yaku Jesii wa Bethilehemu.”
english: Saul said to him, “Whose son are you, you young man?” David answered, “I am the son of your servant Jesse the Bethlehemite.”

kikuyu: “Nĩnjiguĩte gũteta kwa andũ a Isiraeli. Meere atĩrĩ, ‘Hwaĩ-inĩ mũrĩrĩĩaga nyama na rũciinĩ mũkarĩa mĩgate mũkahũũna. Hĩndĩ ĩyo nĩmũkamenya atĩ niĩ, nĩ niĩ Jehova Ngai wanyu.’ ”
english: “I have heard the murmurings of the children of Israel. Speak to them, saying, ‘At evening you shall eat meat, and in the morning you shall be filled with bread. Then you will know that I am Yahweh your God.’ ”

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, Mwathani Jehova ekuuga ũũ: Niĩ nĩngũũkĩrĩra Firaũni mũthamaki wa bũrũri wa Misiri. Nĩngũmuuna moko meerĩ, kũrĩa kũgima na kũrĩa kuunĩku, na ndũme rũhiũ rwa njora ruume guoko-inĩ gwake rũgwe thĩ.
english: Therefore the Lord Yahweh says: ‘Behold, I am against Pharaoh king of Egypt, and will break his arms, the strong arm, and that which was broken. I will cause the sword to fall out of his hand.

kikuyu: Agĩcooka akĩmatiga, akiuma itũũra rĩu inene, agĩthiĩ Bethania, akĩraara kuo. Mũkũyũ Kũhooha
english: He left them and went out of the city to Bethany, and camped there.

kikuyu: Indo icio ituĩke rwĩga rwa kũheagwo Harũni na ariũ ake nĩ andũ a Isiraeli hĩndĩ ciothe. Nĩrĩo iruta rĩrĩa andũ a Isiraeli marĩrutagĩra Jehova kuuma maruta-inĩ ma ũiguano.
english: It shall be for Aaron and his sons as their portion forever from the children of Israel; for it is a wave offering. It shall be a wave offering from the children of Israel of the sacrifices of their peace offerings, even their wave offering to Yahweh.

kikuyu: (Kuuma kũrĩ o gũkiuma andũ arĩa matũũraga ndwere-inĩ cia iria, o andũ na rũgongo rwao kũringana na mĩhĩrĩga yao, mabũrũri-inĩ mao, na o andũ na rũthiomi rwao.) Ariũ a Hamu
english: Of these were the islands of the nations divided in their lands, everyone after his language, after their families, in their nations.

kikuyu: Mũthenya ũcio wakinya- rĩ, nĩmũgakaya nĩ ũndũ wa mũthamaki ũcio mwĩthuurĩire, nake Jehova ndarĩ ũndũ akaamũcookeria mũthenya ũcio.”
english: You will cry out in that day because of your king whom you will have chosen for yourselves; and Yahweh will not answer you in that day.”

kikuyu: Jehova ekuuga atĩrĩ: ‘Angĩkorwo ndiigĩte kĩrĩkanĩro gĩakwa na mũthenya na ũtukũ, o na mawatho marĩa marũmu ma igũrũ na thĩ-rĩ,
english: Yahweh says: “If my covenant of day and night fails, if I have not appointed the ordinances of heaven and earth,

kikuyu: Saũlũ akĩgeria kũmũthecereria rũthingo-inĩ na itimũ rĩake, no Daudi akĩmweherera o rĩrĩa aaikagia itimũ rũthingo-inĩ. Ũtukũ ũcio Daudi akĩũra.
english: Saul sought to pin David to the wall with the spear, but he slipped away out of Saul’s presence; and he stuck the spear into the wall. David fled and escaped that night.

kikuyu: “Cookanĩrĩrai hamwe inyuothe na mũthikĩrĩrie: Nĩ mĩhianano ĩrĩkũ ya kũhooywo ĩrĩ yoimbũranĩra maũndũ macio? Mũndũ ũrĩa Jehova ethuurĩire manyiitanĩre nake nĩwe ũkaahingia muoroto wake wa gũũkĩrĩra bũrũri wa Babuloni; guoko gwake nĩkuo gũgookĩrĩra andũ a Babuloni.
english: “Assemble yourselves, all of you, and Who among them has declared these things? He whom Yahweh loves will do what he likes to Babylon, and his arm will be against the Chaldeans.

kikuyu: “Jehova ekuuga atĩrĩ: ‘Angĩkorwo inyuĩ mwahota gũthũkia kĩrĩkanĩro gĩakwa na mũthenya, na kĩrĩkanĩro gĩakwa na ũtukũ, nĩgeetha mũthenya na ũtukũ itigacooke kũgĩa hĩndĩ ĩrĩa yagĩrĩire-rĩ,
english: “Yahweh says: ‘If you can break my covenant of the day and my covenant of the night, so that there will not be day and night in their time,

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Abimeleku agĩtũmanĩra Isaaka, akĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Ti-itherũ ũyũ nĩ mũtumia waku! Nĩ kĩĩ gĩatũmire uuge atĩ, ‘Ũyũ nĩ mwarĩ wa baba’?” Isaaka akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Tondũ ndeciiririe ndahota kũũragwo nĩ ũndũ wake.”
english: Abimelech called Isaac, and said, “Behold, surely she is your wife. Why did you say, ‘She is my sister’?” Isaac said to him, “Because I said, ‘Lest I die because of her.’ ”

kikuyu: ũguo nĩ kuuga atĩ, njiaro ciao iria ciatigarĩte bũrũri-inĩ, iria andũ a Isiraeli mataaninĩte, acio nĩo maandĩkithirio nĩ Solomoni matuĩke ngombo ciake cia kũrutithio wĩra na hinya, na noguo gũtũire nginya ũmũthĩ.
english: of their children who were left after them in the land, whom the children of Israel didn’t consume—of them Solomon conscripted forced labor to this day.

kikuyu: “Mũndũ angiuha mũirĩtu gathirange ũtoorĩtio kũhikio, nake akome nake, no nginya arĩhe rũracio, nake mũirĩtu ũcio atuĩke mũtumia wake.
english: “If a man entices a virgin who isn’t pledged to be married, and lies with her, he shall surely pay a dowry for her to be his wife.

kikuyu: No Jonathani ndaiguĩte atĩ ithe nĩehĩtithĩtie andũ na mwĩhĩtwa; nĩ ũndũ ũcio agĩtambũrũkia rũthanju rũrĩa aanyiitĩte akĩrũtobokia igua-inĩ rĩa ũũkĩ. Akĩũcama na kanua, namo maitho make magĩthera.
english: But Jonathan didn’t hear when his father commanded the people with the oath. Therefore he put out the end of the rod that was in his hand and dipped it in the honeycomb, and put his hand to his mouth; and his eyes brightened.

kikuyu: Nake akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Aca, mũrũ wa baba! Tiga kũnyiita na hinya. Ũndũ ta ũyũ ndwagĩrĩire gwĩkwo Isiraeli! Tiga gwĩka ũndũ ũyũ wa waganu.
english: She answered him, “No, my brother, do not force me! For no such thing ought to be done in Israel. Don’t you do this folly!

kikuyu: Mwanake ũmwe agĩtengʼera akĩĩra Musa atĩrĩ, “Elidadi na Medadi nĩmararatha mohoro kũu kambĩ.”
english: A young man ran, and told Moses, and said, “Eldad and Medad are prophesying in the camp!”

kikuyu: Nĩmagire wĩhokeku na makĩaga wĩtĩkio o ta maithe mao, makĩaga kwĩhokeka o ta ũta mũgathũku ũrĩa ũtangĩĩhokwo.
english: but turned back, and dealt treacherously like their fathers. They were twisted like a deceitful bow.

kikuyu: tĩrĩrĩ, ceemaga ũgĩtoonya nyũmba ya Ngai. Gũkuhĩrĩria na kũigua ndeto ciake nĩ kwega gũkĩra igongona rĩrĩa rĩrutagwo nĩ andũ arĩa akĩĩgu, tondũ matimenyaga nĩ ũũru mareeka.
english: uard your steps when you go to God’s house; for to draw near to listen is better than to give the sacrifice of fools, for they don’t know that they do evil.

kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo marakara ma mũndũ matirehaga mũtũũrĩre wa ũthingu ũrĩa Ngai endaga.
english: for the anger of man doesn’t produce the righteousness of God.

kikuyu: “Ũyũ nĩwe mũndũ ũrĩa wagire gũtua Ngai kĩĩhitho gĩake kĩa hinya, no eehokire ũtonga wake mũingĩ, na akĩgĩa hinya nĩ ũndũ wa kũniina andũ arĩa angĩ!”
english: “Behold, this is the man who didn’t make God his strength, but trusted in the abundance of his riches, and strengthened himself in his wickedness.”

kikuyu: “Na rĩu-rĩ, Wee Ngai witũ, Mũrungu mũnene ũrĩ hinya na wa gwĩtigĩrwo, o wee ũhingagia kĩrĩkanĩro gĩaku kĩa wendo, ndũkareke moritũ maya moneke arĩ ũndũ mũnini maitho-inĩ maku, moritũ maya matũkorete, na magakora athamaki aitũ na atongoria aitũ, na magakora athĩnjĩri- Ngai na anabii aitũ, na magakora maithe maitũ na andũ aku othe, kuuma matukũ ma athamaki a Ashuri nginya ũmũthĩ.
english: Now therefore, our God, the great, the mighty, and the awesome God, who keeps covenant and loving kindness, don’t let all the travail seem little before you that has come on us, on our kings, on our princes, on our priests, on our prophets, on our fathers, and on all your people, since the time of the kings of Assyria to this day.

kikuyu: Nayo mĩthingi ĩyo ya thingo cia itũũra rĩu inene yagemetio na mĩthemba yothe ya mahiga ma goro. Mũthingi wa mbere warĩ wa njathibi, na wa keerĩ warĩ wa yakuti ĩrĩa ya bururu, na wa gatatũ warĩ wa yacimu, na wa kana warĩ wa thumarati,
english: The foundations of the city’s wall were adorned with all kinds of precious stones. The first foundation was jasper, the second sapphire,‡ the third chalcedony, the fourth emerald,

kikuyu: Ha ũhoro waku, wee mũthiringo ũyũ mũraihu na igũrũ wa kũrangĩra rũũru rwa mbũri, o wee kĩĩhitho kĩrũmu kĩa Mwarĩ wa Zayuni-rĩ, wathani ũrĩa warĩ naguo mbere nĩũgagũcookerera; naguo ũthamaki wa Mwarĩ wa Jerusalemu ũmũcookerere.”
english: You, tower of the flock, the hill of the daughter of Zion, to you it will come. Yes, the former dominion will come, the kingdom of the daughter of Jerusalem.

kikuyu: “Arakũrũithia na agagũthitanga nĩ ũndũ wa wĩtigĩri Ngai waku?
english: Is it for your piety that he reproves you, that he enters with you into judgment?

kikuyu: na Meraiothu nĩwe warĩ ithe wa Amaria, na Amaria nĩwe warĩ ithe wa Ahitubu,
english: Meraioth became the father of Amariah. Amariah became the father of Ahitub.

kikuyu: “Na ũtukũ gatagatĩ gũkĩanĩrĩrwo atĩrĩ: ‘Mũhikania nĩ mũũku! Umai mũkamũtũnge!’
english: But at midnight there was a cry, ‘Behold! The bridegroom is coming! Come out to meet him!’

kikuyu: a rĩrĩ, andũ a Efiraimu makĩũria Gideoni atĩrĩ, “Nĩ kĩĩ gĩtũmĩte ũtwĩke ũguo? Wagire gũtwĩta ũgĩthiĩ kũhũũrana na Amidiani nĩkĩ?” Nao makĩmũtetia mũno.
english: he men of Ephraim said to him, “Why have you treated us this way, that you didn’t call us when you went to fight with Midian?” They rebuked him sharply.

kikuyu: Kũrathimwo-rĩ, nĩ arĩa manyariiragwo nĩ ũndũ wa ũthingu, nĩgũkorwo ũthamaki wa igũrũ nĩ wao.
english: Blessed are those who have been persecuted for righteousness’ sake, for theirs is the Kingdom of Heaven.

kikuyu: “Ũrĩa wothe ũngĩamũkĩra kaana kamwe ta gaka thĩinĩ wa rĩĩtwa rĩakwa, nĩ niĩ aamũkagĩra; na ũrĩa ũngĩnyamũkĩra niĩ to niĩ aamũkagĩra, no nĩamũkagĩra ũrĩa wandũmire.” Ũrĩa ũtarĩ Thũ Iitũ arĩ Mwena Witũ
english: “Whoever receives one such little child in my name receives me; and whoever receives me, doesn’t receive me, but him who sent me.”

kikuyu: Kana eetio itumbĩ amũhe kangʼaurũ?
english: Or if he asks for an egg, he won’t give him a scorpion, will he?

kikuyu: a rĩrĩ, mũthenya ũcio rwĩmbo rũrũ nĩruo rũkainwo bũrũri wa Juda: Tũrĩ na itũũra inene rĩa hinya; Ngai atuuaga ũhonokio ũtuĩke thingo ciarĩo na njirigo ciarĩo cia hinya.
english: n that day, this song will be sung in the land of Judah: “We have a strong city. God appoints salvation for walls and bulwarks.

kikuyu: ,000 kũu mbaara-inĩ mũthenya ũcio. 22 No andũ a Isiraeli makĩũmanĩrĩria mũndũ na ũrĩa ũngĩ na makĩrũgama handũ hao rĩngĩ o harĩa meigĩte mũthenya wa mbere.
english: The people, the men of Israel, encouraged themselves, and set the battle again in array in the place where they set themselves in array the first day.

kikuyu: Maithe maitũ nĩwe meehokaga; maakwĩhokire nawe ũkĩmahonokia.
english: Our fathers trusted in you. They trusted, and you delivered them.

kikuyu: ndaahubanĩirio nĩ maaĩ maingĩ, magĩikũrũkĩra igũrũ rĩa mũtwe wakwa, na niĩ ngĩĩciiria ndĩ hakuhĩ kũniinwo.
english: Waters flowed over my head. I said, “I am cut off.”

kikuyu: no gũtũũra muoyo ndĩ na mwĩrĩ ũyũ nĩ ũndũ wanyu nĩ kwagĩrĩire mũno makĩria.
english: Yet to remain in the flesh is more needful for your sake.

kikuyu: Mwathani nĩagathambia gĩko kĩrĩa kĩũru kĩa andũ-a-nja a Zayuni; nĩagatheria Jerusalemu marooro ma thakame arĩ na roho wa gũtuanĩra ciira o na roho wa gũcina na mwaki.
english: when the Lord shall have washed away the filth of the daughters of Zion, and shall have purged the blood of Jerusalem from within it, by the spirit of justice and by the spirit of burning.

kikuyu: Ngoroba ya thĩ mũno yarĩ na wariĩ wa mĩkono ĩtano,‡ na ya gatagatĩ yarĩ na wariĩ * 5:11 nĩ ta kilo 4,400,000 † 5:11 nĩ ta lita 440,000 * 6:1 Mweri wa Zivu nĩguo mweri wa keerĩ wa Kĩyahudi, na nĩguo mweri wa ĩtano kũringana na mĩeri ya rĩu. † 6:2 nĩ ta mita 27 ‡ 6:2 nĩ ta mita 9 § 6:2 nĩ ta mita ikũmi na ithatũ na nuthu (13:5) * 6:3 nĩ ta mita 9 † 6:3 nĩ ta mita inya na nuthu (4:5) ‡ 6:6 nĩ ta mita igĩrĩ na robo (2.25)
english: The lowest floor was five cubits wide, and the middle was six cubits wide, and the third was seven cubits wide; for on the outside he made offsets in the wall of the house all around, that the beams should not be inserted into the walls of the house.

kikuyu: Ngombo ĩngĩgakorwo yũrĩire gwaku, ndũkanamĩneane kũrĩ mwathi wayo.
english: You shall not deliver to his master a  A shekel is about 10 grams or about 0.35 ounces.

kikuyu: “Nĩ niĩ Jehova; rĩu nĩrĩo rĩĩtwa rĩakwa! Naguo riiri wakwa ndikaũhe mũndũ ũngĩ, kana njĩtĩkĩre mĩhianano ya kũhooywo ĩheo ũgooci wakwa.
english: “I am Yahweh. That is my name. I will not give my glory to another, nor my praise to engraved images.

kikuyu: Ngoro ciao nĩ nyũmũ na itirĩ tha, no niĩ ngenagĩra watho waku.
english: Their heart is as callous as the fat, but I delight in your law.

kikuyu: Mũgeeka atĩa mũthenya ũrĩa mwamũre wa ciathĩ cianyu, mĩthenya ĩyo ya gĩathĩ kĩa Jehova?
english: What will you do in the day of solemn assembly, and in the day of the feast of Yahweh?

kikuyu: “Mũndũ ũtarĩ na thaahu acooke ahakũre mũhu ũcio wa moori ĩyo na aũige handũ hatarĩ na thaahu nja ya kambĩ. Mũhu ũcio ũigagwo nĩ kĩrĩndĩ kĩa andũ a Isiraeli, nĩgeetha ũhũthagĩrwo maaĩ-inĩ ma kũniina thaahu; ũcio nĩguo wa gũtheranagia kuuma mehia-inĩ.
english: “A man who is clean shall gather up the ashes of the heifer, and lay them up outside of the camp in a clean place; and it shall be kept for the congregation of the children of Israel for use in water for cleansing impurity. It is a sin offering.

kikuyu: Ningĩ Musa akĩĩra Kora atĩrĩ, “Wee na arũmĩrĩri aku othe mũgooka mbere ya Jehova rũciũ, wee, nao, na Harũni.
english: Moses said to Korah, “You and all your company go before Yahweh, you, and they, and Aaron, tomorrow.

kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa aathiiaga, andũ makĩara nguo ciao njĩra-inĩ.
english: As he went, they spread their cloaks on the road.

kikuyu: Atĩrĩrĩ, haandai bendera kĩrĩma igũrũ kĩrĩa gĩtarĩ mĩtĩ, anĩrĩrai na mũmahenerie, nĩguo matoonyere ihingo-inĩ cia arĩa marĩ igweta.
english: Set up a banner on the bare mountain! Lift up your voice to them! Wave your hand, that they may go into the gates of the nobles.

kikuyu: Ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ, ũguo noguo kũgĩaga gĩkeno kĩnene igũrũ nĩ ũndũ wa mũndũ ũmwe mwĩhia wĩrirĩte, gũkĩra ũrĩa gũkenagwo nĩ ũndũ wa andũ mĩrongo kenda na kenda athingu arĩa matabatarĩtio nĩ kwĩrira.
english: I tell you that even so there will be more joy in heaven over one sinner who repents, than over ninety-nine righteous people who need no repentance.

kikuyu: marũmanĩrĩire ũrĩa maaciarirwo, ũkurure marĩĩtwa matandatũ kahiga-inĩ kamwe, na matandatũ kau kangĩ.
english: Six of their names on the one stone, and the names of the six that remain on the other stone, in the order of their birth.

kikuyu: Mũrangĩri wa njeera akĩarahũka, na rĩrĩa aarorire akĩona mĩrango ya njeera ĩrĩ mĩhingũku, akĩruta rũhiũ rwake rwa njora akĩenda kwĩyũraga tondũ eeciiririe atĩ andũ arĩa moohetwo nĩmoorĩte.*
english: The jailer, being roused out of sleep and seeing the prison doors open, drew his sword and was about to kill himself, supposing that the prisoners had escaped.

kikuyu: Jehova nĩwe mũnene, na nowe wagĩrĩire kũgoocwo mũno; ũnene wake gũtirĩ mũndũ ũngĩhota kũũtuĩria.
english: Great is Yahweh, and greatly to be praised! His greatness is unsearchable.

kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Wĩthiĩre, wĩtĩkio waku nĩguo watũma ũhone.” O rĩmwe akĩambĩrĩria kuona, nake akĩrũmĩrĩra Jesũ.
english: Jesus said to him, “Go your way. Your faith has made you well.” Immediately he received his sight and followed Jesus on the way.

kikuyu: rĩrĩa angĩkorwo ehĩtie ũguo na atuĩke mũhĩtia-rĩ, no nginya acookie kĩrĩa aiyĩte, kana atunyanĩte, kana kĩrĩa ehokeirwo, kana kĩndũ kĩorĩte agĩkĩona,
english: then it shall be, if he has sinned, and is guilty, he shall restore that which he took by robbery, or the thing which he has gotten by oppression, or the deposit which was committed to him, or the lost thing which he found,

kikuyu: Ikombe ithatũ ciaturĩtwo ihaana ta mahũa ma mũrothi, irĩ na tũkonyo na mahũa, igakorwo rũhonge-inĩ rũmwe, nacio ithatũ igakorwo rũhonge-inĩ rũu rũngĩ; honge icio ciothe ithathatũ ciahaanaga ũguo ciumĩte mũtĩ-inĩ ũcio wa kũigĩrĩrwo matawa.
english: three cups made like almond blossoms in one branch, a bud and a flower, and three cups made like almond blossoms in the other branch, a bud and a flower; so for the six branches going out of the lamp stand.

kikuyu: Mwĩtharei mwĩhonokie, inyuĩ andũ a Benjamini! Mwĩtharei muume Jerusalemu! Huhai karumbeta mũrĩ kũu Tekoa! Ĩkĩrai kĩmenyithia kũu Bethi-Hakeremu! Nĩgũkorwo ũũru nĩũũkĩte uumĩte na mwena wa gathigathini, na nĩ ũũru wa kũniinanwo kũnene mũno.
english: Flee for safety, you children of Benjamin, out of the middle of Jerusalem! Blow the trumpet in Tekoa and raise up a signal on Beth Haccherem, for evil looks out from the north with a great destruction.

kikuyu: nyuĩ ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ, nĩmũũĩ atĩ gũũka gwitũ kũrĩ inyuĩ gũtiarĩ gwa tũhũ.
english: or you yourselves know, brothers, our visit to you wasn’t in vain,

kikuyu: Abineri agĩĩta Joabu aanĩrĩire, akĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Rũhiũ rwa njora rũgũtũũra rũniinanaga nginya tene? Anga ndũramenya atĩ ũhoro ũyũ ũkaarigĩrĩria na ũrũrũ? Ũgũikara nginya rĩ ũterĩte andũ aku matige gũtengʼeria ariũ a ithe wao?”
english: Then Abner called to Joab, and said, “Shall the sword devour forever? Don’t you know that it will be bitterness in the latter end? How long will it be then, before you ask the people to return from following their brothers?”

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ wa ũnene waku wa magegania, nĩwakurumanirie arĩa magũũkĩrĩire. Nĩwaitũrũrire marakara maku mahiũ; makĩmacina taarĩ mahuti.
english: In the greatness of your excellency, you overthrow those who rise up against you. You send out your wrath. It consumes them as stubble.

kikuyu: Mwanake ũcio akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Ndĩrakorirwo ndĩ hau kĩrĩma-inĩ kĩa Giliboa, na hau nĩho ndĩronire Saũlũ, etiranĩtie na itimũ rĩake, akiriĩ gũkorererwo nĩ ngaari cia ita na ahaici a mbarathi.
english: The young man who told him said, “As I happened by chance on Mount Gilboa, behold, Saul was leaning on his spear; and behold, the chariots and the horsemen followed close behind him.

kikuyu: na Nethaneli wa kana, nake Radai wa gatano,
english: Nethanel the fourth, Raddai the fifth,

kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Simoni onire atĩ Roho aaheanagwo na ũndũ wa kũigĩrĩrwo moko nĩ atũmwo-rĩ, akĩenda amahe mbeeca,
english: Now when Simon saw that the Holy Spirit was given through the laying on of the apostles’ hands, he offered them money,

kikuyu: No aatuirwo mũthingu arĩ na ũhoro ũrĩkũ? Hihi nĩ thuutha wa kũrua, kana nĩ mbere ĩyo? Ti thuutha wa kũrua, no nĩ mbere ĩyo, hĩndĩ ĩrĩa ataaruĩte!
english: How then was it counted? When he was in circumcision, or in uncircumcision? Not in circumcision, but in uncircumcision.

kikuyu: Ti- itherũ o na hĩndĩ ĩrĩa twarĩ na inyuĩ nĩtwamwĩraga kaingĩ atĩ no nginya tũnyariirwo. O ta ũrĩa mũũĩ atĩ ũguo noguo gwacookire gũgĩtuĩka.
english: For most certainly, when we were with you, we told you beforehand that we are to suffer affliction, even as it happened, and you know.

kikuyu: Twarĩrai Ngai wa Jerusalemu indo ciothe iria mwĩhokeirwo cia kũhũthagĩrwo mahooya-inĩ thĩinĩ wa hekarũ ya Ngai wanyu.
english: The vessels that are given to you for the service of the house of your God, deliver before the God of Jerusalem.

kikuyu: Musa akĩnengera Harũni na ariũ ake mbeeca icio ciakũũrĩte andũ, o ta ũrĩa aathĩtwo nĩ kiugo kĩa Jehova.
english: and Moses gave the redemption money to Aaron and to his sons, according to Yahweh’s word, as Yahweh commanded Moses.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio magĩĩta Rebeka, makĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Nĩũgũthiĩ na mũndũ ũyũ?” Nake akiuga atĩrĩ, “Nĩngũthiĩ.”
english: They called Rebekah, and said to her, “Will you go with this man?” She said, “I will go.”

kikuyu: Na ithuĩ-rĩ, tũkĩgĩe na indo cia goro cia mĩthemba yothe, na tũiyũrie nyũmba ciitũ na indo cia ndaho;
english: We’ll find all valuable wealth. We’ll fill our houses with plunder.

kikuyu: Nake Mũthamaki Daudi agĩtoonya, agĩikara thĩ mbere ya Jehova, akiuga atĩrĩ: “Niĩ ndĩkĩrĩ ũ, Wee Jehova Ngai, nayo nyũmba yakwa-rĩ, ĩkĩrĩ kĩ, atĩ nĩkĩo ũnginyĩtie nginya haha?
english: Then David the king went in and sat before Yahweh; and he said, “Who am I, Yahweh God, and what is my house, that you have brought me this far?

kikuyu: Ha ũhoro ũkoniĩ Dameski: “Hamathu na Aripadi nĩamaku, nĩ ũndũ nĩmaiguĩte ndeto njũru. Nĩmorĩtwo nĩ hinya, makaagaaga ta maaĩ ma iria.
english: Of Damascus: “Hamath and Arpad are confounded, for they have heard evil news. They have melted away. There is sorrow on the sea.

kikuyu: Na gũkĩgĩa hwaĩ-inĩ na gũkĩgĩa rũciinĩ. Ũcio ũgĩtuĩka mũthenya wa kana.
english: There was evening and there was morning, a fourth day.

kikuyu: Andũ magaatuĩkanagĩria bũrũri-inĩ marĩ anyamaarĩku na marĩ ahũtu; na rĩrĩ, rĩrĩa makahũũta mũno, nĩmakarakara mũno na matiire maitho na igũrũ, nao marume mũthamaki wao na Ngai wao.
english: They will pass through it, very distressed and hungry. It will happen that when they are hungry, they will worry, and curse their king and their God. They will turn their faces upward,

kikuyu: Nao Asamaria aingĩ kuuma itũũra rĩu makĩmwĩtĩkia nĩ ũndũ wa ũira wa mũtumia ũcio, tondũ nĩameerire atĩrĩ, “Nĩanjĩĩrire maũndũ marĩa mothe ndaneeka.”
english: From that city many of the Samaritans believed in him because of the word of the woman, who testified, “He told me everything that I have done.”

kikuyu: Tũũrai mwĩhokete Jehova nginya tene, nĩgũkorwo Jehova, o we Jehova, nĩwe Rwaro rwa Ihiga rwa tene na tene.
english: Trust in Yahweh forever; for in Yah, Yahweh, is an everlasting Rock.

kikuyu: Inyuĩ no nginya mũtuĩke andũ matarĩ ũcuuke mbere ya Jehova Ngai wanyu. Mũnabii
english: You shall be blameless with Yahweh your God.

kikuyu: Nake akĩmacookeria atĩrĩ, “Mũngĩkorwo na wĩtĩkio o mũnini ta kabegũ ga karatarĩ-rĩ, mwera mũkũyũ ũyũ atĩrĩ, ‘Kũũrũka, ũkehaande iria-inĩ,’ naguo no ũmwathĩkĩre.
english: The Lord said, “If you had faith like a grain of mustard seed, you would tell this sycamore tree, ‘Be uprooted and be planted in the sea,’ and it would obey you.

kikuyu: nao athiritũ ao maarĩ: Shebania, na Hodia, na Kelita, na Pelaia, na Hanani,
english: and their brothers, Shebaniah, Hodiah, Kelita, Pelaiah, Hanan,

kikuyu: Ũndũ ũyũ nĩũbataire ũũgĩ. Mũndũ o wothe angĩkorwo arĩ na ũmenyo-rĩ, nĩeke ithabu rĩa namba ya nyamũ ĩyo, nĩgũkorwo nĩ namba ya mũndũ. Nayo namba yake nĩ 666. 14 Gatũrũme na Andũ 144,000
english: Here is wisdom. He who has understanding, let him calculate the number of the beast, for it is the number of a man. His number is six hundred sixty-six.

kikuyu: Rũũĩ rwa Kishoni nĩ rwamathereririe, rũu rũũĩ rwa tene, Rũũĩ rwa Kishoni. Wee ngoro yakwa wĩtware, na ũgĩe na hinya!
english: The river Kishon swept them away, that ancient river, the river Kishon. My soul, march on with strength.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa ciũmbe icio ciathiiaga, ngĩigua mũgambo wa mathagu ma cio, taarĩ mũrurumo wa maaĩ magĩtherera, na taarĩ mũgambo wa Mwene-Hinya-Wothe, taarĩ mbugĩrĩrio ya thigari cia ita. Na rĩrĩa ciũmbe icio ciarũgamire, igĩthuna mathagu ma cio.
english: When they went, I heard the noise of their wings like the noise of great waters, like the voice of the Almighty, a noise of tumult like the noise of an army. When they stood, they let down their wings.

kikuyu: Ningĩ makĩigua mũgambo mũnene uumĩte igũrũ ũkĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Ambatai na gũkũ.” Nao makĩambata igũrũ marĩ itu-inĩ, thũ ciao imeroreire.
english: I heard a loud voice from heaven saying to them, “Come up here!” They went up into heaven in a cloud, and their enemies saw them.

kikuyu: Ningĩ agĩthima wariĩ wa njĩra ĩyo ya itoonyero rĩu, nayo yarĩ ya mĩkono ikũmi,* na ũraihu wayo warĩ wa mĩkono ikũmi na ĩtatũ.†
english: He measured the width of the opening of the gate, ten cubits; and the length of the gate, thirteen cubits;

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo thĩinĩ wake nĩmũtongetio maũndũ-inĩ mothe, mũgatongio ũhoro- inĩ wa mĩario yanyu yothe o na ũhoro-inĩ wothe wa kũmenya maũndũ,
english: that in everything you were enriched in him, in all speech and all knowledge—

kikuyu: nao andũ a Aavi magĩthondeka Nibihazu na Tarataka, nao andũ a Sefariri maacinaga ciana na mwaki irĩ ta magongona kũrĩ Adarameleki na Anameleku, ngai cia Sefarivaimu.
english: and the Avvites made Nibhaz and Tartak; and the Sepharvites burned their children in the fire to Adrammelech and Anammelech, the gods of Sepharvaim.

kikuyu: “Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, wee Oholiba, Mwathani Jehova ekuuga ũũ: Nĩngarahũra endwa aku magũũkĩrĩre, acio watiganire nao ũkĩmamena, ndĩmarehe magũũkĩrĩre kuuma mĩena yothe,
english: “Therefore, Oholibah, the Lord Yahweh says: ‘Behold, I will raise up your lovers against you, from whom your soul is alienated, and I will bring them against you on every side:

kikuyu: Aranagĩriai mũndũ na ũrĩa ũngĩ na ciugo cia thaburi, na nyĩmbo cia kũinĩra Ngai, o na nyĩmbo cia kũguna maroho. Inagai na mũtungage na nyĩmbo ngoro-inĩ cianyu nĩ ũndũ wa Mwathani,
english: speaking to one another in psalms, hymns, and spiritual songs; singing and making melody in your heart to the Lord;

kikuyu: “ ‘Ningĩ kũngĩkorwo o na mwekwo maũndũ macio no mũkaarega kũnjookerera na mũgĩthiĩ na mbere kũnjũkĩrĩra-rĩ,
english: “ ‘If by these things you won’t be turned back to me, but will walk contrary to me,

kikuyu: O rĩmwe ngĩkorwo ndĩ thĩinĩ wa Roho, na rĩrĩ, hau mbere yakwa harĩ na gĩtĩ kĩa ũnene kũu igũrũ, na nĩ harĩ na mũndũ wagĩikarĩire.
english: Immediately I was in the Spirit. Behold, there was a throne set in heaven, and one sitting on the throne

kikuyu: Ndũkegwatanie na andũ arĩa manyuuaga njoohi, kana arĩa makorokagĩra nyama,
english: Don’t be among ones drinking too much wine, or those who gorge themselves on meat;

kikuyu: Itũũra rĩĩrĩ inene na kĩndũ kĩrĩa gĩothe kĩrĩ thĩinĩ warĩo nĩikwamũrĩrwo Jehova, cianangwo. No Rahabu ũrĩa mũmaraya wiki, na andũ arĩa othe marĩ hamwe nake thĩinĩ wa nyũmba yake, mekũhonokio, tondũ nĩahithire athigaani arĩa twatũmĩte.
english: The city shall be devoted, even it and all that is in it, to Yahweh. Only Rahab the prostitute shall live, she and all who are with her in the house, because she hid the messengers that we sent.

kikuyu: “Nĩ kwarĩ mũndũ warĩ gĩtonga wehumbaga nguo cia rangi wa ndathi na cia mũthemba wa gatani ĩrĩa njega, nake nĩeekenagia o mũthenya.
english: “Now there was a certain rich man, and he was clothed in purple and fine linen, living in luxury every day.

kikuyu: Ngoro ĩrĩ na gũkũũrana maũndũ nĩĩmaathaga ũmenyo, no kanua ka mũndũ mũkĩĩgu kehũũnagia na ũrimũ.
english: The heart of one who has understanding seeks knowledge, but the mouths of fools feed on folly.

kikuyu: Aya nĩo maarĩ anene ake: Azaria mũrũ wa Zadoku aarĩ mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai;*
english: These were the princes whom he had: Azariah the son of Zadok, the priest;

kikuyu: Nao magĩthiĩ Kaperinaumu, na rĩrĩa mũthenya wa Thabatũ wakinyire, Jesũ agĩtoonya thunagogi na akĩambĩrĩria kũrutana.
english: They went into Capernaum, and immediately on the Sabbath day he entered into the synagogue and taught.

kikuyu: Ndĩramwĩra ũguo nĩgeetha mũndũ o na ũrĩkũ ndakanamũheenie na ngarari cia mawara.
english: Now I say this that no one may delude you with persuasiveness of speech.

kikuyu: Ndamwĩra atĩrĩ na ma, andũ nĩmakarekerwo mehia mao mothe o na irumi ciao ciothe iria magaakorwo marumĩte Ngai nacio.
english: “Most certainly I tell you, all sins of the descendants of man will be forgiven, including their blasphemies with which they may blaspheme;

kikuyu: Ngai aamoombire mũndũ mũrũme na mũndũ-wa-nja na akĩmarathima. Na aarĩkia kũmomba, akĩmeeta “mũndũ.”
english: He created them male and female, and blessed them. On the day they were created, he named them Adam.†

kikuyu: Meranaga atĩrĩ, “Ũkai, tũmaniine matige gũtuĩka rũrĩrĩ, nĩguo rĩĩtwa rĩa Isiraeli rĩtikanacooke kũririkanwo rĩngĩ.”
english: “Come,” they say, “let’s destroy them as a nation, that the name of Israel may be remembered no more.”

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo andũ othe a Isiraeli nĩmakaigua ũhoro ũcio nao nĩmagetigĩra, na gũtirĩ mũndũ gatagatĩ kanyu ũgeeka ũndũ mũũru ta ũcio rĩngĩ.
english: All Israel shall hear, and fear, and shall not do any more wickedness like this among you.

kikuyu: Thĩinĩ wa andũ-a-nja aku arĩa atĩĩku harĩ na aarĩ a athamaki; mũhiki ũcio wa mũthamaki aikarĩte mwena waku wa ũrĩo egemetie na thahabu ya Ofiri.
english: Kings’ daughters are among your honorable women. At your right hand the queen stands in gold of Ophir.

kikuyu: Nao andũ othe a bũrũri magĩkena. Narĩo itũũra rĩkĩhoorera, tondũ Athalia nĩarĩkĩtie kũũragwo na rũhiũ rwa njora nyũmba-inĩ ya ũthamaki.
english: So all the people of the land rejoiced, and the city was quiet. They had slain Athaliah with the sword at the king’s house.

kikuyu: Nake Jehova akĩĩra Musa atĩrĩ, “Tara marigithathi ma arũme othe ma andũ a Isiraeli arĩa makinyĩtie mweri ũmwe na makĩria, na wandĩke marĩĩtwa mao.
english: Yahweh said to Moses, “Count all the firstborn males of the children of Israel from a month old and upward, and take the number of their names.

kikuyu: na Gathu, na Maresha, na Zifu,
english: Gath, Mareshah, Ziph,

kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, tũnyũmba tũu twarĩ igũrũ twarĩ tũkundakundeeru, nĩ ũndũ ithaku nĩcioete handũ hanene gũkĩra tũnyũmba tũrĩa twakĩtwo ngoroba ĩyo yarĩ mũhuro na ya gatagatĩ.
english: Now the upper rooms were shorter; for the galleries took away from these more than from the lower and the middle in the building.

kikuyu: “Mwĩmenyererei, ngoro cianyu itikanakorwo itahĩtwo nĩ ndĩa, na ũrĩĩu na metanga ma muoyo ũyũ, naguo mũthenya ũcio ũmũkorerere ta mũtego o rĩrĩa mũtangĩĩgereria.
english: “So be careful, or your hearts will be loaded down with carousing, drunkenness, and cares of this life, and that day will come on you suddenly.

kikuyu: Icunjĩ iria ciarĩ gatagatĩ ka buremu, o na buremu cio nyene, ciarĩ na mĩhiano ya mĩrũũthi, na ya ndegwa, na ya makerubi. Mwena wa igũrũ na wa mũhuro wa mĩhiano ya mĩrũũthi na ya ndegwa nĩ gwekĩrĩtwo magemio ma mahũa mature.
english: and on the panels that were between the ledges were lions, oxen, and cherubim; and on the ledges there was a pedestal above; and beneath the lions and oxen were wreaths of hanging work.

kikuyu: Jesũ aarĩkia kuuma gatarũ, aakinya thĩ agĩtũngwo nĩ mũndũ warĩ na ndaimono oimĩte itũũra-inĩ. Mũndũ ũcio nĩaikarĩte ihinda inene ategwĩkĩra nguo kana agatũũra nyũmba, no aatũũraga mbĩrĩra-inĩ.
english: When Jesus stepped ashore, a certain man out of the city who had demons for a long time met him. He wore no clothes, and didn’t live in a house, but in the tombs.

kikuyu: wee itũũra rĩiyũrĩte ngũĩ, o wee itũũra inene rĩa mbugĩrĩrio na ũrĩĩu? Andũ aku arĩa makuĩte matiũragĩtwo na rũhiũ rwa njora, kana magakuĩra mbaara-inĩ.
english: You that are full of shouting, a tumultuous city, a joyous town, your slain are not slain with the sword, neither are they dead in battle.

kikuyu: No tũngĩgĩa na irio, na tũgĩe na nguo-rĩ, no tũiganwo nĩcio.
english: But having food and clothing, we will be content with that.

kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa mwarĩĩaga na mũkanyua-rĩ, githĩ mũtiarĩĩaga ndĩa ĩyo nĩ ũndũ wa inyuĩ ene?
english: When you eat and when you drink, don’t you eat for yourselves and drink for yourselves?

kikuyu: Ningĩ mũthenya wa ĩnana no nginya atware ndirahũgĩ igĩrĩ kana tũtutuura twĩrĩ harĩ mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai hau itoonyero-inĩ rĩa Hema- ya-Gũtũnganwo.
english: On the eighth day he shall bring two turtledoves or two young pigeons to the priest, to the door of the Tent of Meeting.

kikuyu: “Ndakwĩra atĩrĩ, ũkĩra, oya kĩbarĩ gĩaku ũinũke mũciĩ.”
english: “I tell you, arise, take up your mat, and go to your house.”

kikuyu: “ ‘Ta thikĩrĩria, wee Joshua, mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai ũrĩa mũnene, hamwe na athiritũ aku aya maikarĩte thĩ mbere yaku, o acio kĩmenyithia kĩa maũndũ marĩa magooka: Nĩngarehe ndungata yakwa, o we Rũhonge.’
